name: Magmar
flavorText: It dislikes cold places, so it blows scorching flames to make the environment suitable for itself.

name: Roselia

attacks.name: Petal Dance
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads. Roselia is now Confused.

flavorText: Roselia that drink nutritionally rich springwater are said to reveal rare coloration when they bloom.

name: Coalossal

abilities.name: Tar Generator
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may attach a Fire Energy card, a Fighting Energy card, or 1 of each from your discard pile to your Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: It's usually peaceful, but the vandalism of mines enrages it. Offenders will be incinerated with flames that reach 2,700 degrees Fahrenheit.

name: Skiploom

abilities.name: Cotton Balloon
abilities.text: If Skiploom has any Grass Energy attached to it, any damage done to Skiploom by attacks from your opponent's Evolved Pokémon is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: U-turn
attacks.text: Switch Skiploom with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It blooms when the weather warms. It floats in the sky to soak up as much sunlight as possible.

name: Flaaffy
flavorText: It stores electricity in its fluffy fleece. If it stores up too much, it will start to go bald in those patches.

name: Palkia-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Strafe
attacks.text: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Dimension Heal
attacks.text: Heal from this Pokémon 20 damage for each Plasma Energy attached to this Pokémon.

name: Sonia
rules: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon or up to 2 basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Squirtle

attacks.name: Bubble
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Withdraw
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to Squirtle during your opponent's next turn. (Any other effects of attacks still happen.)

flavorText: After birth, its back swells and hardens into a shell. It powerfully sprays foam from its mouth.

attacks.name: Hind Kick
attacks.text: If you have any Benched Pokémon, flip a coin. If heads, switch Blaine's Ponyta with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Supersonic
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

name: Goldeen
flavorText: Its tail fin billows like an elegant ballroom dress, giving it the nickname "Water Queen."

abilities.name: Duplicate
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for another Ditto and switch it with Ditto. (Any cards attached to Ditto, damage counters, Special Conditions, and effects on it are now on the new Pokémon.) If you do, put Ditto on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward. You can't use more than 1 Duplicate Poké-Power each turn.

attacks.name: Fireworks
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, discard a Fire Energy card attached to Ditto.

name: Eelektrik

ancientTrait.name: α Recovery
ancientTrait.text: When this Pokémon is healed, double the amount healed.

attacks.name: Thrash
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage. If tails, this Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: These Pokémon have a big appetite. When they spot their prey, they attack it and paralyze it with electricity.

abilities.name: Rough Skin
abilities.text: If Carvanha is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent's attack (even if Carvanha is Knocked Out), put 1 damage counter on the Attacking Pokémon.

attacks.name: Big Bite
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat until the end of your opponent's next turn.

name: Liepard

attacks.name: Gentle Bite
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 60 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Mach Claw
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: Their beautiful form comes from the muscles they have developed. They run silently in the night.

name: Lanturn

attacks.name: Energy Split
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon that has any Energy cards attached to it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Aqua Bolt
attacks.text: Does 60 damage plus 10 more damage for each Water Energy attached to Lanturn.

flavorText: Lanturn's light can shine up from great depths. It is nicknamed "The Deep-Sea Star."

name: Drilbur
flavorText: It makes its way swiftly through the soil by putting both claws together and rotating at high speed.

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Metal Ball
attacks.text: Put 1 damage counter on the Defending Pokémon.

name: Jigglypuff

attacks.name: Hypnoblast
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Looking into its cute, round eyes causes it to sing a relaxing melody, inducing its enemies to sleep.

name: Gallade

attacks.name: Powerful Storm
attacks.text: Does 20 damage times the amount of Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Swift Lunge
attacks.text: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: When trying to protect someone, it extends its elbows as if they were swords and fights savagely.

name: Nidorina

attacks.name: Rescue
attacks.text: Search your discard pile for up to 2 Pokémon, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand.

flavorText: When it senses danger, it raises all the barbs on its body. These barbs grow slower than NIDORINO's.

name: Togetic

attacks.name: Sweet Kiss
attacks.text: Your opponent may draw a card.

flavorText: It is said to appear in front of kindly people to scatter a glowing down called "joy dust."

name: Tauros

attacks.name: Stomp
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage; if tails, this attack does 20 damage.

attacks.name: Rampage
attacks.text: Does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Tauros. Flip a coin. If tails, Tauros is now Confused (after doing damage).

flavorText: When it targets an enemy, it charges furiously while whipping its body with its long tails.

attacks.name: Agility
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Blaine's Ponyta.

attacks.name: Super Psywave
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. Count the number of Energy cards attached to that Pokémon. Put that many damage counters on the Pokémon.

name: Machop

attacks.name: Dual Chop
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Its whole body is composed of muscles. Even though it's the size of a human child, it can hurl 100 grown-ups.

attacks.name: Zzzap
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to each Pokémon with a Poké-Body or Poké-Power (yours and your opponent's). (Don't apply Weakness or Resistance.)

attacks.name: Lightning Storm
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, put 2 damage counters on Raichu.

name: Lilligant

attacks.name: Lead
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Supporter card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Dream Dance
attacks.text: Both this Pokémon and the Defending Pokémon are now Asleep.

flavorText: Even veteran Trainers face a challenge in getting its beautiful flower to bloom. This Pokémon is popular with celebrities.

name: Electrode

attacks.name: Orb Collector
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It stores an overflowing amount of electric energy inside its body. Even a small shock makes it explode.

name: Rhyhorn

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: It is inept at turning because of its four short legs. It can only charge and run in one direction.

name: Zorua
flavorText: It changes so it looks like its foe, tricks it, and then uses that opportunity to flee.

name: Drowzee

attacks.name: Psychic Boom
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage times the amount of Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It finds really fun dreams tasty. When it makes friends with people, it may show them the most delicious dreams it's ever eaten.

name: Litwick

attacks.name: Searing Flame
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: Litwick shines a light that absorbs the life energy of people and Pokémon, which becomes the fuel it burns.

name: Eelektrik

attacks.name: Shocking Smash
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: These Pokémon have a big appetite. When they spot their prey, they attack it and paralyze it with electricity.

attacks.name: Random Spark
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Pikachu
flavorText: Its nature is to store up electricity. Forests where nests of Pikachu live are dangerous, since the trees are so often struck by lightning.

name: Groudon

attacks.name: Rock Smash
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

attacks.name: Break Ground
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: This legendary Pokémon is said to represent the land. It went to sleep after dueling Kyogre.

name: Dhelmise

abilities.name: Steelworker
abilities.text: Your Metal Pokémon's attacks do 10 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Anchor Shot
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: Swinging its massive anchor, it can KO Wailord in a single blow. What appears to be green seaweed is actually its body.

name: Dedenne

attacks.name: Thunder Shock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It uses its tail to absorb electricity from power plants or from outlets in houses, and then it fires the electricity from its whiskers.

name: Dewpider
flavorText: When two Dewpider meet, they display their water bubbles to each other. Then the one with the smaller bubble gets out of the other's way.

name: Fiery Flint
rules: You can play this card only if you discard 2 other cards from your hand.
Search your deck for up to 4 Fire Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

attacks.name: Steel Headbutt
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage.

name: Cosmoem

attacks.name: Teleport
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: There's something accumulating around the black core within its hard shell. People think this Pokémon may come from another world.

name: Scolipede

attacks.name: Poison Horn
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Steamroller
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: It clasps its prey with the claws on its neck until it stops moving. Then it finishes it off with deadly poison.

attacks.name: Spark
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Aron

attacks.name: Iron Head
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It usually lives deep in mountains. However, hunger may drive it to eat railroad tracks and cars.

name: Dark Octillery

attacks.name: Ink Blast
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy attached to Dark Octillery but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. You can't add more than 20 damage in this way.

attacks.name: Tentacle Wrap
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed. If tails, during your opponent's next turn, your opponent pays Colorless more to retreat the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: It likes to sleep in rock caves and will even steal other Octillery's nests.

name: Swoobat

attacks.name: Energy Gift
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for 2 Psychic Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: It shakes its tail vigorously when it emits ultrasonic waves strong enough to reduce concrete to rubble.

name: Makuhita
flavorText: It toughens up by slamming into thick trees over and over. It gains a sturdy body and dauntless spirit.

name: Cubone
flavorText: When it thinks of its dead mother, it cries. Its crying makes the skull it wears rattle hollowly.

abilities.name: Dark Lift
abilities.text: If Houndour has any Darkness Energy attached to it, the Retreat Cost for Houndour is 0.

attacks.name: Firebreathing
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.

attacks.name: Hypnotic Ray
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

name: Mantine

abilities.name: Mantine Surf
abilities.text: If this Pokémon has any Energy attached to it, it has no Retreat Cost.

flavorText: While elegantly swimming in the sea, it ignores Remoraid that cling to its fins seeking food scraps.

name: Electrike
flavorText: Using electricity stored in its fur, it stimulates its muscles to heighten its reaction speed.

name: Cubone

abilities.name: Lonely Bone
abilities.text: Any damage done to Cubone by your opponent's attacks is reduced by 20 for each Marowak in your discard pile (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Bone Rush
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: If it is sad or lonely, the skull it wears shakes and emits a plaintive and mournful sound.

name: Cramorant

attacks.name: Water Arrow
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It's so strong that it can knock out some opponents in a single hit, but it also may forget what it's battling midfight.

attacks.name: Kick Away
attacks.text: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon, if any.

name: Lucario

abilities.name: Stance
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may prevent all effects of your opponent's attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon until the end of your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: Not only does it perceive auras, but it has also gained the power to control them. It employs them in battle.

name: Drilbur

attacks.name: Hone Claws
attacks.text: During your next turn, each of this Pokémon's attacks does 30 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: It can dig through the ground at a speed of 30 mph. It could give a car running aboveground a good race.

name: Hippowdon

attacks.name: Sand Bazooka
attacks.text: You may move 1 Energy attached to this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Rock Tumble
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: It is surprisingly quick to anger. It holds its mouth agape as a display of its strength.

name: Oddish

attacks.name: Find a Friend
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a Grass Pokémon, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: Awakened by moonlight, it roams actively at night. In the day it stays quietly underground.

name: Frogadier

attacks.name: Afterimage Strike
attacks.text: If any damage is done to this Pokémon by attacks during your opponent's next turn, flip a coin. If heads, prevent that damage.

flavorText: It can throw bubble-covered pebbles with precise control, hitting empty cans up to a hundred feet away.

name: Drapion

attacks.name: Poison Claws
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It has the power in its clawed arms to make scrap of a car. The tips of its claws release poison.

name: Druddigon

attacks.name: Revenge
attacks.text: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack during his or her last turn, this attack does 70 more damage.

flavorText: It races through narrow caves, using its sharp claws to catch prey. The skin on its face is harder than a rock.

name: Drifblim
flavorText: At dusk, swarms of them are carried aloft on winds. When noticed, they suddenly vanish.

name: Rhyperior

attacks.name: Rock Black
attacks.text: Flip a coin for each Fighting Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Rock Wrecker
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance. This Pokémon can't attack during your next turn.

flavorText: From holes in its palms, it fires out Geodude. Its carapace can withstand volcanic eruptions.

name: Solrock

abilities.name: Sunbeam
abilities.text: The maximum HP of each of your Lunatone in play is 130.

attacks.name: Scorching Light
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed. If tails, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: It absorbs solar energy during the day. Always expressionless, it can sense what its foe is thinking.

name: Makuhita

attacks.name: Continuous Slap
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It toughens its body by slamming into thick trees. Many snapped trees can be found near its nest.

name: Nidorino

attacks.name: Double Kick
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: An aggressive Pokémon that is quick to attack. The horn on its head secretes a powerful venom.

name: Swinub

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.text: Swinub does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: If it smells something enticing, it dashes headlong off to find the source of the aroma.

name: Eevee

attacks.name: Lunge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Thanks to its unstable genetic makeup, this special Pokémon conceals many different possible evolutions.

name: Chinchou

attacks.name: Supersonic
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Flail
attacks.text: Does 10 damage times the number of damage counters on Chinchou.

flavorText: It shoots positive and negative electricity between the tips of its two antennae and zaps its enemies.

name: Wormadam

attacks.name: Strike Back
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage times the number of damage counters on this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Iron Head
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: When Burmy evolved, its cloak became a part of this Pokémon's body. The cloak is never shed.

name: Dunsparce

attacks.name: Glare
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: When spotted, this Pokémon escapes backward by furiously boring into the ground with its tail.

name: Combee

attacks.name: Honey Scent
attacks.text: Remove 2 damage counters from 1 of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Flitter
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

flavorText: It collects and delivers honey to its colony. At night, they cluster to form a beehive and sleep.

name: Shieldon

attacks.name: Endure
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, if Shieldon would be Knocked Out by damage from an attack, Shieldon is not Knocked Out and its remaining HP becomes 10 instead.

attacks.name: Rock Slide
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It is outstandingly armored. As a result, it can eat grass and berries without having to fight.

name: Glaceon-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Freezing Gaze
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, your opponent's Pokémon-GX and Pokémon-EX in play, in their hand, and in their discard pile have no Abilities, except for Freezing Gaze.

attacks.name: Frost Bullet
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Polar Spear-GX
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage for each damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Grimer

attacks.name: Poison Gas
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Sticky Hands
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed; if tails, this attack does 10 damage.

flavorText: It has recently been found that Grimers are formed in waste-processing plants, and can move into towns via sewers.

name: M Latios-EX
rules: Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Sonic Ace
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 120 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Cubone

attacks.name: Burdensome Bone
attacks.text: This Pokémon can't attack during your next turn.

flavorText: The skull it wears on its head is that of its dead mother. According to some, it will evolve when it comes to terms with the pain of her death.

name: Bidoof

attacks.name: Yawn
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Self-abandonment
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage to the Defending Pokémon. If tails, Bidoof does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: With nerves of steel, nothing can perturb it. It is more agile and active than it appears.

name: Alolan Marowak

attacks.name: Limbo Limbo
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 basic Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Alolan Club
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each of your Pokémon in play that has Alolan in its name.

flavorText: Its custom is to mourn its lost companions. Mounds of dirt by the side of the road mark the graves of the Marowak.

attacks.name: Copy
attacks.text: Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon's attacks. Copy copies that attack. This attack does nothing if Sudowoodo doesn't have the Energy necessary to use that attack. (You must still do anything else required in order to use that attack.)

attacks.name: Energy Draw
attacks.text: Search your deck for a basic Energy card and attach it to Sudowoodo. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Regirock

abilities.name: Rock Peak Growl
abilities.text: Your Registeel's attacks do 10 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Tough Swing
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: Its entire body is made of rock. If any part chips off in battle, it attaches rocks to repair itself.

name: Dusknoir

abilities.name: Sinister Hand
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may move 1 damage counter from 1 of your opponent's Pokémon to another of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Shadow Punch
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: It is said to take lost spirits into its pliant body and guide them home.

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.text: Numel does 10 damage to itself.

name: Timburr
flavorText: These Pokémon appear at building sites and help out with construction. They always carry squared logs.

name: Misty's Determination
rules: Discard a card from your hand. If you do, look at the top 8 cards of your deck and put 1 of them into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Staravia

attacks.name: Whirlwind
attacks.text: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Clutch
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It flies around forests and fields in search of bug Pokémon. It stays within a huge flock.

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

name: Smeargle

attacks.name: Color Pick
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 basic Energy cards, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Trace
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, choose an attack on 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. Trace copies that attack except for its Energy cost. (You must still do anything else required for that attack.) Smeargle performs that attack.

flavorText: It marks its territory by using its tail like a paintbrush. There are more than 5,000 different marks.

abilities.name: Pure Body
abilities.text: To attach a Lightning Energy card from your hand to Raikou, you must discard an Energy card attached to Raikou. (Attach the Lightning Energy, and then discard an Energy card from Raikou.)

attacks.name: Lightning Sphere
attacks.text: You may flip a coin. If heads, discard all Lightning Energy cards attached to Raikou. This attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage for each Energy card discarded in this way.

name: Tropical Beach
rules: Once during each player's turn, that player may draw cards until he or she has 7 cards in his or her hand. If he or she does, that player's turn ends.
This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

name: Jynx

attacks.name: Mimic
attacks.text: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw a number of cards equal to the number of cards in your opponent's hand.

attacks.name: Lick
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It rocks its body rhythmically. It appears to alter the rhythm depending on how it is feeling.

name: Spearow

attacks.name: Roost
attacks.text: Remove 4 damage counters from Spearow. Spearow can't retreat during your next turn.

flavorText: It flaps its short wings to flush out insects from tall grass. It then plucks them with its stubby beak.

name: Yveltal BREAK
rules: BREAK Evolution Rule: Yveltal BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

attacks.name: Baleful Night
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon that has any damage counters on it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Hypno

attacks.name: Meditate
attacks.text: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hypnoblast
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: As a matter of course, it makes anyone it meets fall asleep and has a taste of their dreams. Anyone having a good dream, it carries off.

name: Crobat

abilities.name: Night Sight
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may draw a card.

attacks.name: Ultra-Toxic Fang
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 4 damage counters instead of 1 on that Pokémon between turns.

flavorText: Having four wings allows it to fly more quickly and quietly so it can sneak up on prey without its noticing.

name: Pikachu

attacks.name: Slam
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: This intelligent Pokémon roasts hard berries with electricity to make them tender enough to eat.

name: Jigglypuff

attacks.name: Lullaby
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: When its huge eyes light up, it sings a mysteriously soothing melody that lulls its enemies to sleep.

name: Starmie

attacks.name: Recover
attacks.text: Discard 1 Water Energy card attached to Starmie in order to use this attack. Remove all damage counters from Starmie.

attacks.name: Star Freeze
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Its central core glows with the seven colors of the rainbow. Some people value this core as a gem.

abilities.name: Pure Body
abilities.text: To attach a Water Energy card from your hand to Suicune, you must discard an Energy card attached to Suicune. (Attach the Water Energy, and then discard an Energy card from Suicune.)

attacks.name: Hypno Wave
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 20 more damage. If tails, this attack does 30 damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

name: Machoke

attacks.name: Karate Chop
attacks.text: This attack does 60 damage minus 10 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Submission
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: Its formidable body never gets tired. It helps people by doing work such as the moving of heavy goods.

name: Fiery Flint
rules: You can play this card only if you discard 2 other cards from your hand.
Search your deck for up to 4 Fire Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

attacks.name: Pebble Throw
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Double Pinchers
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Smog
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

name: Koffing

attacks.name: Smokescreen
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

flavorText: Its thin, filmy body is filled with gases that cause constant sniffles, coughs and teary eyes.

name: Gurdurr

attacks.name: Bulk Up
attacks.text: During your next turn, each of this Pokémon's attacks does 20 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: They strengthen their bodies by carrying steel beams. They show off their big muscles to their friends.

attacks.name: Poisonpowder
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

name: Litwick

attacks.name: Teleportation Burst
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: While shining a light and pretending to be a guide, it leaches off the life force of any who follow it.

attacks.name: Gesture
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon and switch it with the Defending Pokémon. Your opponent chooses the Defending Pokémon to switch.

name: Omanyte

abilities.name: Clairvoyance
abilities.text: Your opponent plays with his or her hand face up. This power stops working while Omanyte is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Water Gun
attacks.text: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each Water Energy attached to Omanyte but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. You can't add more than 20 damage in this way.

flavorText: Although long extinct, in rare cases, it can be genetically resurrected from fossils.

attacks.name: Horn Charge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Overrun
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads and if your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose 1 of them and this attack does 20 damage to it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Decidueye

attacks.name: Leaf Blade
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

attacks.name: Brave Bird
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: It fires arrow quills from its wings with such precision they can pierce a pebble at distances over a hundred yards.

name: Hatterene V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Confounding Pulse
attacks.text: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon. The new Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Mental Crush
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Confused, this attack does 90 more damage.

name: Marowak

attacks.name: Bone Lock
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Vortex Chop
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon has any Resistance, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: From its birth, this savage Pokémon constantly holds bones. It is skilled in using them as weapons.

name: Mareep
flavorText: Its fleece grows continually. In the summer, the fleece is fully shed, but it grows back in a week.

name: Pyukumuku

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Surprise Fist
attacks.text: You and your opponent play Rock-Paper-Scissors. If you win, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: The tradition known as Pyukumuku chucking started from the custom of throwing Pyukumuku back into the sea after they wash onshore.

name: Unown [A]

abilities.name: ANALYZE
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Unown A is on your Bench, you may look at the top 2 cards of your deck and put them back on top of your deck in any order.

flavorText: Shaped like ancient writing, it is a huge mystery whether language or UNOWN came first.

name: Eelektross

abilities.name: Energy Connect
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may move a basic Energy attached to 1 of your Benched Pokémon to your Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Electricannon
attacks.text: You may discard all Lightning Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 50 more damage.

flavorText: They crawl out of the ocean using their arms. They will attack prey on shore and immediately drag it into the ocean.

name: Hoppip
flavorText: Its body is so light, it must grip the ground firmly with its feet to keep from being blown away.

name: Excadrill

attacks.name: Tunnel Strike
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Dig Uppercut
attacks.text: Put a card from your discard pile into your hand.

flavorText: More than 300 feet below the surface, they build mazelike nests. Their activity can be destructive to subway tunnels.

name: Porygon

attacks.name: Data Check
attacks.text: Look through your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: A Pokémon that consists entirely of programming code. It is capable of moving freely in cyberspace.

name: Dragalge

abilities.name: Poison Barrier
abilities.text: Your opponent's Poisoned Pokémon can't retreat.

attacks.name: Poison Breath
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Their poison is strong enough to eat through the hull of a tanker, and they spit it indiscriminately at anything that enters their territory.

name: Gastrodon

attacks.name: Eerie Fluid
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Earthquake
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It has strong regenerative capabilities. Even if parts of it are bitten off by fish Pokémon, it will return to normal within a few hours.

name: Ampharos

attacks.name: Dazzle Blast
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Its tail shines bright and strong. It has been prized since long ago as a beacon for sailors.

name: Croagunk

attacks.name: Paralyzing Jab
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It rarely fights fairly, but that is strictly to ensure survival. It is popular as a mascot.

attacks.name: Psyshock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Cross Chop
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.

name: Malamar-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Hyper Hypnosis
abilities.text: When you attach an Energy from your hand to this Pokémon, you may use this Ability. Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: MAXamar
attacks.text: Flip a coin for each Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 60 damage times the number of heads.

name: Suicune

abilities.name: Frozen Flow
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, you may have your opponent switch their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Aurora Gain
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Said to be the embodiment of north winds, it can instantly purify filthy, murky water.

abilities.name: Duplicate
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for another Ditto and switch it with Ditto. (Any cards attached to Ditto, damage counters, Special Conditions, and effects on it are now on the new Pokémon.) If you do, put Ditto on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward. You can't use more than 1 Duplicate Poké-Power each turn.

attacks.name: Linear Attack
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Rayquaza-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Dragon Strike
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon can't use Dragon Strike during your next turn.

name: Bronzor

attacks.name: Gyro Swap
attacks.text: Put a number of damage counters on the Defending Pokémon equal to the number of Colorless Energy in Bronzor's Retreat Cost (after applying effects to the Retreat Cost).

attacks.name: Psyshock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Implements shaped like it were discovered in ancient tombs. It is unknown if they are related.

name: Giratina

attacks.name: Hex
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack does 50 more damage.

attacks.name: Shadow Claw
attacks.text: Discard a random card from your opponent's hand.

flavorText: It was banished for its violence. It silently gazed upon the old world from the Distortion World.

name: Alolan Raichu

attacks.name: Electro Rain
attacks.text: Discard any amount of Lightning Energy from this Pokémon. Then, for each Energy you discarded in this way, choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon and do 30 damage to it. (You can choose the same Pokémon more than once.) This damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

flavorText: It focuses psychic energy into its tail and rides it like it's surfing. Another name for this Pokémon is "hodad."

name: Shuppet
flavorText: It loves vengeful emotions and hangs in rows under the eaves of houses where vengeful people live.

name: Starmie

attacks.name: Strange Wave
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 in any combination of Water and Psychic Energy cards and attach them to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Its sparkling core is called "the gem of the sea." This core can be made into high-priced accessories that are traded in secret.

name: Sigilyph

attacks.name: Reflective Shield
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, if this Pokémon is damaged by an attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 5 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.

attacks.name: Psy Report
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals his or her hand.

flavorText: The guardians of an ancient city, they always fly the same route while keeping watch for invaders.

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

name: Clefairy

attacks.name: Moonlight
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It is said that happiness will come to those who see a gathering of Clefairy dancing under a full moon.

attacks.name: Flail
attacks.text: Does 10 damage times the number of damage counters on Corphish.

name: Axew

attacks.name: Strong Bond
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Supporter card named Iris, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: Its large tusks have a tendency to break, but each time they grow back, they grow in harder and sturdier.

name: Gastly

attacks.name: Sleep Poison
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep and Poisoned.

flavorText: Born from gases, anyone would faint if engulfed by its gaseous body, which contains poison.

attacks.name: Paralyzing Gaze
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Suspicious Food Tin
rules: Heal 80 damage from 1 of your Pokémon that has at least 1 Psychic Energy attached. If you healed any damage in this way, discard a Psychic Energy from it.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Wartortle

attacks.name: Withdraw
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to Wartortle during your opponent's next turn. (Any other effects of attacks still happen.)

flavorText: Often hides in water to stalk unweary prey. When swimming quickly, it moves its ears to maintain balance.

name: Mienfoo

attacks.name: Triple Smash
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 times the number of heads.

flavorText: They has mastered elegant combos. As they concentrate, their battle moves become swifter and more precise.

name: Greninja-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Shuriken Flurry
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may put 3 damage counters on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Haze Slash
attacks.text: You may shuffle this Pokémon and all cards attached to it into your deck.

attacks.name: Shadowy Hunter-GX
attacks.text: This attack does 130 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Electabuzz
flavorText: While it's often blamed for power outages, the truth is the cause of outages is more often an error on the part of the electric company.

attacks.name: Thundershock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Smoochum

abilities.name: Baby Evolution
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put Jynx from your hand onto Smoochum (this counts as evolving Smoochum) and remove all damage counters from Smoochum.

attacks.name: Psykiss
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, choose a Special Energy card attached to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon and have your opponent shuffle that card into his or her deck.

flavorText: It tests everything by touching with its lips, which remember what it likes and dislikes.

name: Gastly
flavorText: Born from gases, anyone would faint if engulfed by its gaseous body, which contains poison.

abilities.name: Knockout Gas
abilities.text: If Koffing is your Active Pokémon and is Knocked Out by an opponent's attack, the Attacking Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.

name: Torterra

attacks.name: Giga Drain
attacks.text: Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Earthquake
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Small Pokémon occasionally gather on its unmoving back to begin building their nests.

name: Bronzor

attacks.name: Iron Defense
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: Implements shaped like it were discovered in ancient tombs. It is unknown if they are related.

name: Silcoon

attacks.name: Ascension
attacks.text: Search your deck for a card that evolves from Silcoon and put it onto Silcoon. (This counts as evolving Silcoon.) Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Sticky String
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It wraps silk around the branches of a tree. It drinks rainwater on its silk while awaiting evolution.

name: Diglett

attacks.name: Sand Veil
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Diglett during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Mini Earthquake
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: If a Diglett digs through a field, it leaves the soil perfectly tilled and ideal for planting crops.

name: Koffing

attacks.name: Poison Gas
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: First discovered in a weapons factory, this Pokémon can now be found almost anywhere.

name: Araquanid

attacks.name: Liquidation
attacks.text: During your next turn, the Defending Pokémon takes 60 more damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: It usually passes its time in the water. When its belly is full, it stores its subdued prey in the water bubble on its head.

name: Espurr

attacks.name: Perplexing Eyes
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon's Weakness is now Psychic until the end of your next turn. (The amount of Weakness doesn't change.)

flavorText: The organ that emits its intense psychic power is sheltered by its ears to keep power from leaking out.

name: Floatzel

attacks.name: Jet Screw
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 10 more damage.

flavorText: It is a common sight around fishing ports. It is known to rescue people and carry off prey.

name: Lunala ◇
rules: ◇ (Prism Star) Rule: You can't have more than 1 ◇ card with the same name in your deck. If a ◇ card would go to the discard pile, put it in the Lost Zone instead.

attacks.name: Full Moon Star
attacks.text: For each of your opponent's Pokémon in play, attach a Psychic Energy card from your discard pile to your Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Psystorm
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage times the amount of Energy attached to all Pokémon.

flavorText: Said to live in another world, this Pokémon devours light, drawing the moonless dark veil of night over the brightness of day.

name: Sceptile-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Agility
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Strong Slash
attacks.text: This Pokémon can't use Strong Slash during your next turn.

name: Eelektross

attacks.name: Electrified Bite Mark
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, if they attach an Energy card from their hand to the Defending Pokémon, put 6 damage counters on that Pokémon.

attacks.name: Electro Sprinkler
attacks.text: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your Benched Pokémon and 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: They crawl out of the ocean using their arms. They will attack prey on shore and immediately drag it into the ocean.

name: Cyndaquil

attacks.name: Leer
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can't attack Cyndaquil during your opponent's next turn. (Benching or evolving either Pokémon ends this effect.)

attacks.name: Swift
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, Pokémon Powers, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: It usually stays hunched over. If it is angry or surprised, it shoots flames out of its back.

name: Dratini

attacks.name: Shed
attacks.text: Remove 1 damage counter from Dratini.

attacks.name: Fury Attack
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Large from birth, this Pokémon increases in size throughout its life by regularly shedding its skin.

name: Snom
flavorText: It eats snow that piles up on the ground. The more snow it eats, the bigger and more impressive the spikes on its back grow.

name: Zoroark-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Trade
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may discard a card from your hand. If you do, draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Riotous Beating
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each of your Pokémon in play.

attacks.name: Trickster-GX
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon's attacks and use it as this attack. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Sigilyph

abilities.name: Safeguard
abilities.text: Prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon by Pokémon-EX.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.text: Does 10 more damage for each Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: The guardians of an ancient city, they use their psychic power to attack enemies that invade their territory.

abilities.name: Poison Payback
abilities.text: If Cacnea is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent's attack (even if Cacnea is Knocked Out), the Attacking Pokémon is now Poisoned.

name: Tapu Lele-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Wonder Tag
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may search your deck for a Supporter card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Energy Drive
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage times the amount of Energy attached to both Active Pokémon. This damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

attacks.name: Tapu Cure-GX
attacks.text: Heal all damage from 2 of your Benched Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Ledian

attacks.name: Comet Punch
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Baton Pass
attacks.text: You may switch Ledian with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you do, move as many Energy cards attached to Ledian as you like to the new Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It uses starlight as energy. When more stars appear at night, the patterns on its back grow larger.

attacks.name: 3-Pronged Tail
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Full Speed Charge
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads to the Defending Pokémon and 20 damage times the number of tails to Blaine's Tauros.

name: Lunatone

attacks.name: Double Draw
attacks.text: Draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Moonblast
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 20 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Because it turns active on nights of the full moon, it is said to have some link to the lunar phases.

name: Drilbur
flavorText: It makes its way swiftly through the soil by putting both claws together and rotating at high speed.

name: Unown [F]

abilities.name: FAKE
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Unown F is on your Bench, you may use this power. Put a coin next to your Active Pokémon without showing your opponent and cover it with your hand. Your opponent guesses if the coin is heads or tails. If he or she is wrong, draw a card.

attacks.name: Hidden Power
attacks.text: You may flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. If heads, this attack's base damage is 30.

flavorText: Shaped like ancient writing, it is a huge mystery whether language or UNOWN came first.

name: Squirtle
flavorText: The shell is soft when it is born. It soon becomes so resilient, prodding fingers will bounce off it.

name: Unown [K]
rules: You may have up to 4 Basic Pokémon cards in your deck with Unown in their names.

abilities.name: [Keep]
abilities.text: Your opponent's attacks, Pokémon Powers, and Trainer cards can't discard Energy cards from your Pokémon with Unown in their names. (Any other effects still happen.)

flavorText: Their shapes look like hieroglyphs on ancient tablets. It is said that the two are somehow related.

name: Hariyama

abilities.name: Thick Fat
abilities.text: Any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent's Fire or Water Pokémon is reduced by 30 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: It stomps on the ground to build power. It can send a 10-ton truck flying with a straight-arm punch.

attacks.name: Telekinesis
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

name: Octillery

attacks.name: Crash Bomber
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon has any Special Energy cards attached to it, this attack does 30 damage plus 30 more damage. Then, discard a Special Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Aqua Liner
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. This attack does 40 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It lives in the gaps of boulders and in holes on the seafloor. Its suction cups grip prey tightly.

name: Croagunk

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Inflating its poison sacs, it makes an eerie blubbering sound for intimidation.

attacks.name: Stomp
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 20 more damage.

attacks.name: Giant Tail
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Hypnosis
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

name: Hariyama

attacks.name: Fake Out
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Pivot Throw
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is increased by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: It loves to match power with big-bodied Pokémon. It can knock a truck flying with its arm thrusts.

name: Carvanha

attacks.name: Scary Face
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can't attack or retreat during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Whirlpool
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: They swarm any foes that invades their territory. Their sharp fangs can tear out boat hulls.

name: Misty's Water Command
rules: Move any number of Water Energy from your Pokémon to your Psyduck, Horsea, Staryu, Starmie-GX, Magikarp, Gyarados, or Lapras in any way you like.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Parasect

attacks.name: Wild Spores
attacks.text: If Parasect evolved from Paras during this turn, this attack does 40 damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep and Poisoned.

attacks.name: Extend Fungus
attacks.text: Remove 2 damage counters from Parasect.

flavorText: A mushroom grown larger than the host's body controls PARASECT. It scatters poisonous spores.

name: Electrike

attacks.name: Thunderbolt
attacks.text: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It stores static electricity in its fur for discharging. It gives off sparks if a storm approaches.

name: Infernape

abilities.name: Flaming Fighter
abilities.text: Put 6 damage counters instead of 2 on your opponent's Burned Pokémon between turns.

attacks.name: Burst Punch
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: It tosses its enemies around with agility. It uses all its limbs to fight in its own unique style.

name: Pokémon Center Lady
rules: Heal 60 damage and remove all Special Conditions from 1 of your Pokémon.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Zubat

attacks.name: Poison Spray
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: While flying, it constantly emits ultrasonic waves from its mouth to check its surroundings.

name: Lillipup

attacks.name: Knock Away
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.

flavorText: It faces strong opponents with great courage. But, when at a disadvantage in a fight, this intelligent Pokémon flees.

name: Skarmory

attacks.name: Iron Wing
attacks.text: Discard a Metal Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: After nesting in bramble bushes, the wings of its chicks grow hard from scratches by thorns.

attacks.name: Fury Attack
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

name: Frosmoth

abilities.name: Ice Dance
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn, you may attach a Water Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Benched Water Pokémon.

flavorText: It shows no mercy to any who desecrate fields and mountains. It will fly around on its icy wings, causing a blizzard to chase offenders away.

name: Dratini

attacks.name: Gentle Wrap
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: This Pokémon is full of life energy. It continually sheds its skin and grows steadily larger.

name: Solrock

attacks.name: Cosmic Spin
attacks.text: If Lunatone is on your Bench, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: Solar energy is the source of its power, so it is strong during the daytime. When it spins, its body shines.

attacks.name: Snarl
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage to the Defending Pokémon. If tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Magnemite

attacks.name: Supersonic
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: It's frequently the cause of power outages, which is why some power plants send out electrical signals that it can't stand.

name: Team Yell Grunt
rules: Put an Energy attached to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon into their hand.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Dawn Wings Necrozma-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Invasion
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon is on your Bench, you may switch it with your Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Dark Flash
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

attacks.name: Moon's Eclipse-GX
attacks.text: You can use this attack only if you have more Prize cards remaining than your opponent. Prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: M Rayquaza-EX
rules: Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

ancientTrait.name: Δ Wild
ancientTrait.text: Any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent's Grass, Fire, Water, or Lightning Pokémon is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Dragon Ascent
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy attached to this Pokémon.

abilities.name: Strange Tentacles
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), as long as the number of Energy cards attached to the Defending Pokémon is less than the number of Energy cards attached to your Active Pokémon, you may choose an Energy card, if any, in your opponent's discard pile and attach it to the Defending Pokémon. This power can't be used if Tentacruel is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

name: Kabuto

abilities.name: Revive Friends
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a card named Kabuto and put it on your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward. Treat the new Kabuto as a Basic Pokémon. This power can't be used if Kabuto is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed (or if your Bench is full).

attacks.name: Work Together
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each Omanyte, Omastar, Kabuto, and Kabutops on your Bench.

flavorText: On rare occasions, some have been found as fossils which they became while hiding on the ocean floor.

name: Lucario

attacks.name: Focus Blast
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Spike Lariat
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 60 damage plus 20 more damage.

flavorText: It has the ability to sense the auras of all things. It understands human speech.

name: Stantler

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Mystifying Horns
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: The curved antlers subtly change the flow of air to create a strange space where reality is distorted.

attacks.name: Spearhead
attacks.text: Draw a card.

name: Tapu Lele-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Wonder Tag
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may search your deck for a Supporter card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Energy Drive
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage times the amount of Energy attached to both Active Pokémon. This damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

attacks.name: Tapu Cure-GX
attacks.text: Heal all damage from 2 of your Benched Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Exeggcute

attacks.name: Sleep Powder
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Psyshock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Its shell is very durable, allowing it to survive even if the shell becomes cracked.

name: Staryu
flavorText: An enigmatic Pokémon that can effortlessly regenerate any appendage it loses in battle.

name: Umbreon

abilities.name: Dark Shade
abilities.text: Each of your Team Plasma Pokémon in play gets +20 HP.

flavorText: When exposed to the moon's aura, the rings on its body glow faintly and it's filled with a mysterious power.

name: Trubbish

attacks.name: Acid Spray
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Inhaling the gas they belch will make you sleep for a week. They prefer unsanitary places.

name: Clefable

attacks.name: Follow Me
attacks.text: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Moonblast
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 30 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Its hearing is so acute it can hear a pin drop over half a mile away. It lives on quiet mountains.

name: Throh

attacks.name: Circle Throw
attacks.text: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Storm Throw
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: When they encounter foes bigger than themselves, they try to throw them. They always travel in packs of five.

name: Cherubi

attacks.name: Nap
attacks.text: Remove 2 damage counters from Cherubi.

attacks.name: Bullet Seed
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: The small ball holds the nutrients needed for evolution. Apparently, it is very sweet and tasty.

name: Bronzong

abilities.name: Metal Fortress
abilities.text: Prevent all effects of your opponent's attacks, including damage, done to your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Guard Press
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Ancient people believed that petitioning Bronzong for rain was the way to make crops grow.

attacks.name: Energy Burst
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage times the number of Energy attached to Starmie and the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Star Back
attacks.text: Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Pokémon.

name: Weepinbell

attacks.name: Poisonpowder
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It spits out poisonpowder to immobilize the enemy, and then finishes the enemy with a spray of acid.

name: Lampent

attacks.name: Luring Light
attacks.text: Switch the Defending Pokémon with 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: This ominous Pokémon is feared. Through cities it wanders, searching for the spirits of the fallen.

attacks.name: Mislead
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. If either of them is heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Ethereal Flame
attacks.text: Discard all Fire Energy cards attached to Ninetales. This attack does 30 damage plus 20 more damage for each card discarded this way.

name: Stufful
flavorText: Despite its adorable appearance, when it gets angry and flails about, its arms and legs could knock a pro wrestler sprawling.

name: Geodude

attacks.name: Stone Barrage
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: At rest, it looks just like a rock. Carelessly stepping on it will make it swing its fists angrily.

name: Pineco
flavorText: It hangs and waits for flying insect prey to come near. It does not move about much on its own.

name: Mawile

attacks.name: Selfish Draw
attacks.text: Look at the top card of your deck. You may put it into your hand. If not, discard it and draw a card.

attacks.name: Destructive Jaw
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed and discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: It chomps with its gaping mouth. Its huge jaws are actually steel horns that have been transformed.

name: Mr. Mime

abilities.name: Scoop-Up Block
abilities.text: Your opponent's Pokémon that have any damage counters on them, and any cards attached to those Pokémon, can't be put into your opponent's hand.

attacks.name: Psy Bolt
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Its pantomime skills are wonderful. You may become enraptured while watching it, but next thing you know, Mr. Mime has made a real wall.

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

name: Goomy

attacks.name: Gooey
attacks.text: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: The weakest Dragon-type Pokémon, it lives in damp, shady places, so its body doesn't dry out.

abilities.name: Natural Healing
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may remove 1 damage counter from Blaine's Vulpix. This power can't be used if Blaine's Vulpix is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Tail Fan
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

name: Gabite

attacks.name: Healing Scale
attacks.text: Remove 1 damage counter from each of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Sand Tomb
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It loves sparkly things. It seeks treasures in caves and hoards the loot in its nest.

name: Yveltal

attacks.name: Strafe
attacks.text: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Oblivion Wing
attacks.text: Attach a Darkness Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: When this legendary Pokémon's wings and tail feathers spread wide and glow red, it absorbs the life force of living creatures.

name: Blaziken-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Fist of Focus
attacks.text: Attach an Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Flare Storm
attacks.text: Flip a coin for each Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.

name: Lance ◇
rules: You can play this card only if 1 of your Pokémon was Knocked Out during your opponent's last turn.
Search your deck for up to 2 Dragon Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).
◇ (Prism Star) Rule: You can't have more than 1 ◇ card with the same name in your deck. If a ◇ card would go to the discard pile, put it in the Lost Zone instead.

name: Electrode-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Extra Energy Bomb
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may attach 5 Energy cards from your discard pile to your Pokémon, except Pokémon-GX or Pokémon-EX, in any way you like. If you do, this Pokémon is Knocked Out.

attacks.name: Crush and Burn-GX
attacks.text: Discard any amount of Energy from your Pokémon. This attack does 50 more damage for each card you discarded in this way. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Croagunk

attacks.name: Poison Jab
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Inflating its poison sacs, it fills the area with an odd sound and hits flinching opponents with a poison jab.

name: Unown [B]

abilities.name: BOUNCE
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Unown B is on your Bench, you may flip a coin. If heads, discard all cards attached to any 1 of your Unown and shuffle that Pokémon back into your deck.

flavorText: Shaped like ancient writing, it is a huge mystery whether language or UNOWN came first.

name: Fraxure

attacks.name: Paralyzing Gaze
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: A broken tusk will not grow back, so it diligently sharpens its tusks on river rocks after the end of a battle.

attacks.name: Thundershock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Excadrill

attacks.name: Drill Run
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: It can help in tunnel construction. Its drill has evolved into steel strong enough to bore through iron plates.

name: Nidoran ♀
flavorText: Small and very docile, it protects itself with its small, poisonous horn when attacked.

attacks.name: Influence
attacks.text: Search your deck for Omanyte, Kabuto, Aerodactyl, Lileep, or Anorith and put up to 2 of them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward. Treat the new Benched Pokémon as Basic Pokémon.

name: Electivire

attacks.name: Thunder Shock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Electrified Bolt
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any Special Energy attached, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: A single Electivire can provide enough electricity for all the buildings in a big city for a year.

attacks.name: Supersonic
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Call Will-o'-the-wisp
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. For each heads, if you have a Fire Energy card in your discard pile, put it into your hand.

name: Trubbish

attacks.name: Pile Up
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a Pokémon Tool card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: The combination of garbage bags and industrial waste caused the chemical reaction that created this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Linear Attack
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Machop
flavorText: It's said that not even pro wrestlers can take down a Machop.

name: Pikachu
flavorText: Its nature is to store up electricity. Forests where nests of Pikachu live are dangerous, since the trees are so often struck by lightning.

attacks.name: Aqua Wave
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage.

name: Ledyba

attacks.name: Comet Punch
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: When the weather turns cold, lots of Ledyba gather from everywhere to cluster and keep each other warm.

name: Tsareena-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Side Eye
attacks.text: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon. The new Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Jumping Side Kick
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Confused, this attack does 90 more damage.

attacks.name: Queen's Command-GX
attacks.text: Your opponent discards 4 cards from their hand. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Morpeko V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Spark
attacks.text: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Electro Wheel
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, switch it with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Stun Spore
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Trapinch

attacks.name: Mountain Munch
attacks.text: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

flavorText: It lives in arid deserts. It makes a sloping pit trap in sand where it patiently awaits prey.

attacks.name: Super Thunder Wave
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, each Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Drifloon

attacks.name: Delivery
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, put any 1 card from your discard pile into your hand.

attacks.name: Reaction
attacks.text: Switch Drifloon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It tugs on the hands of children to steal them away. However, it gets pulled around instead.

name: Meowstic

attacks.name: Allure
attacks.text: Draw 3 cards.

attacks.name: Hand Kinesis
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage for each card in your hand.

flavorText: When in danger, it raises its ears and releases enough psychic power to grind a 10-ton truck to dust.

name: Nosepass
flavorText: When endangered, it may protect itself by raising its magnetism and drawing iron objects to its body.

name: Haunter

abilities.name: Gothic Fear
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may leave both Active Pokémon Confused.

attacks.name: Poison Ring
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. That Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It licks with its gaseous tongue to steal the victim's life force. It lurks in darkness for prey.

name: Espeon-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Psybeam
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage times the amount of Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Divide-GX
attacks.text: Put 10 damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Unown [K]

abilities.name: Kind
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may remove 2 damage counters from 1 of the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: Shaped like ancient writing, it is a huge mystery whether language or UNOWN came first.

name: Eevee

attacks.name: Signs of Evolution
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 cards that evolve from Eevee, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Bounce
attacks.text: Switch Eevee with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Because its genetic makeup is irregular, it quickly changes its form due to a variety of causes.

name: Zekrom

attacks.name: Wild Shock
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 60 damage to itself. Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: When the interior part of its tail spins like a motor, Zekrom can generate many bolts of lightning to blast its surroundings.

attacks.name: Spark
attacks.text: Choose 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to each of those Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Porygon2

attacks.name: Tri Attack
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: With planetary development software installed, it became capable of working in space.

name: Trubbish

attacks.name: Poison Gas
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Inhaling the gas they belch will make you sleep for a week. They prefer unsanitary places.

name: Machamp

abilities.name: Counterattack
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent's attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 3 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.

flavorText: Its four ruggedly developed arms can launch a flurry of 1,000 punches in just two seconds.

name: Suicune-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Phantom Winds
