abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon is on your Bench, you may shuffle it and all cards attached to it into your deck.

attacks.name: Cure Stream
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon's attacks do 30 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Brinicles-GX
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

abilities.name: Duplicate
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for another Ditto and switch it with Ditto. (Any cards attached to Ditto, damage counters, Special Conditions, and effects on it are now on the new Pokémon.) If you do, put Ditto on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward. You can't use more than 1 Duplicate Poké-Power each turn.

attacks.name: Thundershock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Mienshao

attacks.name: Haul In
attacks.text: Search your deck for 2 Pokémon Tool cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Meditate
attacks.text: Does 10 more damage for each damage counter on the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: They use the long fur on their arms as a whip to strike their opponents.

attacks.name: Jolt
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Unown [G]

abilities.name: GUARD
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Unown G is on your Bench, you may discard all cards attached to Unown G and attach Unown G to 1 of your Pokémon as a Pokémon Tool card. As long as Unown G is attached to a Pokémon, prevent all effects of attacks, excluding damage, done to that Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hidden Power
attacks.text: If Unown G has any damage counters on it, this attack's base damage is 10.

flavorText: Shaped like ancient writing, it is a huge mystery whether language or UNOWN came first.

name: Steelix-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Wild Edge
attacks.text: You may do 50 more damage. If you do, this Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.

attacks.name: Iron Tail
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 100 damage times the number of heads.

name: Omastar

attacks.name: Water Gun
attacks.text: Does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each Water Energy attached to Omastar but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. You can't add more than 20 damage in this way.

attacks.name: Spike Cannon
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: A prehistoric Pokémon that died out when its heavy shell made it impossible for it to catch prey.

name: Shuppet

attacks.name: Perplex
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: It loves vengeful emotions and hangs in rows under the eaves of houses where vengeful people live.

name: Mankey

abilities.name: Peek
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may look at one of the following: the top card of either player's deck, a random card from your opponent's hand, or one of either player's Prizes. This power can't be used if Mankey is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

flavorText: Extremely quick to anger. It could be docile one moment, then thrashing away the next.

name: Vigoroth

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, Vigoroth does 10 damage to itself.

attacks.name: Fight Back
attacks.text: If Vigoroth has any damage counters on it, this attack does 50 damage plus 20 more damage.

flavorText: Its heart beats at a tenfold tempo, so it cannot sit still even for a moment.

name: Cyndaquil

attacks.name: Fireworks
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, discard 1 Energy card attached to Cyndaquil.

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage; if tails, this attack does 10 damage.

flavorText: It is timid and always curls itself up in a ball. If attacked, it flares up its back for protection.

attacks.name: Thunder Wave
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Bouncing Ball
attacks.text: Voltorb does 10 damage to itself.

name: Pikachu

attacks.name: Nuzzle
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Volt Tackle
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: Its nature is to store up electricity. Forests where nests of Pikachu live are dangerous, since the trees are so often struck by lightning.

name: Excadrill

attacks.name: Reinforced Drill
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool card attached to it, this attack does 30 more damage.

attacks.name: Mach Claw
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: It can help in tunnel construction. Its drill has evolved into steel strong enough to bore through iron plates.

attacks.name: Charge
attacks.text: Search your discard pile for a Lightning Energy card and attach it to Pikachu.

attacks.name: Slam
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

name: Raichu

attacks.name: Never Give Up
attacks.text: You can use this attack only if you have at least 3 more Prize cards remaining than your opponent. Attach all Lightning Energy cards from your discard pile to your Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: As electricity builds up inside its body, it becomes more aggressive. One theory is that the electricity buildup is actually causing stress.

name: Bronzor
flavorText: Ancient people believed that the pattern on Bronzor's back contained a mysterious power.

name: Hoopa

abilities.name: Scoundrel Guard
abilities.text: Prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon by your opponent's Pokémon-GX or Pokémon-EX.

flavorText: In its true form, it possess a huge amount of power. Legends of its avarice tell how it once carried off an entire castle to gain the treasure hidden within.

name: Swampert-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Mud Flood
attacks.text: Reveal the top 4 cards of your deck. This attack does 40 more damage for each Water Energy you find there. Shuffle the revealed cards back into your deck.

attacks.name: Hydro Tackle
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.

name: Unown [C]

abilities.name: CALL
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Unown C is on your Bench, you may flip coin. If heads, search your deck for any 1 Unown and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: Shaped like ancient writing, it is a huge mystery whether language or UNOWN came first.

name: Banette

abilities.name: Red Eyes
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may put a Basic Pokémon from your opponent's discard pile onto their Bench.

attacks.name: Enemy Show
attacks.text: For each of your opponent's Pokémon in play, put 1 damage counter on your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: Strong feelings of hatred turned a puppet into a Pokémon. If it opens its mouth, its cursed energy escapes.

name: Sabrina's Suggestion
rules: Your opponent reveals their hand. You may choose a Supporter card you find there and use the effect of that card as the effect of this card.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Misdreavus

attacks.name: Dual Draw
attacks.text: Each player draws 3 cards.

flavorText: It loves to bite and yank people's hair from behind without warning, just to see their shocked reactions.

attacks.name: Singe
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

name: Primeape

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Tantrum
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, Primeape is now Confused (after doing damage).

flavorText: Always furious and tenacious to boot. It will not abandon chasing its quarry until its quarry is caught.

attacks.name: Random Spark
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Darkness Call
attacks.text: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon, if any.

name: Abra

attacks.name: Psyshock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Using its ability to read minds, it will identify impending danger and teleport to safety.

name: Turffield Stadium
rules: Once during each player's turn, that player may search their deck for an Evolution Grass Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into their hand. Then, that player shuffles their deck.
This Stadium stays in play when you play it. Discard it if another Stadium comes into play. If a Stadium with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

name: Switch Raft
rules: Switch your Active Water Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you do, heal 30 damage from the Pokémon you moved to your Bench.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Luxio

attacks.name: Spark
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Thunderous Claw
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, discard all Lightning Energy attached to Luxio.

flavorText: Its claws loose electricity with enough amperage to cause fainting. They live in small groups.

name: Tangela

attacks.name: Bind
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Poisonpowder
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Its whole body is swathed with wide vines that are similar to seaweed. These vines shake as it walks.

name: Sneasel

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: A smart and sneaky Pokémon, it makes its opponents flinch by suddenly showing the claws hidden in its paws.

attacks.name: Dig Drain
attacks.text: Remove 1 damage counter from Larvitar.

abilities.name: Miracle Shift
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), discard a basic Energy card attached to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, choose a basic Energy card from your discard pile and attach it to that Pokémon. This power can't be used if Togetic is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Mini-Metronome
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon's attacks. Mini-Metronome copies that attack except for its Energy cost. (You must still do anything else required in order to use that attack.) (No matter what type the Defending Pokémon is, Togetic is still Colorless.) Togetic performs that attack.

name: Gengar

attacks.name: Sinister Fog
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 1 damage counter on each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Creep Show
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has 3 or more damage counters on it, that Pokémon is Knocked Out.

flavorText: Hiding in people's shadows at night, it absorbs their heat. The chill it causes makes the victims shake.

attacks.name: Dig Deep
attacks.text: Search your discard pile for an Energy card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand.

name: Bronzong BREAK
rules: BREAK Evolution Rule: Bronzong BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

attacks.name: Metal Rain
attacks.text: Discard as many Metal Energy attached to this Pokémon as you like. For each Energy card discarded in this way, choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon and do 30 damage to it. Don't apply Weakness and Resistance. (You may choose the same Pokémon more than once.)

name: Flygon

abilities.name: Sand Slammer
abilities.text: At any time between turns, if this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, put 1 damage counter on each of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Flying Beatdown
attacks.text: You may discard a Grass Energy and a Fighting Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you do, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Known as "The Desert Spirit," this Pokémon hides in the sandstorms it causes by beating its wings.

attacks.name: Double Tackle
attacks.text: Does 20 damage to each Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Whirlpool
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

name: Gible

attacks.name: Ascension
attacks.text: Search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It skulks in caves, and when prey or an enemy passes by, it leaps out and chomps them. The force of its attack sometimes chips its teeth.

name: Pichu
rules: If your Active Pokémon is a Baby Pokémon and your opponent announces an attack, your opponent flips a coin (before doing anything else). If tails, your opponent's turn ends.

attacks.name: Energy Patch
attacks.text: Take a basic Energy card attached to 1 of your Pokémon and attach it to another of your Pokémon.

name: M Steelix-EX
rules: Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Canyon Axe
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Earthquake
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Manectric

attacks.name: Strafe
attacks.text: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Flash Impact
attacks.text: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It stimulates its own muscles with electricity, so it can move quickly. It eases its soreness with electricity, too, so it can recover quickly as well.

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Tremor
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to each of your own Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Pebble Throw
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Sneasel

attacks.name: Corner
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: A smart and sneaky Pokémon. A pair may work together to steal eggs by having one lure the parents away.

name: Magnemite

attacks.name: Magnetism
attacks.text: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each Magnemite, Magneton, and Dark Magneton on your Bench.

flavorText: It is said that enough Magnemites and Magnetons gather in one place, they can create a powerful magnetic barrier.

name: Timburr

attacks.name: Pummel
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: These Pokémon appear at building sites and help out with construction. They always carry squared logs.

name: Timburr
flavorText: These Pokémon appear at building sites and help out with construction. They always carry squared logs.

name: Balloon Berry
rules: Attach Balloon Berry to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.
When the Pokémon Balloon Berry is attached to retreats, discard Balloon Berry instead of discarding Energy cards.

name: Wailmer

attacks.name: Tsunami
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: On sunny days, it lands on beaches to bounce like a ball and play. It spouts water from its nose.

attacks.name: Dig Under
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

name: Combee
rules: Honey: Once during your turn, when you put Combee from your hand onto your Bench, you may search your discard pile for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench.

attacks.name: Alert
attacks.text: Draw a card. Then, you may switch Combee with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Psywave
attacks.text: Does 10 damage times the amount of Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

name: Wartortle

attacks.name: Double Slap
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Rocket Tackle
attacks.text: Wartortle does 10 damage to itself. Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to Wartortle by attacks during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It is said to live 10,000 years. Its furry tail is popular as a symbol of longevity.

name: Bewear

abilities.name: Fluffy
abilities.text: This Pokémon takes 30 less damage from the attacks of your opponent's non-Fire Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Cross-Cut
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is an Evolution Pokémon, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: This immensely dangerous Pokémon possesses overwhelming physical strength. Its habitat is generally off-limits.

name: Garbodor

abilities.name: Garbotoxin
abilities.text: If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool card attached to it, each Pokémon in play, in each player's hand, and in each player's discard pile has no Abilities (except for Garbotoxin).

attacks.name: Offensive Bomb
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.

flavorText: Consuming garbage makes new kinds of poison gases and liquids inside their bodies.

name: Toxicroak

attacks.name: Revenge
attacks.text: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack during his or her last turn, this attack does 70 more damage.

attacks.name: Poison Jab
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: The croaking that Toxicroak produces before a battle is for churning the poison it has stored in its poison sac.

name: Eevee

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for as many Eevee as you like and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Lunge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: A rare Pokémon that adapts to harsh environments by taking on different evolutionary forms.

name: Mr. Mime

abilities.name: Bench Barrier
abilities.text: Prevent all damage done to your Benched Pokémon by attacks.

attacks.name: Juggling
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It is adept at conning people. It is said to be able to create walls out of thin air by miming.

name: Porygon-Z

attacks.name: Cyber Crush
attacks.text: Discard all Special Energy attached to each of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Slowing Beam
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon's attacks cost Colorless more.

flavorText: Its programming was modified to enable it to travel through alien dimensions. Seems there might have been an error…

name: Nidorina
flavorText: When feeding its young, it first chews the food into a paste, then spits it out for the offspring.

name: Haxorus

attacks.name: Dragonaxe
attacks.text: Does 40 damage times the amount of Metal Energy attached to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Strike of the Champion
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is a Team Plasma Pokémon, it is Knocked Out. (If the Defending Pokémon is not a Team Plasma Pokémon, this attack does nothing.)

flavorText: Its tusks are incredibly destructive. They can easily slice through a thick, sturdy steel column every time.

name: Sliggoo

attacks.name: Gooey
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It drives away opponents by excreting a sticky liquid that can dissolve anything. Its eyes devolved, so it can't see anything.

name: Pupitar

attacks.name: Pressurized Gas
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Rocket Tackle
attacks.text: Pupitar does 10 damage to itself. Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to Pupitar by attacks during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: Its body is as hard as bedrock. By venting pressurized gas, it can launch itself like a rocket.

name: Baltoy

attacks.name: Reverse Spin
attacks.text: Your opponent shuffles his or her hand into his or her deck and draws 4 cards.

flavorText: It move by spinning on its foot. It is a rare Pokémon that was discovered in ancient ruins.

name: Magikarp
flavorText: For no reason, it jumps and splashes about, making it easy for predators like Pidgeotto to catch it mid-jump.

name: Sawk
flavorText: The sound of Sawk punching boulders and trees can be heard all the way from the mountains where they train.

name: Gastly

attacks.name: Nightmare
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Its gas-like body allows it to enter almost anywhere, but it is vulnerable to high winds.

attacks.name: Water Gun
attacks.text: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each Water Energy attached to Mudkip but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. You can't add more than 20 damage in this way.

name: Ho-Oh-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Sacred Fire
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Phoenix Burn
attacks.text: This Pokémon can't use Phoenix Burn during your next turn.

attacks.name: Eternal Light-GX
attacks.text: Put 3 in any combination of Fire Pokémon-GX or Fire Pokémon-EX from your discard pile onto your Bench. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Gulpin

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

flavorText: Almost all of its body is its stomach. Its harsh digestive juices quickly dissolve anything it swallows.

name: Magneton

attacks.name: Tri Attack
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Delicate equipment can malfunction in areas inhabited by Magneton, which send out mysterious electrical signals.

name: Lunala

attacks.name: Shatter Shot
attacks.text: This attack does 40 damage times the amount of Psychic Energy attached to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Wings of the Moone
attacks.text: Move all Energy from this Pokémon to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: It is said to be a female evolution of Cosmog. When its third eye activates, away it flies to another world.

name: Hydreigon-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Dragon Road
abilities.text: If there is any Stadium card in play, the Retreat Cost of each of your Dragon Pokémon in play is ColorlessColorless less.

attacks.name: Shred
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Call for Friend
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, you may search your deck for a Basic Pokémon card with Brock in its name and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward. (You can't use this attack if your Bench is full.)

attacks.name: Hook Shot
attacks.text: Don't apply Resistance for this attack. (Any other effects that would happen after applying Resistance still happen.)

name: Cubchoo

attacks.name: Secondary Chills
attacks.text: You can use this attack only if you go second, and only on your first turn. Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Their snot is a barometer of health. When healthy, their snot is sticky and the power of their ice moves increases.

name: Xurkitree-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Flashing Head
abilities.text: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent's Pokémon that have any Special Energy attached to them.

attacks.name: Rumbling Wires
attacks.text: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Lighting-GX
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals their hand. Add a card you find there to their Prize cards face down. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Cranidos
flavorText: In rock layers where Cranidos fossils are found, the fossilized trunks of trees snapped in two are also often found.

name: Chandelure

abilities.name: Cursed Shadow
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, you may put 3 damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Eerie Glow
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned and Confused.

flavorText: It absorbs a spirit, which it then burns. By waving the flames on its arms, it puts its foes into a hypnotic trance.

name: Unown [N]

abilities.name: Nod
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if you have Unown N, Unown O, and Unown D on your Bench, you may ask your opponent to take a Prize card. If he or she does, you take a Prize card. If he or she doesn't, draw a card.

attacks.name: Hidden Power
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: Shaped like ancient writing, it is a huge mystery whether language or UNOWN came first.

name: Excadrill

attacks.name: Dig
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Earthquake
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: More than 300 feet below the surface, they build mazelike nests. Their activity can be destructive to subway tunnels.

name: Zeraora

attacks.name: Fighting Lightning
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon V or Pokémon-GX, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: It runs as fast as lightning strikes, shredding its opponents with its high-voltage claws.

attacks.name: Fire Tail Slap
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, discard a Fire Energy attached to Flareon .

name: Larvitar
flavorText: It feeds on soil. After it has eaten a large mountain, it will fall asleep so it can grow.

name: Machamp BREAK
rules: BREAK Evolution Rule: Machamp BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

attacks.name: Boomerang Lariat
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon's attacks do 100 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

name: Meowth

attacks.name: Pay Day
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, draw a card.

flavorText: Adores circular objects. Wanders the streets on a nightly basis to look for dropped loose change.

name: Girafarig

attacks.name: Agility
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Girafarig.

attacks.name: Psybeam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Its tail has a small brain of its own. Beware! If you get close, it may react to your scent and bite.

name: Pineco

attacks.name: Focus Energy
attacks.text: During your next turn, Pineco's Surprise Attack attack's base damage is 80.

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It likes to make its shell thicker by adding layers of tree bark. The additional weight doesn't bother it.

name: Grimmsnarl V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Spiky Knuckle
attacks.text: Put 2 Darkness Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand.

abilities.name: Rare Metal
abilities.text: All basic Energy cards attached to Steelix provide Metal Energy instead of their usual types.

attacks.name: Squeeze
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Metal Tail
attacks.text: Before doing damage, you may flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 80 damage. If tails, this attack does nothing.

name: Lucario-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Missile Jab
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

attacks.name: Corkscrew Smash
attacks.text: You may draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.

name: Mr. Mime-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Magic Odds
abilities.text: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by your opponent's attacks if that damage is exactly 10, 30, 50, 70, 90, 110, 130, 150, 170, 190, 210, 230, or 250.

attacks.name: Breakdown
attacks.text: For each card in your opponent's hand, put 1 damage counter on their Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Life Trick-GX
attacks.text: Heal all damage from this Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Gastly

attacks.name: Sneaky Placement
attacks.text: Put 1 damage counter on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: Its body is made of gas. Despite lacking substance, it can envelop an opponent of any size and cause suffocation.

name: Teddiursa

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.text: Teddiursa does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: Before food becomes scarce in wintertime, its habit is to hoard food in many hidden locations.

name: Raichu

attacks.name: Nuzzle
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Powerful Spark
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage times the amount of Lightning Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.

flavorText: As electricity builds up inside its body, it becomes more aggressive. One theory is that the electricity buildup is actually causing stress.

name: Trubbish
flavorText: Wanting more garbage, they follow people who litter. They always belch poison gas.

name: Pikachu

attacks.name: Charge
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Lightning Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Pikachu that can generate powerful electricity have cheek sacs that are extra soft and super stretchy.

name: Sandygast

attacks.name: Absorb Life
attacks.text: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Born from a sand mound playfully built by a child, this Pokémon embodies the grudges of the departed.

name: Garbodor

abilities.name: Garbotoxin
abilities.text: If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool card attached to it, each Pokémon in play, in each player's hand, and in each player's discard pile has no Abilities (except for Garbotoxin).

flavorText: It clenches opponents with its left arm and finishes them off with foul-smelling poison gas belched from its mouth.

attacks.name: Double-edge
attacks.text: Koffing does 10 damage to itself.

attacks.name: Leap Out
attacks.text: Switch Magikarp with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Rage
attacks.text: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Magikarp.

name: Mareanie

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It plunges the poison spike on its head into its prey. When the prey has weakened, Mareanie deals the finishing blow with its 10 tentacles.

abilities.name: Duplicate
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for another Ditto and switch it with Ditto. (Any cards attached to Ditto, damage counters, Special Conditions, and effects on it are now on the new Pokémon.) If you do, put Ditto on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward. You can't use more than 1 Duplicate Poké-Power each turn.

attacks.name: Spiral Drain
attacks.text: Remove 3 damage counter from Ditto (all if there are less than 3).

name: Shinx

attacks.name: Under Pressure
attacks.text: If your opponent has 3 or fewer Prize cards remaining, this attack does 50 more damage.

flavorText: All of its fur dazzles if danger is sensed. It flees while the foe is momentarily blinded.

name: Zangoose

attacks.name: Cutting Claw
attacks.text: If your opponent has Seviper in play, this attack does 40 damage plus 50 more damage.

flavorText: It has feuded with SEVIPER for many generations. Its sharp claws are its biggest weapons.

attacks.name: Shell Scatter
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Forretress G can't use Shell Scatter during your next turn.

attacks.name: Bomb Risk
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's). (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Flip a coin. If tails, Forretress G does 80 damage to itself.

name: Goodra

abilities.name: Gooey Regeneration
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you do, heal 60 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Heavy Whip
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavorText: This very friendly Dragon-type Pokémon will hug its beloved Trainer, leaving that Trainer covered in sticky slime.

name: Quilava

attacks.name: Fireworks
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, discard a Fire Energy attached to Quilava.

flavorText: It intimidates foes with the heat of its flames. The fire burn more strongly when it readies to fight.

abilities.name: Thick Skin
abilities.text: Zangoose can't be affected by any Special Conditions.

attacks.name: Invite and Strike
attacks.text: Switch the Defending Pokémon with 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to the new Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Chop Up
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon that has any damage counters on it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Mankey

attacks.name: Mischief
attacks.text: Shuffle your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Anger
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage; if tails, this attack does 20 damage.

flavorText: Normally friendly, it quickly becomes angry if it doesn't get what it wants.

name: Gurdurr

attacks.name: Pummel
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

attacks.name: Hammer Arm
attacks.text: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

flavorText: With strengthened bodies, they skillfully wield steel beams to take down buildings.

name: Phanpy

abilities.name: Ultra-Thick Skin
abilities.text: As long as Phanpy has Energy attached to it, any damage done to Phanpy by attacks is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Rock Smash
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage.

flavorText: As a sign of affection, it bumps with its snout. However, it is so strong, it may send you flying.

name: Gabite

attacks.name: Ascension
attacks.text: Search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Shiny objects are its passion. It can be found in its cave, scarcely moving, its gaze fixed on the jewels it's amassed or Carbink it has caught.

name: Rampardos

attacks.name: Clean Hit
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is an Evolution Pokémon, this attack does 60 more damage.

attacks.name: Wild Crash
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Basic Pokémon, it is Knocked Out.

flavorText: Records exist of a revived fossil that evolved into Rampardos. It proceeded to escape and then destroy a skyscraper with a headbutt.

name: Drednaw V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Solid Shell
abilities.text: This Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Powerful Bite
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

name: Palossand

attacks.name: Sand Tomb
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Dust Storm
attacks.text: Discard all cards from both Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Possessed people controlled by this Pokémon transformed its sand mound into a castle. As it evolved, its power to curse grew ever stronger.

name: Lucario

attacks.name: Vacuum Wave
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

attacks.name: Fight Alone
attacks.text: If you have fewer Pokémon in play than your opponent, this attack does 60 more damage for each Pokémon fewer you have in play.

flavorText: By reading the auras of all things, it can tell how others are feeling from over half a mile away.

name: Rapidash

attacks.name: Stomp
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage; if tails, this attack does 20 damage.

attacks.name: Agility
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Rapidash.

flavorText: Very competitive, this Pokémon will chase anything that moves fast in the hope of racing it.

name: Weavile

attacks.name: Hail
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Vilify
attacks.text: Discard as many Pokémon as you like from your hand. This attack does 30 damage times the number of Pokémon you discarded.

flavorText: They communicate by clawing signs in boulders and work together to surround enemies.

name: Rattata
flavorText: Living wherever there is food available, it ceaselessly scavenges for edibles the entire day.

name: Beartic

attacks.name: Resolute Claws
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon-GX or a Pokémon-EX, this attack does 60 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Blizzard Burn
attacks.text: This Pokémon can't attack during your next turn.

flavorText: It freezes its breath to create fangs and claws of ice to fight with. Cold northern areas are its habitat.

name: Alcremie

attacks.name: Decorate
attacks.text: Attach any number of basic Energy cards from your hand to your Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Draining Kiss
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: When it trusts a Trainer, it will treat them to berries it's decorated with cream.

abilities.name: Exoskeleton
abilities.text: Any damage done to Clamperl by attacks is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Bubble
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Magnemite

attacks.name: Mirror Shot
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack doesn't happen.

flavorText: It's frequently the cause of power outages, which is why some power plants send out electrical signals that it can't stand.

name: Weavile

attacks.name: Dark Penalty
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon has no Pokémon Tool card attached to it, this attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Evolution made it even more devious. It communicates by clawing signs in boulders.

name: Onix

attacks.name: Harden
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, if this Pokémon would be damaged by an attack, prevent that attack's damage done to this Pokémon if that damage is 60 or less.

flavorText: It usually lives underground. It searches for food while boring its way through the ground at 50 miles per hour.

name: Hippopotas

attacks.name: Double Headbutt
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It shuts its nostrils tight then travels through sand as if walking. They form colonies of around ten.

name: Meowth

attacks.name: Exhausted Tackle
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon. If tails, this Pokémon does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: Adores round objects. It wanders the streets on a nightly basis to look for dropped loose change.

name: Illumise

attacks.name: Sweet Scent
attacks.text: Remove 3 damage counters from 1 of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Vulcan Beat
attacks.text: Flip a coin for each Volbeat you have in play. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Its fragrance attracts a swarm of Volbeat, so they draw over 200 patterns in the night sky.

name: Trubbish

attacks.name: Tool Drop
attacks.text: Does 20 damage for each Pokémon Tool card attached to Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's).

flavorText: Wanting more garbage, they follow people who litter. They always belch poison gas.

attacks.name: Thunder Jolt
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, Dratini does 10 damage to itself.

name: Buzzwole-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Jet Punch
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Knuckle Impact
attacks.text: This Pokémon can't attack during your next turn.

attacks.name: Absorption-GX
attacks.text: This attack does 40 damage for each of your remaining Prize cards. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Porygon-Z

ancientTrait.name: θ Stop
ancientTrait.text: Prevent all effects of your opponent's Pokémon's Abilities done to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Digital Reboot
attacks.text: Devolve as many of your Benched Pokémon as many times as you like. Put each Evolution card removed this way into your hand.

attacks.name: Dazzle Blast
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Its programming was modified to enable it to travel through alien dimensions. Seems there might have been an error…

name: Claydol

attacks.name: Rapid Spin
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. Then, your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Rock Smash
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: An ancient clay figurine that came to life as a Pokémon from exposure to a mysterious ray of light.

name: Vikavolt V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Paralyzing Bolt
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, they can't play any Item cards from their hand.

attacks.name: Super Zap Cannon
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Double Stab
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Second Strike
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon already has at least 2 damage counters on it, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage.

name: Kabuto

attacks.name: Fast Evolution
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Evolution cards, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: It is thought to have inhabited beaches 300 million years ago. It is protected by a stiff shell.

name: Pincurchin

attacks.name: Spinning Fan
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It feeds on seaweed, using its teeth to scrape it off rocks. Electric current flows from the tips of its spines.

abilities.name: Poison Payback
abilities.text: If Wurmple is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent's attack (even if Wurmple is Knocked Out), the Attacking Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: String Pull
attacks.text: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with 1 of the Defending Pokémon. Your opponent chooses the Defending Pokémon to switch.

name: Unown [O]

abilities.name: ONE
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if you have Unown O, Unown N, and Unown E on your Bench and you have 1 card left in your hand, you may draw cards until you have 7 cards in your hand.

attacks.name: Hidden Power
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Shaped like ancient writing, it is a huge mystery whether language or UNOWN came first.

name: Eevee

attacks.name: Sand Attack
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

flavorText: A rare Pokémon that adapts to harsh environments by taking on different evolutionary forms.

name: Reshiram

abilities.name: Turboblaze
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, you may attach a Fire Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Dragon Pokémon.

attacks.name: Bright Wing
attacks.text: Discard a Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: When Reshiram's tail flares, the heat energy moves the atmosphere and changes the world's weather.

attacks.name: Taunt
attacks.text: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose 1 of them and switch it with the Defending Pokémon.

name: Moltres & Zapdos & Articuno-GX
rules: TAG TEAM rule: When your TAG TEAM is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Sky Legends-GX
attacks.text: Shuffle this Pokémon and all cards attached to it into your deck. If this Pokémon has at least 1 extra Fire, Water, and Lightning Energy attached to it (in addition to this attack's cost), this attack does 110 damage to 3 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Pikachu

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.

flavorText: It occasionally uses an electric shock to recharge a fellow Pikachu that is in a weakened state.

attacks.name: Thrust
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. If heads, discard 1 Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Rising Lunge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage.

name: Espurr
flavorText: It has enough psychic energy to blast everything within 300 feet of itself, but it has no control over its power.

abilities.name: Beating Wings
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Pidgeot is your Active Pokémon, you may shuffle 1 of your Benched Pokémon and all cards attached to it into your deck. This power can't be used if Pidgeot is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Sharp Beak
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 30 more damage.

name: Blissey

attacks.name: Double Slap
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Nurse's Egg
attacks.text: Heal 100 damage from each of your Benched Pokémon. Then, discard 2 Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: The eggs it lays are filled with happiness. Eating even one bite will bring a smile to anyone.

name: Voltorb
flavorText: Usually found in power plants. Easily mistaken for a Monster Ball, it has zapped many people.

name: Drifloon

attacks.name: Constrict
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Linear Attack
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: A Pokémon formed by the spirits of people and Pokémon. It loves damp, humid seasons.

attacks.name: Destructive Roar
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard 1 Energy card attached to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Gigacrush
attacks.text: Each player discards the top 3 cards from his or her deck.

attacks.name: Double Kick
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

name: Mareep

attacks.name: Thunder Jolt
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, Mareep does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: Its fleece grows continually. In the summer, the fleece is fully shed, but it grows back in a week.

name: Nidoqueen

attacks.name: Double Kick
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Poison Fang
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 2 damage counters instead of 1 on that Pokémon between turns.

flavorText: The body is covered by stiff, needle-like scales. If it becomes excited, the needles bristle outwards.

name: Gothita

attacks.name: Hypnotic Gaze
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Double Slap
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Their ribbonlike feelers increase their psychic power. They are always staring at something.

abilities.name: Dark Gaze
abilities.text: As long as Umbreon is your Active Pokémon, Benched Pokémon (yours and your opponent's) can't use Poké-Powers.

attacks.name: Sharp Claws
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 30 more damage.

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Grass Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Bellsprout

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon named Bellsprout and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward. (You can't use this attack if your Bench is full.)

flavorText: A carnivorous Pokémon that traps and eats bugs. It uses its root feet to soak up needed moisture.

name: Unown [D]

abilities.name: DRAW
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Unown D is on your Bench, you may flip a coin. If heads, each player may draw a card. (You draw your card first.)

flavorText: Shaped like ancient writing, it is a huge mystery whether language or UNOWN came first.

attacks.name: Run Around
attacks.text: Switch Doduo with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Random Peck
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each heads.

name: Tropius

attacks.name: Green Call
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. For each heads, search your deck for a Grass Pokémon, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. If you do, shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: The bunch of fruit around its neck ripens twice a year and is delicious. It's a highly favored tropical snack.

name: Healing Field
rules: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play.
Once during each player's turn, he or she may flip a coin. If heads, that player removes 2 damage counters from his or her Active Pokémon (1 if it only has 1).

name: Necrozma-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Light's End
abilities.text: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Colorless Pokémon.

attacks.name: Prismatic Burst
attacks.text: Discard all Psychic Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 60 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.

attacks.name: Black Ray-GX
attacks.text: This attack does 100 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon-GX and Pokémon-EX. This damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

attacks.name: Poison Breath
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

name: Shining Rayquaza

attacks.name: Dragon Pulse
attacks.text: Discard the top 2 cards of your deck.

attacks.name: Sky Judgment
attacks.text: Discard 3 Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It lives in the ozone layer far above the clouds and cannot be seen from the ground.

attacks.name: Strike and Run
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward. You may switch Dunsparce with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Sudden Flash
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, each Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Knock Away
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.

name: Banette-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Shady Move
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, you may move 1 damage counter from 1 Pokémon to another Pokémon.

attacks.name: Shadow Chant
attacks.text: This attack does 10 more damage for each Supporter card in your discard pile. You can't add more than 100 damage in this way.

attacks.name: Tomb Hunt-GX
attacks.text: Put 3 cards from your discard pile into your hand. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Raichu
flavorText: Its long tail serves as a ground to protect itself from its own high-voltage power.

name: M Lucario-EX
rules: Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Rising Fist
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

name: Mewtwo-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Full Burst
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage times the amount of Energy attached to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Super Absorption
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Psystrike-GX
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Machop

attacks.name: Dual Chop
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It loves working out. As it gazes at its muscles, which continue to swell day by day, it becomes more and more dedicated to its training.

name: Shieldon
rules: Put this card onto your Bench only with the effect of Armor Fossil Shieldon

attacks.name: Rock Head
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: It was generated from a fossil dug out of a layer of clay that was older than anyone knows. It has a sturdy face.

attacks.name: Flail
attacks.text: Does 10 damage for each damage counter on Goldeen.

name: Bronzong

abilities.name: Metal Links
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may attach a Metal Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: In ages past, this Pokémon was revered as a bringer of rain. It was found buried in the ground.

name: Sandile

attacks.name: Sand Tomb
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It moves along below the sand's surface, except for its nose and eyes. A dark membrane shields its eyes from the sun.

attacks.name: Rock Toss
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

name: Mewtwo-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Photon Wave
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 30 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

name: Magnezone

abilities.name: Double Type
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is in play, it is Lightning and Metal type.

attacks.name: Magnetic Bolt
attacks.text: Put a Trainer card from your discard pile into your hand.

flavorText: It uses radar to monitor its territory. Intruders are quickly disposed of with a hyper beam.

name: Luxio

abilities.name: Top Entry
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if you drew this Pokémon from your deck at the beginning of your turn and your Bench isn't full, before you put it into your hand, you may put it onto your Bench.

flavorText: Strong electricity courses through the tips of its sharp claws. A light scratch causes fainting in foes.

name: Cresselia-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Sparkling Particles
abilities.text: At any time between turns, heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Psychic Protection
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon has no Weakness.

name: Gigalith

attacks.name: Core Cannon
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon for each Fighting Energy attached to this Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Compressing the energy from its internal core lets it fire off an attack capable of blowing away a mountain.

name: Nidoran ♀

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon named Nidoran ♂ or Nidoran ♀ and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward. (You can't use this attack if your Bench is full.)

flavorText: Although small, its venomous barbs make this Pokémon dangerous. The female has smaller horns.

name: Stunfisk

attacks.name: Raging Thunder
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Electric Trap
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage for each of your Pokémon that has any damage counters on it.

flavorText: Its skin is very hard, so it is unhurt even if stepped on by sumo wrestlers. It smiles when transmitting electricity.

name: Marowak

attacks.name: Heavy Bone
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, Marowak can't use Heavy Bone during your next turn.

attacks.name: Bone Rush
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: From its birth, this savage Pokémon constantly holds bones. It is skilled in using them as weapons.

name: Wela Volcano Park
rules: Whenever a player flips a coin for the Special Condition Burned between turns, that Special Condition isn't removed even if the result is heads.
This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

name: Gurdurr

attacks.name: Steel Swing
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 60 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: They strengthen their bodies by carrying steel beams. They show off their big muscles to their friends.

name: Druddigon

attacks.name: Big Swing
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. If either of them is tails, this attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Shred
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: It runs through the narrow tunnels formed by Excadrill and Onix. It uses its sharp claws to catch prey.

name: Jynx

attacks.name: Dazzle Dance
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Mysterious Dance
attacks.text: For each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon, put 1 damage counter on your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: It sways its hips to a rhythm all its own. The precise movements of Jynx living in Alola are truly wonderful.

name: Gligar

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It flies straight at its target's face, then clamps down on the startled victim to inject poison.

name: Mareep

attacks.name: Static Electricity
attacks.text: Search your deck for a number of Lightning Energy cards up to the number of Mareep in play (both yours and your opponent's) and attach them to Mareep. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: If static electricity builds in its body, its fleece doubles in volume. Touching it will shock you.

name: Throh

attacks.name: Scarf Hold
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: When it tightens its belt, it becomes stronger. Wild Throh use vines to weave their own belts.

name: Girafarig

attacks.name: Tail Bite
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage; if tails, this attack does 10 damage.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.text: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: Its tail has a brain of its own, which can respond to stimuli such as scents. Many unsuspecting people have been bitten by it.

name: Toxapex

abilities.name: Toxic Spikes
abilities.text: Whenever your opponent's Active Pokémon retreats, their new Active Pokémon is Poisoned.

attacks.name: Venoshock
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 50 more damage.

flavorText: Toxapex crawls along the ocean floor on its 12 legs. It leaves a trail of Corsola bits scattered in its wake.

name: Gligar

attacks.name: Stun Poison
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed and Poisoned.

flavorText: It spends its days hanging from cliffs, swooping down on any prey it spots from its high vantage point.

name: Electrike

attacks.name: Random Spark
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Using electricity stored in its fur, it stimulates its muscles to heighten its reaction speed.

name: Makuhita

attacks.name: Sand Attack
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

flavorText: It toughens its body by slamming into thick trees. Many snapped trees can be found near its nest.

name: Shining Arceus

abilities.name: Fabled Defense
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, prevent all damage done to your Benched Pokémon by your opponent's attacks.

attacks.name: Ultimate Arrow
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: According to the legends of Sinnoh, this Pokémon emerged from an egg and shaped all there is in this world.

name: Dialga-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Chrono Wind
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is a Pokémon-EX, it can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Full Metal Impact
attacks.text: Discard 2 Metal Energy attached to this Pokémon.

name: Elgyem

abilities.name: Weak Barrier
abilities.text: If this Pokémon has any Psychic Energy attached to it, this Pokémon has no Weakness.

attacks.name: Quick Blow
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: It uses its strong psychic power to squeeze its opponent's brain, causing unendurable headaches.

attacks.name: Loaded Needle
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 30 more damage. If tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

name: Hoothoot

attacks.name: Hypnosis
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It always stands on one foot. It changes feet so fast that the movement can rarely be seen.

name: Krokorok

attacks.name: Torment
attacks.text: Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon's attacks. That Pokémon can't use that attack during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: The special membrane covering its eyes can sense the heat of objects, so it can see its surroundings even in darkness.

name: Natu

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused

flavorText: It usually forages for food on the ground but may, on rare occasions, hop onto branches to peck at shoots.

attacks.name: Group Therapy
attacks.text: You and your opponent remove 1 damage counter from each of your Pokémon with damage counters on them.

attacks.name: Pulled Punch
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon has no damage counters on it, this attack does 40 damage. If it has any damage counters on it, this attack does 10 damage.

name: Cacnea

attacks.name: Sneaky Attack
attacks.text: If Cacnea has any Darkness Energy attached to it, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.

attacks.name: Shoot Needle
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. For each heads, choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon and this attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) (You can choose the same Pokémon more than once, but you can't do more than 10 damage to that Pokémon in this way.)

flavorText: By storing water in its body, this desert dweller can survive 30 days without water.

name: Umbreon

attacks.name: Shadow Drain
attacks.text: Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Slashing Strike
attacks.text: This Pokémon can't use Slashing Strike during your next turn.

flavorText: When exposed to the moon's aura, the rings on its body glow faintly and it gains a mysterious power.

abilities.name: Self-healing
abilities.text: Whenever you attach a Water Energy card from your hand to Vaporeon, remove all Special Conditions affecting Vaporeon.

attacks.name: Hypnosplash
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Aqua Trick
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon has any Energy cards attached to it, flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 of those Energy cards and move it to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. If your opponent has no Benched Pokémon, ignore this effect.

name: Azurill

abilities.name: Baby Evolution
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put Marill from your hand onto Azurill (this counts a evolving Azurill) and remove all damage counters from Azurill.

attacks.name: Delivery
attacks.text: Put any 1 card from your discard pile into your hand.

flavorText: A Pokémon that lives by water. It moves quickly on land by bouncing on its big tail.

name: Garbodor

attacks.name: Biosmog
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to that Pokémon.

flavorText: Consuming garbage makes new kinds of poison gases and liquids inside their bodies.

name: Raticate

attacks.name: Super Fang
attacks.text: Does damage to the Defending Pokémon equal to half the Defending Pokémon's remaining HP (rounded up to the nearest 10).

flavorText: It uses its whiskers to maintain its balance. It seems to slow down if they are cut off.

name: Sandslash

attacks.name: Spike Armor
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, if Sandslash is damaged by an opponent's attack (even if Sandslash is Knocked Out), put 4 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.

attacks.name: Poison Spike
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, that Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It curls up, then rolls into foes with its back. Its sharp spines inflict severe damage.

name: Joltik

attacks.name: Jolt
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Since it can't generate its own electricity, it sticks onto large-bodied Pokémon and absorbs static electricity.

name: Dedenne-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Dedechange
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may discard your hand and draw 6 cards. You can't use more than 1 Dedechange Ability each turn.

attacks.name: Tingly Return-GX
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed. Put this Pokémon and all cards attached to it into your hand. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Kricketot
flavorText: When its antennae hit each other, it sounds like the music of a xylophone.

name: Genesect-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Drive Change
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put a Pokémon Tool card attached to this Pokémon into your hand.

attacks.name: Rapid Blaster
attacks.text: Discard as many Metal Energy attached to this Pokémon as you like. This attack does 20 more damage for each Energy card discarded in this way.

name: Baltoy

attacks.name: Psychic Balance
attacks.text: If you have less cards in your hand than your opponent, draw cards until you have the same number of cards as your opponent. (If you have more or the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, this attack does nothing.)

attacks.name: Spin Turn
attacks.text: Switch Baltoy with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It moves by spinning on its foot. It is a rare Pokémon that was discovered in ancient ruins.

name: Absol

attacks.name: Mind Jack
attacks.text: Does 20 more damage for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Fearsome Shadow
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals his or her hand.

flavorText: It appears from deep in the mountains to warn people about upcoming disasters it has sensed with its horn.

name: Ivysaur

abilities.name: Evolutionary Pollen
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you play Ivysaur from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may use this power. Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: When the bud on its back starts swelling, a sweet aroma wafts to indicate the flower's coming bloom.

name: M Lucario-EX
rules: Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Rising Fist
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Swift
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Rage
attacks.text: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Cyndaquil.

attacks.name: Tail Whip
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

name: Scyther

attacks.name: Slashing Strike
attacks.text: During your next turn, Scyther can't use Slashing Strike.

flavorText: It slashes through grass with its sharp scythes, moving too fast for the human eye to track.

name: Giovanni's Exile
rules: Discard up to 2 of your Benched Pokémon that have no damage counters on them and all cards attached to them.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Unown [X]

abilities.name: X-RAY
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if you have Unown X on your Bench, you may look at the top card of your opponent's deck and put it back on top of his or her deck.

attacks.name: Hidden Power
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Shaped like ancient writing, it is a huge mystery whether language or UNOWN came first.

name: Meowth

attacks.name: Coin Hurl
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon and flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for this attack. (Any other effects that would happen after applying Weakness and Resistance still happen.)

flavorText: A popular Pokémon that earns money for its owner with its "Pay Day" ability.

name: Espeon-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Psybeam
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage times the number of Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Divide-GX
attacks.text: Put 10 damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Vulpix

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: At the time of birth, it has just one tail. Its tail splits from the tip as it grows older.

name: Metang

attacks.name: Agility
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of an attack, including damage, done to Metang during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It is formed by two Beldum joining together. Its steel body won't be scratched if it collides with a jet.

name: Kangaskhan

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Comet Punch
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It raises its offspring in its belly pouch. It lets the baby play only when it feels safe.

attacks.name: Call for Friends
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Bastiodon

attacks.name: Counter Head
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, if this Pokémon is damaged by an attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon equal to the damage done to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Fortress of Rage
attacks.text: This attack does 10 more damage for each of your Benched Pokémon that has any damage counters on it.

flavorText: Any frontal attack is repulsed. It is a docile Pokémon that feeds on grass and berries.

name: Pidgey

attacks.name: Peck Off
attacks.text: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tool cards attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: A common sight in forests and woods. It flaps its wings at ground level to kick up blinding sand.

name: Houndour
flavorText: Around dawn, its ominous howl echoes through the area to announce that this is its territory.

name: Fearow

ancientTrait.name: Δ Evolution
ancientTrait.text: You may play this card from your hand to evolve a Pokémon during your first turn or the turn you play that Pokémon.

attacks.name: Repeating Drill
attacks.text: Flip 5 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Nosedive
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: It has the stamina to fly all day on its broad wings. It fights by using its sharp beak.

name: Clefairy

attacks.name: Mini-Metronome
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks and use it as this attack.

flavorText: It is said that happiness will come to those who see a gathering of Clefairy dancing under a full moon.

name: Duskull

attacks.name: Silhouette
attacks.text: Put 1 damage counter on the Defending Pokémon. If the Defending Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, put 2 damage counters on that Pokémon instead.

flavorText: It doggedly pursues its prey wherever it goes. However, the chase is abandoned at sunrise.

name: Gothita

attacks.name: Trip Over
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: It stares intently at everything. It can become so obsessed with watching that it doesn't notice attacks.

abilities.name: Baby Evolution
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put Wobbuffet from your hand onto Wynaut (this counts as evolving Wynaut) and remove all damage counters from Wynaut.

attacks.name: Confusion Wave
attacks.text: Both Wynaut and the Defending Pokémon are now Confused.

name: Pidgey

attacks.name: Messenger
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Pokémon, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle Pidgey and all cards attached to it back into your deck.

flavorText: Common in grassy areas and forests, it is very docile and will chase off enemies by flapping up sand.

name: Persian

attacks.name: Fake Out
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Ambush
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: Its lithe muscles allow it to walk without making a sound. It attacks in an instant.

abilities.name: Plunge
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Poliwrath is on your Bench, you may flip a coin. If heads, take all Energy cards attached to your Active Pokémon, if any, and attach them to Poliwrath. Then switch Poliwrath with your Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Water Punch
attacks.text: Flip a number of coins equal to the amount of Water Energy attached to Poliwrath. This attack does 40 damage plus 10 more damage for each heads.

name: Nidoqueen

ancientTrait.name: Ω Barrage
ancientTrait.text: This Pokémon may attack twice a turn. (If the first attack Knocks Out your opponent's Active Pokémon, you may attack again after your opponent chooses a new Active Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Poison Jab
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Dynamite Punch
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 20 damage to itself. Don't apply Weakness to this damage.

flavorText: The body is covered by stiff, needle-like scales. If it becomes excited, the needles bristle outwards.

name: Conkeldurr

attacks.name: Wake-Up Slap
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack does 60 more damage. Then, remove all Special Conditions from that Pokémon.

attacks.name: Dynamic Punch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage and your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: It is thought that Conkeldurr taught humans how to make concrete more than 2,000 years ago.

name: Galarian Obstagoon

abilities.name: Untamed Shout
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may put 3 damage counters on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Obstruct
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Basic Pokémon.

flavorText: It evolved after experiencing numerous fights. While crossing its arms, it lets out a shout that would make any opponent flinch.

name: Cosmog

abilities.name: Cosmic Guard
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).

flavorText: In ages past, it was called the child of the stars. It's said to be a Pokémon from another world, but no specific details are known.

name: Mewtwo-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Shatter Shot
