attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage times the amount of Psychic Energy attached to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Damage Change
attacks.text: Switch all damage counters on this Pokémon with those on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

name: Munna

abilities.name: Long-Distance Hypnosis
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep. If tails, your Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: This Pokémon appears before people and Pokémon who are having nightmares and eats those dreams.

name: Zinnia
rules: You can play this card only if 1 of your Pokémon was Knocked Out during your opponent's last turn.
Attach up to 2 basic Energy cards from your hand to 1 of your Dragon Pokémon.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

attacks.name: Teleportation Burst
attacks.text: Switch Ralts with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

name: Hitmonchan
flavorText: The spirit of a pro boxer has infused this Pokémon. It throws punches that are faster than a bullet train.

name: White Kyurem

attacks.name: Field Crush
attacks.text: If your opponent has a Stadium card in play, discard it.

attacks.name: Freezing Flames
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any Fire Energy attached to it, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: It generates a powerful, freezing energy inside itself, but its body became frozen when the energy leaked out.

attacks.name: Slow Trip Gas
attacks.text: At the end of your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Knock Over
attacks.text: You may discard any Stadium card in play.

name: Roggenrola

attacks.name: Stone Edge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: They were discovered a hundred years ago in an earthquake fissure. Inside each one is an energy core.

name: Timburr

attacks.name: Pummel
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.

flavorText: It fights by swinging a piece of lumber around. It is close to evolving when it can handle the lumber without difficulty.

attacks.name: Hypnosis
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Lunge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

name: Marill

attacks.name: Water Splash
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.

flavorText: The end of its tail serves as a buoy that keeps it from drowning, even in a vicious current.

name: Gardevoir V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Swelling Pulse
attacks.text: If this Pokémon was healed during this turn, this attack does 80 more damage.

name: Meowstic

attacks.name: Energy Present
attacks.text: Attach up to 2 Energy cards from your hand to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: The eyeball patterns on the interior of its ears emit psychic energy. It keeps the patterns tightly covered because that power is too immense.

name: Deoxys

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage times the amount of Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Power Blast
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: DNA from a space virus mutated and became a Pokémon. It appears where auroras are seen.

abilities.name: Dark Moon
abilities.text: As long as Umbreon is your Active Pokémon and has a Darkness Energy attached to it, once during your turn (before your attack), you may look at your opponent's hand. Choose from it a number of cards up to the number of Darkness Energy attached to Umbreon and shuffle them into your opponent's deck. Your opponent then draws the same number of cards from his or her deck. This power can't be used if Umbreon is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Shadow Bind
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

name: Necrozma-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Light's End
abilities.text: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Colorless Pokémon.

attacks.name: Prismatic Burst
attacks.text: Discard all Psychic Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 60 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.

attacks.name: Black Ray-GX
attacks.text: This attack does 100 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon-GX and Pokémon-EX. This damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Riolu

attacks.name: Detect
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It's tough enough to run right through the night, and it's also a hard worker, but it's still just a youngster.

name: Turtwig

attacks.name: Leech Seed
attacks.text: If this attack does any damage to the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance), remove 1 damage counter from Turtwig.

flavorText: Photosynthesis occurs across its body under the sun. The shell on its back is actually hardened soil.

name: Nuzleaf

attacks.name: Surprise Punch
attacks.text: Move an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: The sound of its grass flute makes its listeners uneasy. It lives deep in forests.

name: Luxray

attacks.name: Raid
attacks.text: If this Pokémon evolved from Luxio during this turn, this attack does 100 more damage.

flavorText: Luxray's ability to see through objects comes in handy when it's scouting for danger.

abilities.name: Mirror Coat
abilities.text: If Natu is Burned or Poisoned by an opponent's attack (even if Natu is Knocked Out), the Attacking Pokémon is now affected by the same Special Conditions (1 if there is only 1).

attacks.name: Razor Wind
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Double Scratch
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

name: Lugia-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Aero Ball
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage times the amount of Energy attached to both Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Deep Hurricane
attacks.text: If there is any Stadium card in play, this attack does 70 more damage. Then, discard that Stadium card.

name: Chinchou
flavorText: On the dark ocean floor, its only means of communication is its constantly flashing lights.

name: Team Aqua Schemer
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.
Discard any 1 Pokémon from your hand. Then draw 3 cards. If you discarded a Pokémon with Team Aqua in its name, draw 4 cards instead.

name: Cosmoem

attacks.name: Nap
attacks.text: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: There's something accumulating around the black core within its hard shell. People think this Pokémon may come from another world.

name: Hoppip

attacks.name: Sprout
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon named Hoppip and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward. (You can't use this attack if your Bench is full.)

flavorText: To keep from being blown away by the wind, they gather in clusters. They do enjoy gentle breezes, though.

name: Honedge

attacks.name: Stab
attacks.text: Put 1 damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: If anyone dares to grab its hilt, it wraps a blue cloth around that person's arm and drains that person's life energy completely.

name: Garbodor

attacks.name: Ensnarl
attacks.text: Does 20 damage times the number of Colorless in the Defending Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

attacks.name: Double Ducts
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 80 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Consuming garbage makes new kinds of poison gases and liquids inside their bodies.

name: Carnivine

attacks.name: Poison Breath
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Sweet Saliva
attacks.text: Remove 1 damage counter from each of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It binds itself to trees in marshes. It attracts prey with its sweet-smelling drool and gulps them down.

name: Gurdurr

attacks.name: Pummel
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: This Pokémon is so muscular and strongly built that even a group of wrestlers could not make it budge an inch.

name: Malamar

ancientTrait.name: θ Stop
ancientTrait.text: Prevent all effects of your opponent's Pokémon's Abilities done to this Pokémon.

abilities.name: Contrary
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, whenever your opponent flips a coin during his or her turn, treat it as tails.

attacks.name: Conform
attacks.text: If you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: It lures its prey close with hypnotic motions, then wraps its tentacles around it before finishing it off with digestive fluids.

name: Clefairy

attacks.name: Sing
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Metronome
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks and use it as this attack.

flavorText: Its adorable appearance makes it popular as a pet. However, it is rare and difficult to find.

name: Omanyte

abilities.name: Revive Fossil
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a card that evolves from Mysterious Fossil and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward. Treat that card as a Basic Pokémon. This power can't be used if Omanyte is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed (or if your Bench is full).

attacks.name: Bind
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Revived from an ancient fossil, this Pokémon uses air stored in its shell to sink and rise in water.

attacks.name: Surprise
attacks.text: Choose 1 card from your opponent's hand without looking. Look at the card you chose, then have your opponent shuffle that card into his or her deck.

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

name: Popplio

attacks.name: Disarming Voice
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: This Pokémon snorts body fluids from its nose, blowing balloons to smash into its foes. It's famous for being a hard worker.

name: Galarian Cursola V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Gnawing Aura
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, whenever your opponent attaches an Energy card from their hand to 1 of their Pokémon, put 3 damage counters on that Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hollow Missile
attacks.text: Put 3 damage counters on your opponent's Benched Pokémon in any way you like.

name: Heatran

attacks.name: Steel Drop
attacks.text: If there is any Stadium card in play, this attack does 40 more damage.

attacks.name: Steam Blast
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It dwells in volcanic caves. It digs in with its cross-shaped feet to crawl on ceilings and walls.

name: Empoleon

attacks.name: Fury Attack
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Cold Crush
attacks.text: You may discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you do, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: If anyone were to hurt its pride, it would slash them with wings that can cleave through an ice floe.

name: Sandile

attacks.name: Gentle Slap
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: They live hidden under hot desert sands in order to keep their body temperature from dropping.

name: Krookodile

attacks.name: Torment
attacks.text: Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon's attacks. That Pokémon can't use that attack during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Krookoroll
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavorText: It can expand the focus of its eyes, enabling it to see objects in the far distance as if it were using binoculars.

name: Weedle

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Often found in forests, eating leaves. It has a sharp, venomous stinger on its head.

name: Tapu Koko

attacks.name: Allure
attacks.text: Draw 2 cards.

flavorText: The lightning-wielding guardian deity of Melemele, Tapu Koko is brimming with curiosity and appears before people from time to time.

name: Buizel
flavorText: It has a flotation sac that is like an inflatable collar. It floats on water with its head out.

name: Sandslash

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Curls up into a spiny ball when threatened. It can roll while curled up to attack or escape.

name: Slowpoke

attacks.name: Rambunctious Party
attacks.text: Look at the top 5 cards of your deck. Choose as many Basic Pokémon as you like and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

flavorText: A sweet sap leaks from its tail's tip. Although not nutritious, the tail is pleasant to chew on.

name: Pokémon Breeder Fields
rules: Flip a coin for 1 or 2 of your non-Baby Pokémon that can evolve. For each heads, search your deck for a later-Stage card that matches that Pokémon. Then put that card into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Duskull

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It doggedly pursues its prey wherever it goes. However, the chase is abandoned at sunrise.

name: Oddish

attacks.name: Sleep Powder
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Poisonpowder
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It wanders around at night sowing its seeds to create more Oddishes.

name: Lickitung

attacks.name: Tongue Whip
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Its long tongue, slathered with a gooey saliva, sticks to anything, so it is very useful.

name: Houndoom

attacks.name: Fire Fang
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Puncturing Fangs
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: Long ago, people imagined its eerie howls to be the call of the grim reaper.

name: Jessie & James
rules: Each player discards 2 cards from their hand. Your opponent discards first.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Musharna

attacks.name: Telekinesis
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

attacks.name: Dream Waltz
attacks.text: This attack can be used even if this Pokémon is Asleep. If this Pokémon is Asleep, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: The dream mist coming from its forehead changes into many different colors depending on the dream that was eaten.

name: Vulpix

attacks.name: Fireworks
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, discard a Fire Energy attached to Vulpix.

flavorText: As it develops, its single white tail gains color and splits into six. It is quite warm and cuddly.

name: Doduo

attacks.name: Double Stab
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Doduo Delivery
attacks.text: Draw 2 cards.

abilities.name: Solid Shell
abilities.text: Any damage done to Squirtle by attacks is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Bubblebeam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Duraludon

attacks.name: Metal Sharpener
attacks.text: Attach a Metal Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Pokémon.

flavorText: Its body resembles polished metal, and it's both lightweight and strong. The only drawback is that it rusts easily.

attacks.name: Leech Life
attacks.text: After your attack, remove from Nincada the number of damage counters equal to the damage you did to the Defending Pokémon. If Nincada has fewer damage counters than that, remove all of them.

name: Oddish

attacks.name: Stun Spore
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Sprout
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon named Oddish and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward. (You can't use this attack if your Bench is full.)

flavorText: During the day, it keeps its face buried in the ground. At night, it wanders around sowing its seeds.

attacks.name: Strange Powder
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused; if tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Fling
attacks.text: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

name: Sealeo

attacks.name: Ice Rider
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It habitually spins things on its nose. By doing so, it learns textures and odors.

abilities.name: Good Neighbor
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Wigglytuff is on your bench, you may flip a coin. If heads, each player removes up to 2 damage counters from his or her Active Pokémon. This power can't be used if you have already used another Wigglytuff's Good Neighbor Poké-Power this turn.

attacks.name: Do the Wave
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage times the number of Pokémon you have in play.

name: Machamp-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Cross-Cut
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is an Evolution Pokémon, this attack does 60 more damage.

attacks.name: Bedrock Breaker
attacks.text: Discard any Stadium card in play.

attacks.name: Muscle Punch-GX
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Marshadow-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Shadow Hunt
abilities.text: This Pokémon can use the attacks of Basic Pokémon in your discard pile. (You still need the necessary Energy to use each attack.)

attacks.name: Peerless Hundred Blows-GX
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage times the number of basic Energy attached to this Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Pineco

attacks.name: Focus Energy
attacks.text: During your next turn, Pineco's Surprise Attack attack's base damage is 80.

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It likes to make its shell thicker by adding layers of tree bark. The additional weight doesn't bother it.

name: Nosepass

attacks.name: Harden
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, if Nosepass would be damaged by an attack, prevent that attack's damage done to Nosepass if that damage is 30 or less.

attacks.name: Knock Away
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage.

flavorText: When endangered, it may protect itself by raising its magnetism and drawing iron objects to its body.

name: Lucario

abilities.name: Precognitive Aura
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if you have Garchomp in play, you may search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Missile Jab
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: They can detect the species of a living being—and its emotions—from over half a mile away. They control auras and hunt their prey in packs.

name: Boldore

attacks.name: Rock Cannon
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Earthquake
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Because its energy was too great to be contained, the energy leaked and formed orange crystals.

name: Cursed Stone
rules: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.
At any time between turns, each player puts 1 damage counter on his or her Pokémon that has a Poké-Power.

name: Damage Mover
rules: Move 3 damage counters from 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Unown [Z]

abilities.name: ZERO
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Unown Z is on your Bench and you have no cards in your deck, you may discard all cards attached to Unown Z and put Unown Z on top of your deck.

attacks.name: Hidden Power
attacks.text: Remove as many damage counters as you like from each Unown you have in play. Put that many damage counters on the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: Shaped like ancient writing, it is a huge mystery whether language or UNOWN came first.

abilities.name: Fragrance Trap
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon and switch the Defending Pokémon with it. This Power can't be used if Victreebel is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Corrosive Acid
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

abilities.name: Feathery
abilities.text: As long as Swablu is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to Swablu by opponent's attacks.

attacks.name: Razor Wind
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

name: Sableye

attacks.name: Filch
attacks.text: Draw a card.

attacks.name: Rip Claw
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It hides in the darkness of caves. Its diet of gems has transformed its eyes into gemstones.

name: Growlithe
flavorText: It fears nothing, not even opponents larger than itself.

name: Ace Trainer
rules: You can play this card only if you have more Prize cards left than your opponent.
Each player shuffles his or her hand into his or her deck. Then, draw 6 cards. Your opponent draws 3 cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Galvantula

attacks.name: Live Wire
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. Also apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: They employ an electrically charged web to trap their prey. While it is immobilized by shock, they leisurely consume it.

name: Metang

attacks.name: Psyshock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Bullet Punch
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: It is formed by two Beldum joining together. Its steel body won't be scratched if it collides with a jet.

name: Porygon
flavorText: A man-made Pokémon created using advanced scientific means. It can move freely in cyberspace.

attacks.name: Eerie Light
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

name: Chinchou

attacks.name: Luring Glow
attacks.text: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Its antennae, which evolved from a fin, have both positive and negative charges flowing through them.

name: Banette-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Shady Move
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, you may move 1 damage counter from 1 Pokémon to another Pokémon.

attacks.name: Shadow Chant
attacks.text: This attack does 10 more damage for each Supporter card in your discard pile. You can't add more than 100 damage in this way.

attacks.name: Tomb Hunt-GX
attacks.text: Put 3 cards from your discard pile into your hand. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Lopunny

attacks.name: Ice Beam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: The ears appear to be delicate. If they are touched roughly, it kicks with its graceful legs.

name: Umbreon

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Shadow Shutdown
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, discard all Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: When exposed to the moon's aura, the rings on its body glow faintly and it gains a mysterious power.

name: M Mewtwo-EX
rules: Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Vanishing Strike
attacks.text: If there is any Stadium card in play, this attack does 50 more damage, and this attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance or any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

name: Bidoof

attacks.name: Amnesia
attacks.text: Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon's attacks. That Pokémon can't use that attack during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Scavenge
attacks.text: Search your discard pile for a Trainer card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand.

flavorText: With nerves of steel, nothing can perturb it. It is more agile and active than it appears.

name: Charjabug

abilities.name: Battery
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may attach this card from your hand to 1 of your Vikavolt or Vikavolt-GX as a Special Energy card. This card provides 2 Lightning Energy only while it's attached to a Pokémon.

flavorText: It buries itself in fallen leaves and barely moves, munching away on humus. If you accidentally step on one, you'll get a shock!

name: Cosmog

attacks.name: Dust Gathering
attacks.text: Draw a card.

flavorText: Its body is gaseous and frail. It slowly grows as it collects dust from the atmosphere.

name: Helioptile

attacks.name: Thunder Jolt
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: When spread, the frills on its head act like solar panels, generating the power behind this Pokémon's electric moves.

name: Garchomp

attacks.name: Quick Dive
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Royal Blades
attacks.text: If you played Cynthia from your hand during this turn, this attack does 100 more damage.

flavorText: The protuberances on its head serve as sensors. It can even detect distant prey.

attacks.name: Fidget
attacks.text: Shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Karate Chop
attacks.text: Does 40 damage minus 10 damage for each damage counter on Brock's Mankey.

attacks.name: Sonicboom
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

name: Growlithe

attacks.name: Stoke
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Fire Energy card and attach it to Growlithe. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.text: Growlithe does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: A Pokémon with a loyal nature. It will remain motionless until it is given an order by its Trainer.

name: Tentacool

attacks.name: Lost in the Waves
attacks.text: Return this Pokémon and all cards attached to it to your hand.

flavorText: Drifts in shallow seas. Anglers who hook them by accident are often punished by their stingers.

attacks.name: Nap
attacks.text: Remove 2 damage counters from Seedot.

attacks.name: Double Spin
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 times the number of heads.

name: Sawk

attacks.name: Five Fierce Chops
attacks.text: Flip 5 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads. This Pokémon can't attack during your next turn.

flavorText: Tying their belts gets them pumped and makes their punches more destructive. Disturbing their training angers them.

name: Machop
flavorText: It loves working out. As it gazes at its muscles, which continue to swell day by day, it becomes more and more dedicated to its training.

name: Clefable

abilities.name: Lunar Blessing
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if your Active Pokémon has any Psychic Energy attached, you may heal 20 damage from it, and it recovers from a Special Condition.

flavorText: A timid fairy Pokémon that is rarely seen, it will run and hide the moment it senses people.

name: Shiftry

abilities.name: Giant Fan
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. Your opponent shuffles that Pokémon and all cards attached to it into his or her deck.

attacks.name: Whirlwind
attacks.text: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

name: Pidgeotto

attacks.name: Sand-Attack

attacks.name: Ambush
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: The claws on its feet are well developed. It can carry prey such as an Exeggcute to its nest over 60 miles away.

name: Glameow

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Rip Claw
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: With its sharp glare, it puts foes in a mild hypnotic state. It is a very fickle Pokémon.

attacks.name: Thunder Wave
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Zekrom

attacks.name: Energy Stream
attacks.text: Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Concealing itself in lightning clouds, it flies throughout the Unova region. It creates electricity in its tail.

name: Energy Retrieval
rules: Put 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

attacks.name: Plasma
attacks.text: If there are any Lightning Energy cards in your discard pile, flip a coin. If heads, attach 1 of them to Raichu.

attacks.name: Shock Bolt
attacks.text: Discard all Lightning Energy cards attached to Raichu or this attack does nothing.

name: Pidgeot

attacks.name: Clutch
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Strong Gust
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Strong Gust attack does 60 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: When hunting, it skims the surface of water at high speed to pick off unwary prey such as Magikarp.

name: Hippopotas

attacks.name: Grainy Sand
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.

attacks.name: Double-edge
attacks.text: Hippopotas does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: It enshrouds itself with sand to protect itself from germs. It does not enjoy getting wet.

name: Vikavolt

abilities.name: Stealthy Body
abilities.text: If there is any Stadium card in play, this Pokémon has no Weakness.

attacks.name: Electricannon
attacks.text: You may discard all Lightning Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 100 more damage.

flavorText: It has an organ that generates electricity in its abdomen. It concentrates energy in its strong jaws and fires off powerful jolts of electricity.

attacks.name: Poison Horn
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

name: Rhyhorn
flavorText: Strong, but not too bright, this Pokémon can shatter even a skyscraper with its charging Tackles.

name: Klink

attacks.name: Disorderly Flip
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: The two minigears that mesh together are predetermined. Each will rebound from other minigears without meshing.

attacks.name: Fake Out
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

abilities.text: You may attach any Technical Machine to Xatu.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, choose a card from your discard pile and put it on top of your deck.

name: Clefairy Doll
rules: Play Clefairy Doll as if it were a Basic Pokémon. While in play, Clefairy Doll counts a a Pokémon (instead of a Trainer card). Clefairy Doll has no attacks, can't retreat, and can't be Asleep, Confused, Paralyzed, or Poisoned. If Clefairy Doll is Knocked Out, it doesn't count as a Knocked Out Pokémon. At any time during your turn before your attack, you may discard Clefairy Doll.

name: Pineco

attacks.name: Burst
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, Pineco does 40 damage to itself and 10 damage to each Pokémon on each player's Bench. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing (not even damage).

flavorText: It likes to make its shell thicker by adding layers of tree bark. The additional weight doesn't bother it.

attacks.name: Poison Spore
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned and this attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Purrloin

name: Girafarig

attacks.name: Psypower
attacks.text: Put 2 damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like.

name: Haxorus

attacks.name: Armor Press
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Dual Chop
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 90 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Their sturdy tusks will stay sharp even if used to cut steel beams. These Pokémon are covered in hard armor.

name: Venomoth

abilities.name: Dangerous Scales
abilities.text: If Venomoth is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent's attack (even if Venomoth is Knocked Out), the Attacking Pokémon is now Asleep and Poisoned.

attacks.name: Disturbance Dive
attacks.text: Prevent all effects of an attack, excluding damage, done to Venomoth during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It flutters its wings to scatter dustlike scales. The scales leach toxins if they contact skin.

name: Krokorok
flavorText: Protected by thin membranes, their eyes can see even in the dead of night. They live in groups of a few individuals.

name: Chansey

attacks.name: Healing Trial
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, remove 3 damage counters from Chansey. If tails, remove 3 damage counters from the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Pulled Punch
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack's base damage is 10 instead of 40.

flavorText: A kindly Pokémon that lays highly nutritious eggs and shares them with injured Pokémon or people.

name: Dodrio

abilities.name: Retreat Aid
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, your Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost is ColorlessColorless less.

attacks.name: Fury Attack
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: An odd species that is rarely found. The three heads respectively represent joy, sadness, and anger.

name: Salamence

ancientTrait.name: Δ Evolution
ancientTrait.text: You may play this card from your hand to evolve a Pokémon during your first turn or the turn you play that Pokémon.

attacks.name: Call for Goons
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Dragon Pulse
attacks.text: Discard the top card of your deck.

flavorText: It's uncontrollable if enraged. It flies around spouting flames and scorching fields and mountains.

name: Reshiram-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Flame Charge
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Fire Energy cards and attach them to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Scorching Column
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.text: You may attach up to 5 Fire Energy cards from your hand to your Pokémon in any way you like. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Cosmoem

attacks.name: Teleport
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Motionless as if dead, its body is faintly warm to the touch. In the distant past, it was called the cocoon of the stars.

name: Rocket's Hideout
rules: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play.
Each Pokémon in play with Dark in its name (even your opponent's) gets +20 HP.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Machop

attacks.name: Steady Punch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.

flavorText: Always brimming with power, it passes time by lifting boulders. Doing so makes it even stronger.

attacks.name: Bad Dream
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep. If tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

name: Mightyena

attacks.name: Bite On
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon isn't an Evolved Pokémon, that Pokémon can't attack, retreat, or use any Poké-Powers during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Harass
attacks.text: Does 10 damage times the number of Pokémon you have in play.

flavorText: It chases down prey in a pack. It will never disobey the commands of a skilled Trainer.

name: Scraggy

attacks.name: Shed Skin
attacks.text: Heal 40 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Lunge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It immediately headbutts anyones that makes eye contact with it. Its skull is massively thick.

attacks.name: Poison Scent
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned and Confused. If tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned and Asleep.

attacks.name: Addictive Pollen
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent can't play Supporter cards during his or her next turn.

name: Bulbasaur

attacks.name: Leech Seed
attacks.text: Unless all damage from this attack is prevented, you may remove 1 damage counter from Bulbasaur.

flavorText: A strange seed was planted on its back at birth. Thus, a plant sprouted and now grows with this Pokémon.

name: Horsea

attacks.text: If an attack damaged Horsea during your opponent's last turn, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage. If not, this attack does 20 damage.

flavorText: If attacked by a larger enemy, it quickly swims to safety by adeptly controlling its dorsal fin.

name: Tynamo

attacks.name: Wild River
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: While one alone doesn't have much power, a chain of many Tynamo can be as powerful as lightning.

name: Loudred

attacks.name: Stomp Off
attacks.text: Discard the top card from your opponent's deck.

name: Popplio

attacks.name: Blot
attacks.text: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon can control water bubbles. It practice diligently so it can learn to make big bubbles.

name: Pikachu V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Circle Circuit
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage for each of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.text: Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon's attacks. That Pokémon can't use that attack during your opponent's next turn.

name: Moltres & Zapdos & Articuno-GX

attacks.name: Sky Legends-GX
attacks.text: Shuffle this Pokémon and all cards attached to it into your deck. If this Pokémon has at least 1 extra Fire, Water, and Lightning Energy attached to it (in addition to this attack's cost), this attack does 110 damage to 3 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Finneon

attacks.name: Aqua Liner
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Mouth Pump
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.

flavorText: After long exposure to sunlight, the patterns on its tail fins shine vividly when darkness arrives.

attacks.name: Poison Tentacles
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

name: Deino

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: They cannot see, so they tackle and bite to learn about their surroundings. Their bodies are covered in wounds.

attacks.text: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with 1 of the Defending Pokémon. Your opponent chooses the Defending Pokémon to switch. The new Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Quick Blow
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage.

name: Gothorita

attacks.name: Double Slap
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Psybeam
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: They use hypnosis to control people and Pokémon. Tales of Gothorita leading people astray are told in every corner.

name: Sableye

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Junk Hunt
attacks.text: Put 2 Item cards from your discard pile into your hand.

flavorText: It hides in the darkness of caves. Its diet of gems has transformed its eyes into gemstones.

name: Slugma

abilities.name: Active Volcano
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may discard the top card of your deck. If that card is a Fire Energy card, attach it to Slugma. This power can't be used if Slugma is affected by a Special Condition.

flavorText: A common sight in volcanic areas, it slowly slithers around in a constant search for warm places.

name: Sandile
flavorText: It moves along below the sand's surface, except for its nose and eyes. A dark membrane shields its eyes from the sun.

name: Cacnea

attacks.name: Sand Attack
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

name: Ampharos Spirit Link
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.
Your turn does not end if the Pokémon this card is attached to becomes M Ampharos-EX.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Porygon2

attacks.name: Destructive Beam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: It was upgraded to enable the exploration of other planets. However, it failed to measure up.

name: Bonsly

abilities.name: Baby Evolution
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put Sudowoodo from your hand onto Bonsly (this counts as evolving Bonsly) and remove all damage counters from Bonsly.

attacks.name: Fake Tears
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent can't play any Trainer cards from his or her hand during your opponent's next turn, and any damage done to Bonsly by attacks is reduced by 30 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: It looks as if it is always crying. It is actually adjusting its body's fluid levels by eliminating excess.

attacks.name: Energy Ball
attacks.text: Does 30 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy attached to Poliwhirl but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. You can't add more than 20 damage in this way.

name: Jigglypuff
flavorText: If it inflates to sing a lullaby, it can perform longer and cause sure drowsiness in its audience.

name: Doublade

attacks.name: Stab Deeply
attacks.text: Put 3 damage counters on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: The complex attack patterns of its two swords are unstoppable, even for an opponent greatly accomplished at swordplay.

name: Deoxys

attacks.name: Reflect
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 40 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Psychic Corkscrew
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: DNA from a space virus mutated and became a Pokémon. It appears where auroras are seen.

name: Hippopotas

attacks.name: Push Down
attacks.text: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Endeavor
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage plus 20 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: It shuts its nostrils tight then travels through sand as if walking. They form colonies of around ten.

attacks.name: Dig Under
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

name: Spearow
flavorText: Eats bugs in grassy areas. It has to flap its short wings at high speed to stay airborne.

attacks.name: Firebreathing
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.

name: Mienfoo

attacks.name: Lunge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It takes pride in the speed at which it can use moves. What it loses in power, it makes up for in quantity.

name: Nihilego-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Empty Light
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may leave both Active Pokémon Confused and Poisoned.

attacks.name: Lock Up
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Symbiont-GX
attacks.text: Add the top 2 cards of your opponent's deck to their Prize cards. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Paras

attacks.name: Spore
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Burrows to suck tree roots. The mushrooms on its back grow by drawing nutrients from the bug host.

name: Team Magma Schemer
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.
Discard any 1 Pokémon from your hand. Then draw 3 cards. If you discarded a Pokémon with Team Magma in its name, draw 4 cards instead.

name: Seadra

attacks.name: Water Gun
attacks.text: Does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each Water Energy attached to Seadra but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. You can't add more than 20 damage in this way.

attacks.name: Agility
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Seadra.

flavorText: Capable of swimming backward by rapidly flapping its wing-like pectoral fins and stout tail.

name: Mankey

attacks.name: Scout
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals their hand.

flavorText: The smallest of things could cause it to lose its temper. Because it doesn't hold in its stress, this Pokémon can live a long time.

name: Weedle

attacks.name: Speed Evolution
attacks.text: Search your deck for a card that evolves from Weedle and put it onto Weedle. (This counts as evolving Weedle.) Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: Its poison stinger is very powerful. Its bright-colored body is intended to warn off its enemies.

name: Heliolisk

attacks.name: Eerie Impulse
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Thunder
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: A now-vanished desert culture treasured these Pokémon. Appropriately, when Heliolisk came to the Galar region, treasure came with them.

name: Charizard-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Crimson Storm
attacks.text: Discard 3 Fire Energy from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Raging Out-GX
attacks.text: Discard the top 10 cards of your opponent's deck. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Lanturn

attacks.name: Strobe Shock
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals their hand. If you find any Energy cards there, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: This Pokémon flashes a bright light that blinds its prey. This creates an opening for it to deliver an electrical attack.

name: Tentacool

ancientTrait.name: α Recovery
ancientTrait.text: When this Pokémon is healed, double the amount healed.

flavorText: Drifts in shallow seas. Anglers who hook them by accident are often punished by their stingers.

name: Sandile
flavorText: They live buried in the sands of the desert. The sun-warmed sands prevent their body temperature from dropping.

name: Magneton

abilities.name: Call Signal
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for up to 3 Supporter cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck. If you searched your deck in this way, this Pokémon is Knocked Out.

flavorText: Delicate equipment can malfunction in areas inhabited by Magneton, which send out mysterious electrical signals.

attacks.name: Minor Errand-Running
attacks.text: Search your deck for a basic Energy card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.

name: Hippopotas

attacks.name: Eleventh Hour Tackle
attacks.text: If there are 3 or fewer cards in your deck, this attack does 130 more damage.

flavorText: It enshrouds itself with sand to protect itself from germs. It does not enjoy getting wet.

name: Grapploct V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Tie Up
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is a Basic Pokémon, it can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Moonsault Press
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 100 more damage.

name: Meowth

attacks.name: Pay Day
attacks.text: Draw a card.

flavorText: It loves anything that shines. It especially adores coins that it picks up and secretly hoards.

name: Woobat

attacks.name: Scout
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals his or her hand.

flavorText: The heart-shaped mark left on a body after a Woobat has been attached to it is said to bring good fortune.

name: Croagunk

attacks.name: Swagger
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Inflating its poison sacs, it fills the area with an odd sound and hits flinching opponents with a poison jab.

attacks.name: Haunt
attacks.text: Put 1 damage counter on the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Dark Mind
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Omanyte

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw 3 cards.

attacks.name: Tickle
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: A Pokémon that was resurrected from a fossil using modern science. It swam in ancient seas.

name: Relicanth

attacks.name: Prehistoric Wisdom
attacks.text: Choose a card from your hand and put it in the Lost Zone. Then, draw 3 cards.

attacks.name: Granite Head
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to Relicanth by attacks is reduced by 30 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Discovered by chance during deep-sea explorations, it has not changed since ancient times.

attacks.name: Razor Wind
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

name: Pidgeot-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Mirror Move
attacks.text: If this Pokémon was damaged by an attack during your opponent's last turn, this attack does the same amount of damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Feather Lance
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Ascension
attacks.text: Search your deck for a card that evolves from Feebas and put it on Feebas. (This counts as evolving Feebas.) Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Hitmonchan

attacks.name: Dodge
attacks.text: If Hitmonchan would be damaged by an attack during your opponent's next turn, flip a coin. If heads, prevent that attack's damage done to Hitmonchan. (Any other effects of attacks still happen.)

flavorText: Its punches are blindingly fast, but it can only fight for three minutes before tiring and needing to rest.

name: Black Kyurem

attacks.name: Dual Claw
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Flash Freeze
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: This legendary ice Pokémon waits for a hero to fill in the missing parts of its body with truth or ideals.

name: Pikachu

attacks.name: Spark
attacks.text: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose 1 of them and this attack does 10 damage to it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: When several of these Pokémon gather, their electricity can build and cause lightning storms.

name: Primeape

attacks.name: Wreck
attacks.text: If there is any Stadium card in play, this attack does 80 more damage. Then, discard that Stadium card.

flavorText: It will never forgive opponents who have angered it. Even after it has beaten them down until they can't move, it never ever forgives.

name: Fearow

attacks.name: Drill Run
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It has the stamina to fly all day on its broad wings. It fights by using its sharp beak.

name: Eco Arm
rules: Shuffle 3 Pokémon Tool cards from your discard pile into your deck.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Swoobat

attacks.name: Jet Woofer
attacks.text: For each Psychic Energy attached to this Pokémon, discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Acrobatics
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: It shakes its tail vigorously when it emits ultrasonic waves strong enough to reduce concrete to rubble.

name: Brionne

attacks.name: Captivate
attacks.text: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It cares deeply for its companions. When its Trainer is feeling down, it performs a cheery dance to try and help.

name: Ledyba

attacks.name: Supersonic
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Comet Punch
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It is very timid. It's afraid to move if alone, but it's active if in a group.

name: Tentacruel

attacks.name: Dancing Tentacles
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.

attacks.name: Stick and Absorb
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon. The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It has 80 tentacles that move about freely. They can sting, causing poisoning and sharp, stabbing pain.

name: Shedinja

abilities.name: Shell Survival
abilities.text: Put this Pokémon into play only with the effect of Ninjask's Cast-Off Shell Ability. (When you are setting up to play, you cannot put it face down as your Active Pokémon or on your Bench.)

attacks.name: Life Squeeze
attacks.text: Put damage counters on your opponent's Active Pokémon until its remaining HP is 10.

flavorText: A most peculiar Pokémon that somehow appears in a Poké Ball when a Nincada evolves.

name: Slowpoke

attacks.name: Whismy Tackle
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It lazes vacantly near water. If something bites its tail, it won't even notice for a whole day.

attacks.name: Claw
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

name: Krokorok

attacks.name: Crunch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: They live in groups of a few individuals. Protective membranes shield their eyes from sandstorms.

name: Jolteon

abilities.name: Speed Cheer
abilities.text: The attacks of your Pokémon-GX in play that evolve from Eevee cost Colorless less. You can't apply more than 1 Speed Cheer Ability at a time.

flavorText: Its lungs contain an organ that creates electricity. The crackling sound of electricity can be heard when it exhales.

name: Hop
rules: Draw 3 cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: M Mewtwo-EX
rules: Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Psychic Infinity
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage times the amount of Energy attached to both Active Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness.

name: Porygon-Z

abilities.name: Plasma Transfer
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may move a Plasma Energy attached to 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Tri Attack
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Its programming was modified to enable work in alien dimensions. It did not work as planned.

attacks.name: Hypnotic Gaze
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

name: Krookodile

attacks.name: Piston Headbutt
attacks.text: Move an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Very violent Pokémon, they try to clamp down on anything that moves in front of their eyes.

name: Vibrava

attacks.name: Sand Wind
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Hyper Beam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: It violently shudders its wings, generating ultrasonic waves to induce headaches in people.

name: Gothitelle

abilities.name: Magic Room
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, your opponent can't play any Item cards from his or her hand.

attacks.name: Madkinesis
attacks.text: Does 20 more damage for each Psychic Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Starry skies thousands of light-years away are visible in the space distorted by their intense psychic power.

name: Archie
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Search your deck for a Pokémon with Team Aqua in its name and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward. Treat the new Benched Pokémon as a Basic Pokémon. If it is a Stage 2 Pokémon, put 2 damage counters on that Pokémon.

attacks.name: Overrun
attacks.text: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 of them and this attack does 10 damage to it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Gigalith

attacks.name: Revenge Cannon
attacks.text: Does 10 more damage for each damage counter on each of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 40 damage to itself.

flavorText: The solar energy absorbed by its body's orange crystals is magnified internally are fired from its mouth.

name: Elgyem
flavorText: Rumors of its origin are linked to a UFO crash site in the desert 50 years ago.

name: M Pidgeot-EX
rules: Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Mach Cyclone
attacks.text: You may have your opponent switch his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

name: Zweilous

attacks.name: Double Hit
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 60 damage for each heads.

flavorText: The two heads do not get along. Whichever head eats more than the other gets to be the leader.

name: Fieldworker
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.
Draw 3 cards. Your opponent may also draw a card.

name: Gastly

attacks.name: Pitch-Dark
attacks.text: You opponent can't play any Trainer cards from his or her hand during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Trick Gas
attacks.text: You may switch Gastly with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon's body is 95% made up of gases, which are blown away by strong gusts of wind.

name: Shieldon

attacks.name: Hard Face
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to Shieldon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Shield Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage.

flavorText: A Pokémon that lived in jungles around 100 million years ago. Its facial hide is extremely hard.

name: Ninjask

abilities.name: Cast-off Shell
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you play Ninjask from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon and if your Bench isn't full, you may put Shedinja onto your Bench as a Basic Pokémon from your hand or your discard pile.

attacks.name: Chip Off
attacks.text: If your opponent has 6 or more cards in his or her hand, discard a number of cards without looking until your opponent has 5 cards left in his or her hand.

flavorText: Because it moves so quickly, it sometimes becomes unseeable. It congregates around tree sap.

attacks.name: Drill Tackle
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. If 1 or both of them are tails, this attack does nothing.

name: Snorlax

abilities.name: Thick Skinned
abilities.text: Snorlax can't become Asleep, Confused, Paralyzed, Poisoned, or Burned. This power stops working while Snorlax is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Body Slam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Very lazy. Just eats and sleeps. As its rotund bulk builds, it becomes steadily more slothful.

name: Sneasel

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Beat Up
attacks.text: Flip a coin for each of your Pokémon in play. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Vicious in nature, it drives Pidgey from their nests and scavenges any leftovers it can find.

name: Kangaskhan-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Triple Draw
attacks.text: Draw 3 cards.

attacks.name: Kindred Kick
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

name: Wigglytuff

attacks.name: Expand
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 30 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Double Slap
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 60 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: The body is soft and rubbery. When angered, it will suck in air and inflate itself to an enormous size.

attacks.name: Tail Whip
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

name: Caterpie

abilities.name: Pupate
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Caterpie is your Active Pokémon, you may flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a card that evolves from Caterpie and put it onto Caterpie. (This counts as evolving Caterpie.) Shuffle your deck afterward. This power can't be used if Caterpie is affected by a Special Condition.

flavorText: It releases a stench from its red antenna to repel enemies. It grows by molting repeatedly.

name: Pikachu

attacks.name: Mega Shot
attacks.text: Discard all Lightning Energy attached to Pikachu and then choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 40 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It occasionally uses an electric shock to recharge a fellow Pikachu that is in a weakened state.

name: Scrafty

attacks.name: Rock Head
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Hammer Kick
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has fewer remaining HP than the Defending Pokémon, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: It can smash concrete blocks with its kicking attacks. The one with the biggest crest is the group leader.

name: Joltik
flavorText: Joltik can be found clinging to other Pokémon. It's soaking up static electricity because it can't produce a charge on its own.

name: Tauros

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.

attacks.name: Seething Anger
attacks.text: Flip a coin for each damage counter on this Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: After heightening its will to fight by whipping itself with its three tails, it charges at full speed.

name: Caterpie

attacks.name: String Shot
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Its short feet are tipped with suction pads that enable it to tirelessly climb slopes and walls.

name: Computer Search
rules: Discard 2 of the other cards from your hand in order to search your deck for any card and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Charjabug

attacks.name: Charge
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Lightning Energy cards and attach them to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: While its durable shell protects it from attacks, Charjabug strikes at enemies with jolts of electricity discharged from the tips of its jaws.

name: Rhydon

attacks.name: Rock Tumble
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

attacks.name: Megahorn
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Protected by an armor-like hide, it is capable of living in molten lava of 3,600 degrees Fahrenheit.

abilities.name: Dense
abilities.text: Any damage done to Numel by attacks from Evolved Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's) is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Nap
attacks.text: Remove 2 damage counters from Krabby (remove 1 if there is only 1).

attacks.name: Minor Errand-Running
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 basic Energy cards, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Poliwag

attacks.name: Hypnosis
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Because it is inept at walking on its newly grown legs, it always swims around in water.

name: Darkrai-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Dark Cloak
abilities.text: Each of your Pokémon that has any Darkness Energy attached to it has no Retreat Cost.

attacks.name: Night Spear
attacks.text: Does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Mienshao

attacks.name: Aero Turn
attacks.text: Return this Pokémon and all cards attached to it to your hand.

flavorText: It wields the fur on its arm like a whip. Its arm attacks come with such rapidity that they cannot even be seen.

name: Machoke

abilities.name: Daunting Pose
abilities.text: Prevent all damage done to your Benched Pokémon by your opponent's attacks. Your opponent's attacks and Abilities can't put damage counters on your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Cross Chop
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: As a result of its continual workouts, it has developed tremendous power. It uses that power to help people with their work.

attacks.name: Bubble
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Liepard

attacks.name: Taunt
attacks.text: Switch the Defending Pokémon with 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Sucker Punch
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any Darkness Energy attached to it, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: Stealthily, it sneaks up on its target, striking from behind before its victim has a chance to react.

name: Kingdra-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Hydro Pump
attacks.text: This attack does 50 more damage times the amount of Water Energy attached to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Reverse Thrust
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Maelstrom-GX
attacks.text: This attack does 40 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Naganadel-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Beast Raid
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each of your Ultra Beasts in play.

attacks.name: Jet Needle
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

attacks.name: Stinger-GX
attacks.text: Both players shuffle their Prize cards into their decks. Then, each player puts the top 3 cards of their deck face down as their Prize cards. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Great Ball
rules: Look at the top 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal a Pokémon you find there and put it into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Toxicroak

attacks.name: Poison Jab
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Exact Revenge
attacks.text: If any of your Psychic Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack during their last turn, this attack does 70 more damage.

flavorText: Its knuckle claws secrete a toxin so vile that even a scratch could prove fatal.

name: Deoxys

attacks.name: Teleportation Burst
attacks.text: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Spear Dive
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

flavorText: DNA from a space virus mutated and became a Pokémon. It appears where auroras are seen.

name: Escavalier

attacks.name: Poke Through
attacks.text: Discard a random card from your opponent's hand.

attacks.name: Spiral Rush
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: Wearing the shell covering they stole from Shelmet, they defend themselves and attack with two lances.

name: Toxel
flavorText: It stores poison in an internal poison sac and secretes that poison through its skin. If you touch this Pokémon, a tingling sensation follows.

name: Zubat

attacks.name: Double Attack
attacks.text: Choose 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to each of them. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Capable of flying safely in dark places, it emits ultrasonic cries to check for any obstacles.

name: Ponyta

attacks.name: Ember
attacks.text: Discard 1 Fire Energy card attached to Ponyta in order to use this attack.

flavorText: A gentle grass-eater, it will allow riders on its back. However, anyone attempting to do so must be careful not to get burned.

name: Conkeldurr

abilities.name: Craftsmanship
abilities.text: This Pokémon gets +20 HP for each Fighting Energy attached to it.

attacks.name: Top Down
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

flavorText: It it thought that Conkeldurr taught humans how to make concrete more than 2,000 years ago.

name: Chimchar
flavorText: It is very agile. Before going to sleep, it extinguishes the flame on its tail to prevent fires.

attacks.name: Fury Punch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage times the number of damage counters on Giovanni's Machop.

name: Zeraora

attacks.name: Crushing Claw
attacks.text: Discard a Special Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Discharge
attacks.text: Discard all Lightning Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 50 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

flavorText: It electrifies its claws and tears its opponents apart with them. Even if they dodge its attack, they'll be electrocuted by the flying sparks.

name: Klang

attacks.name: Disorderly Flip
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: A minigear and big gear comprise its body. If the minigear it launches at a foe doesn't return, it will die.

name: Gyarados

ancientTrait.name: θ Double
ancientTrait.text: This Pokémon may have up to 2 Pokémon Tool cards attached to it.

attacks.name: Full Retaliation
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each damage counter on each of your Benched Magikarp.

attacks.name: Thrash
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage. If tails, this Pokémon does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: Rarely seen in the wild. Huge and vicious, it is capable of destroying entire cities in a rage.

name: Hippowdon

attacks.name: Sand Tomb
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Dust Cannon
attacks.text: This attack does 10 more damage for each Colorless in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

flavorText: It blasts internally stored sand from ports on its body to create a towering twister for attack.

attacks.name: 3-D Attack
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Corner
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Fire Fang
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

name: Eternatus V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Power Accelerator
attacks.text: You may attach a Darkness Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Dynamax Cannon
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon VMAX, this attack does 120 more damage.

name: Old Rod
rules: Flip 2 coins. If both are heads, put a Baby Pokémon, Basic Pokémon, or Evolution card from your discard pile into your hand. If both are tails, put a Trainer card from your discard pile into your hand.

attacks.name: Plasma
attacks.text: If there are any Lightning Energy card in your discard pile, flip a coin. If heads, attach 1 of them to Electrode.

attacks.name: Selfdestruct
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to each Pokémon on each player's Bench. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Electrode does 100 damage to itself.

attacks.name: Kindle
attacks.text: Discard a Fire Energy card attached to Numel and then discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

name: Larvitar

attacks.name: Leer
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can't attack during your opponent's next turn. (Benching or evolving either Pokémon ends this effect.)

flavorText: It is born deep within the ground and has to burrow its way out.

name: Magmar

attacks.name: Smokescreen
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Fireworks
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, discard a Fire Energy attached to Magmar.

flavorText: When it breathes deeply, heat waves form around its body, making it hard to see clearly.

name: Tynamo

attacks.name: Generate Electricity
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Lightning Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: While one alone doesn't have much power, a chain of many Tynamo can be as powerful as lightning.

attacks.name: Yawn
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Spit Poison
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

name: Lunala-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Psychic Transfer
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may move a Psychic Energy from 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Moongeist Beam
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't be healed during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Lunar Fall-GX
attacks.text: Knock Out 1 of your opponent's Basic Pokémon that isn't a Pokémon-GX. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Tapu Bulu

attacks.name: Horn Leech
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Calm Strike
attacks.text: If you have used your GX attack, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: It pulls large trees up by the roots and swings them around. It causes vegetation to grow, and then it absorbs energy from the growth.

name: Krokorok

attacks.name: Crunch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: They live in groups of a few individuals. Protective membranes shield their eyes from sandstorms.

name: Venipede

abilities.name: Poison Point
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent's attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), the Attacking Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Using the feelers on its head and tail, it picks up vibrations in the air to determine its prey's location and state.

name: Rhyperior

abilities.name: Toppling Wind
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may discard the top 3 cards of your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Rock Wrecker
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance. This Pokémon can't attack during your next turn.
