flavorText: From holes in its palms, it fires out Geodude. Its carapace can withstand volcanic eruptions.

name: Charmander

attacks.name: Ember
attacks.text: Discard 1 Fire Energy card attached to Charmander in order to use this attack.

flavorText: Obviously prefers hot places. If it gets caught in the rain, steam is said to spout from the tip of its tail.

attacks.name: Horn Rush
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Granite Head
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to Onix by attacks is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

name: Snubbull

attacks.name: Roar
attacks.text: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It has an active, playful nature. Many women like to frolic with it because of its affectionate ways.

abilities.name: Exoskeleton
abilities.text: Any damage done to Pineco by attacks is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

name: Pancham

attacks.name: Act Tough
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any Darkness Energy attached to it, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: There's no point to the leaf in its mouth, aside from an effort to look cool. It's mischievous, so it's not well suited to inexperienced Trainers.

name: Flygon-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Voice of the Sands
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, you may have your opponent switch his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Spiral Buzz
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.

attacks.name: Hyper Beam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

name: Pawniard
flavorText: Ignoring their injuries, groups attack by sinking the blades that cover their bodies into their prey.

attacks.name: Energy Absorption
attacks.text: Search your discard pile for up to 2 Energy cards and attach them to Manectric G.

attacks.name: Thunder Fang
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Stunky

attacks.name: Double Scratch
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: The foul fluid from its rear is so revolting that it can make people feel queasy up to a mile and a quarter away.

name: Passimian

abilities.name: Power Huddle
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, your Passimian's attacks do 30 more damage to your opponent's Active Evolution Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Rock Hurl
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: They battle with hard berries for weapons. Their techniques are passed from the boss to the group, generation upon generation.

attacks.name: Steel Beak
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage.

attacks.name: Air Cutter
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

name: Solosis
flavorText: They drive away attackers by unleashing psychic power. They can use telepathy to talk with others

attacks.name: Tail Smash
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

name: Shuppet

attacks.name: Haunt
attacks.text: Put 1 damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: There's a proverb that says, "Shun the house where Shuppet gather in the growing dusk."

name: Shining Gyarados
rules: You can't have more than 1 Shining Gyarados in your deck.

attacks.name: Outrage
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Shining Gyarados. If tails, this attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Devastate
attacks.text: Discard 2 Fire Energy cards attached to Shining Gyarados or this attack does nothing. This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Then, flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 Energy card attached to each of your opponent's Pokémon that has any Energy cards attached to it and discard those Energy cards.

flavorText: Once it appears, it goes on a rampage. It remains enraged until it demolishes everything around it.

name: Muk & Alolan Muk-GX
rules: TAG TEAM rule: When your TAG TEAM is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Severe Poison
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 8 damage counters instead of 1 on that Pokémon between turns.

attacks.name: Poison Absorption
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Poisoned, heal 100 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Nasty Goo Mix-GX
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed and Poisoned. If this Pokémon has at least 4 extra Energy attached to it (in addition to this attack's cost), put 15 damage counters instead of 1 on that Pokémon between turns. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Lucario-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Aura Strike
attacks.text: If this Pokémon evolved from Riolu during this turn, this attack does 90 more damage.

attacks.name: Cantankerous Beatdown-GX
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Ledyba

attacks.name: Swift
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, Pokémon Powers, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: Sensitive to cold, Ledyba gather in groups to share warmth when the temperature drops.

name: Bronzor

attacks.name: Knock Away
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.

flavorText: There are researchers who believe this Pokémon reflected like a mirror in the distant past.

name: Psyduck

attacks.name: Dizziness
attacks.text: Draw a card.

attacks.name: Water Gun
attacks.text: Does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each Water Energy attached to Psyduck but not used to pay for this attack. You can't add more than 20 damage in this way.

flavorText: It is often seen holding its head in its paws, as if it were suffering from a headache.

name: Krookodile

abilities.name: Black Eyes
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, you may flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Thrash
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage. If tails, this Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: They never allow prey to escape. Their jaws are so powerful, they can crush the body of an automobile.

name: Krookodile

attacks.name: Bother
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent can't play any Supporter cards from his or her hand during his or her next turn.

attacks.name: Knock Back
attacks.text: Your opponent switches his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Very violent Pokémon, they try to clamp down on anything that moves in front of their eyes.

name: Conkeldurr

attacks.name: Chip Away
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Swing Around
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: They use concrete pillars as walking canes. They know moves that enable them to swing the pillars freely in battle.

name: White Kyurem

attacks.name: Damage Rush
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Cold Fire
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavorText: This legendary ice Pokémon waits for a hero to fill in the missing parts of its body with truth or ideals.

name: Dragonite-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Giga Impact
attacks.text: This Pokémon can't attack during your next turn.

attacks.name: Dragonporter-GX
attacks.text: Put 3 Dragon Pokémon from your discard pile onto your Bench. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Snorlax

abilities.name: Stir and Snooze
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is Asleep, flip 2 coins instead of 1 between turns. If either of them is tails, this Pokémon is still Asleep.

attacks.name: Sleepy Press
attacks.text: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon. This Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It is not satisfied unless it eats over 880 pounds of food every day. When it is done eating, it goes promptly to sleep.

name: Machamp

attacks.name: Settle the Score
attacks.text: This attack does 80 more damage for each Prize card your opponent took on their last turn.

attacks.name: Submission
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: It unleashes megaton-level punches that send opponents flying clear over the horizon.

name: Meowth

attacks.name: Feelin' Fine
attacks.text: Draw a card.

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Adores round objects. It wanders the streets on a nightly basis to look for dropped loose change.

name: Tauros

attacks.name: Stomp
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage; if tails, this attack does 20 damage.

attacks.name: Rampage
attacks.text: Does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Tauros. Flip a coin. If tails, Tauros is now Confused (after doing damage).

flavorText: When it targets an enemy, it charges furiously while whipping its body with its long tails.

attacks.name: Double Spin
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

name: Vikavolt

attacks.name: Powerful Storm
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Thunder Jolt Beam
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: It builds up electricity in its abdomen, focuses it through its jaws, and then fires the electricity off in concentrated beams.

attacks.name: Sand Attack
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

name: Mr. Mime

attacks.name: Mimic
attacks.text: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw a number of cards equal to the number of cards in your opponent's hand.

attacks.name: Juggling
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It is adept at conning people. It is said to be able to create walls out of thin air by miming.

name: Makuhita

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It was originally brought in from another region, but now Makuhita from Alola are more famous.

name: Rhyhorn

attacks.name: Leer
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can't attack Rhyhorn during your opponent's next turn. (Benching either Pokémon ends this effect.)

flavorText: Its massive bones are 1,000 times harder than human bones. It can easily knock a trailer flying.

name: Cheerleader's Cheer
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.
Draw 3 cards. Your opponent may draw a card.

name: Charizard VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: G-Max Wildfire
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Metal Scoop
attacks.text: Search your discard pile for a Metal Energy card and attach it to Eevee.

name: Throh

attacks.name: Squeeze
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Superpower
attacks.text: You may do 20 more damage. If you do, this Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: When they encounter foes bigger than themselves, they try to throw them. They always travel in packs of five.

abilities.name: Thick Shell
abilities.text: All damage done by attacks to Kabuto from Evolved Pokémon is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

name: Primarina

abilities.name: Harmonics
abilities.text: Whenever you attach an Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon, except with an attack, Ability, or Trainer card, attach up to 2 Energy cards to that Pokémon instead of 1.

attacks.name: Hypno Splash
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Its singing voice is its chief weapon in battle. This Pokémon's Trainer must prioritize the daily maintenance of its throat at all costs.

name: Hitmonlee

attacks.name: Special Combo
attacks.text: You can use this attack only if your Hitmonchan used Hit and Run during your last turn. This attack does 90 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: The legs freely contract and stretch. The stretchy legs allow it to hit a distant foe with a rising kick.

name: Devolution Spray
rules: Choose 1 of your own Pokémon in play and a Stage of Evolution. Discard all Evolution cards of that Stage or higher attached to that Pokémon. That Pokémon is no longer Asleep, Confused, Paralyzed, Poisoned, or anything else that might be the result of an attack (just as if you had evolved it).

name: Hydreigon

abilities.name: Weed Out
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may choose 3 of your Benched Pokémon. Then, discard your other Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Dark Destruction
attacks.text: You may discard an Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: The heads on their arms do not have brains. They use all three heads to consume and destroy everything.

name: Toxtricity

attacks.name: Risk Taker
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard the top 5 cards of your opponent's deck. If tails, discard the top 5 cards of your deck.

attacks.name: Thunder Jolt
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: Capable of generating 15,000 volts of electricity, this Pokémon looks down on all that would challenge it.

name: Hau
rules: Draw 3 cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Tropius

attacks.name: Bonus Leaf
attacks.text: Remove 3 damage counters from each of your Benched Pokémon that has any Grass Energy attached to it.

attacks.name: Whirlwind
attacks.text: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Because it continually ate only its favorite fruit, the fruit started growing around its neck.

name: Lunala

abilities.name: Shadow Shield
abilities.text: If this Pokémon has any Psychic Energy attached to it, it takes 20 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Full Moon Ray
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage times the amount of Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It is said to be a female evolution of Cosmog. When its third eye activates, away it flies to another world.

name: Sandile

attacks.name: Sand Dive
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: They live buried in the sands of the desert. The sun-warmed sands prevent their body temperature from dropping.

attacks.name: Amnesia
attacks.text: Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon's attacks. That Pokémon can't use that attack during your opponent's next turn.

name: Golisopod-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: First Impression
attacks.text: If this Pokémon was on the Bench and became your Active Pokémon this turn, this attack does 90 more damage.

attacks.name: Armor Press
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 20 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Crossing Cut-GX
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Larvitar

attacks.name: Rock Slide
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: A Pokémon that eats soil. Once it has eaten a large mountain, it goes to sleep so it can grow.

name: Elgyem

attacks.name: Reflect
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Smash Punch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Rumors of its origin are linked to a UFO crash site in the desert 50 years ago.

attacks.name: Ancestral Memory
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. Either way, you can't use this attack again as long as Giovanni's Magikarp stays in play (even putting Giovanni's Magikarp on the Bench won't let you use it again).

attacks.name: Flail Around
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

name: Rattata

abilities.name: Mischievous Fang
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench, you may discard all Pokémon Tool cards attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Its fangs are long and very sharp. They grow continuously, so it gnaws on hard things to whittle them down.

name: Clamperl

attacks.name: Clamp
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It makes a single pearl during its lifetime. The pearl is said to amplify psychic power.

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

name: Scraggy
flavorText: Its skin has a rubbery elasticity, so it can reduce damage by defensively pulling its skin up to its neck.

name: Buizel

attacks.name: Splash About
attacks.text: If Buizel has less Energy attached to it than the Defending Pokémon, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.

flavorText: It has a flotation sac that is like an inflatable collar. It floats on water with its head out.

attacks.name: Paralyzing Gaze
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Aegislash

attacks.name: Painful Sword
attacks.text: Double the number of damage counters on each of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Megaton Slash
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage to 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Apparently, it can detect innate qualities of leadership. According to legend, whoever it recognizes is destined to become king.

attacks.name: Sleepy Song
attacks.text: Each Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

name: Teddiursa

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It lets honey soak into its paws so it can lick them all the time. Every set of paws tastes unique.

name: Klinklang

abilities.name: Heavy Bumper
abilities.text: Any damage done to this Pokémon by an opponent's attack is reduced by 10 for each Colorless in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Gear Spinner
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Gear Spinner attack does 70 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Its red core functions as an energy tank. It fires the charged energy through its spikes into an area.

name: Eelektrik

attacks.name: Overspark
attacks.text: Discard all Lightning Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

flavorText: It wraps itself around its prey and paralyzes it with electricity from the round spots on its sides. Then it chomps.

name: Mantine

attacks.name: Undulate
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Mantine.

flavorText: Swimming freely in open seas, it may fly out of the water and over the waves if it builds up enough speed.

abilities.name: Anti-Lightning
abilities.text: You can't attach Lightning Energy cards from your hand to Zapdos.

attacks.name: Plasma
attacks.text: If there are any Lightning Energy cards in your discard pile, flip a coin. If heads, attach 1 of them to Zapdos.

attacks.name: Lightning Storm
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, put 2 damage counters on Zapdos.

name: Magnemite

attacks.name: Magnetic Switch
attacks.text: Switch Magnemite with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Thundershock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: The units at the sides of its body generate antigravity energy to keep it aloft in the air.

name: Energy Recycler
rules: Shuffle 5 basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your deck.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Dual Ball
rules: Flip 2 coins. For each heads, search your deck for a Basic Pokémon card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Defense Curl
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to Brock's Sandshrew during your opponent's next turn. (Any other effects of attacks still happen.)

name: Landorus

attacks.name: Shout of Power
attacks.text: Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Lands visited by Landorus grant such bountiful crops that it has been hailed as "The Guardian of the Fields."

name: Combee

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

attacks.name: Shoot Through
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: The trio is together from birth. It constantly gathers honey from flowers to please VESPIQUEN.

name: M Kangaskhan-EX
rules: Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Wham Bam Punch
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.

name: Starmie

attacks.name: Synchro Star
attacks.text: If this Pokémon and your opponent's Active Pokémon have the same amount of Energy attached to them, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: At the center of its body is a red core, which sends mysterious radio signals into the night sky.

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Ascension
attacks.text: Search your deck for a card that evolves from Magikarp and put it on Magikarp. (This counts as evolving Magikarp.) Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Eevee V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw 3 cards.

attacks.name: Brave Buddies
attacks.text: If you played a Supporter card from your hand during this turn, this attack does 80 more damage.

name: Misdreavus

attacks.name: Haunt
attacks.text: Put 2 damage counters on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It likes playing mischievous tricks, such as screaming and wailing to startle people at night.

name: Persian

attacks.name: Feint Attack
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on that Pokémon.

attacks.name: Fasten Claws
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 damage plus 10 more damage.

flavorText: A very haughty Pokémon. Among fans, the size of the jewel in its forehead is a topic of much talk.

name: Chinchou

attacks.name: Scout
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals their hand.

flavorText: Its two antennae glow softly to lure in prey, making it a useful Pokémon for night fishing.

name: Sentret

attacks.name: Scout
attacks.text: Look at your opponent's hand.

flavorText: It stands on its tail so it can see a long way. If it spots an enemy, it cries loudly to warn its kind.

name: Kabuto

abilities.name: Ancient Guidance
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for Helix Fossil, Dome Fossil, or Old Amber and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward. This power can't be used if Kabuto is affected by a Special Condition.

flavorText: It is thought to have inhabited beaches 300 million years ago. It is protected by a stiff shell.

name: Ponyta

attacks.name: Stomp
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage.

flavorText: As a newborn, it can barely stand. However, through galloping, its legs are made tougher and faster.

name: Paras

attacks.name: Double-edge Claw
attacks.text: Paras does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: As its body grows large, large mushrooms named tochukaso start sprouting out of its back.

name: Galvantula

attacks.name: Volt Wave
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It launches electrified fur from its abdomen as its means of attack. Opponents hit by the fur could be in for three full days and nights of paralysis.

attacks.name: Signs of Evolution
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 cards that evolve from Eevee, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.

name: Full Flame
rules: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.
Put 4 damage counters instead of 2 on each Burned Pokémon between turns. The Special Condition Burned can't be removed by evolving or devolving the Burned Pokémon.

name: Aegislash-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Mighty Shield
abilities.text: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from each of your opponent's Pokémon that has any Special Energy attached to it.

attacks.name: Slash Blast
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each Metal Energy attached to this Pokémon.

name: Wartortle

attacks.name: Giant Wave
attacks.text: Wartortle can't use Giant Wave during your next turn.

flavorText: It is said to live 10,000 years. Its furry tail is popular as a symbol of longevity.

name: Kakuna

abilities.name: Exoskeleton
abilities.text: Any damage done to Kakuna by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Dangerous Evolution
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for an Evolution card that evolves from Kakuna and put it onto Kakuna. (This counts as evolving Kakuna.) Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: While awaiting evolution, it hides from predators under leaves and in nooks of branches.

name: Ekans

attacks.name: Glare
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: By dislocating its jaw, it can swallow prey larger than itself. After a meal, it curls up and rests.

name: Altaria-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Bright Tone
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Pokémon-GX and Pokémon-EX.

attacks.name: Sonic Edge
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Euphoria-GX
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep. Heal all damage from all of your Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Scrafty

attacks.name: Spit Acid
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned. Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is also Paralyzed.

flavorText: Groups of them beat up anything that enters their territory. Each can spit acidic liquid from its mouth.

name: Spinarak

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It spins a web using fine – but durable – thread. It then waits patiently for prey to be trapped.

name: Snorlax

abilities.name: Stir and Snooze
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is Asleep, flip 2 coins instead of 1 between turns. If either of them is tails, this Pokémon is still Asleep.

attacks.name: Sleepy Press
attacks.text: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon. This Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It is not satisfied unless it eats over 880 pounds of food every day. When it is done eating, it goes promptly to sleep.

name: Phanpy

attacks.name: Flail
attacks.text: Does 10 damage times the number of damage counters on Phanpy.

flavorText: It swings its long snout around playfully, but because it is so strong, that can be dangerous.

name: Hitmonchan

attacks.name: Hit and Run
attacks.text: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: The arm-twisting punches it throws pulverize even concrete. It rests after three minutes of fighting.

name: Piloswine

attacks.name: Charge Dash
attacks.text: You may do 30 damage plus 30 more damage. If you do, Piloswine does 30 damage to itself.

attacks.name: Icy Wind
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Its shaggy coat makes it unable to see. It checks surroundings with its sensitive nose instead.

name: Machop

attacks.name: Knock Back
attacks.text: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It hefts a Graveler repeatedly to strengthen its entire body. It uses every type of martial arts.

name: Pincurchin

attacks.name: Double Draw
attacks.text: Draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Zing Zap
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It feeds on seaweed, using its teeth to scrape it off rocks. Electric current flows from the tips of its spines.

name: Dual Ball
rules: Flip 2 coins. For each heads, search your deck for a Basic Pokémon, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. If you do, shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Bagon

attacks.name: Rage
attacks.text: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Bagon.

flavorText: Dreaming of one day flying, it practices by leaping off cliffs every day.

name: Meowth δ
rules: This Pokémon is both Darkness Metal type.

attacks.name: Pay Day
attacks.text: Draw a card.

attacks.name: Karate Chop
attacks.text: Does 30 damage minus 10 damage for each damage counter on Machop.

name: Solosis

attacks.name: Nap
attacks.text: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Because their bodies are enveloped in a special liquid, they are fine in any environment, no matter how severe.

attacks.name: Bind
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Luring Flame
attacks.text: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with 1 of the Defending Pokémon. Your opponent chooses the Defending Pokémon to switch. The new Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

name: Buneary

attacks.name: Jump Kick
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It slams foes by sharply uncoiling its rolled ears. It stings enough to make a grown-up cry in pain.

name: Giant Stump
rules: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.
Each player can't have more than 3 Benched Pokémon.
When Giant Stump comes into play, each player discards Benched Pokémon and any cards attached to them until he or she has 3 Benched Pokémon. (You discard your Pokémon first.)

name: Lanturn

abilities.name: Blinking Lights
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may look at the top card of your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Swirling Flow
attacks.text: You may have your opponent shuffle their deck.

flavorText: When the bacteria living inside its antennae absorb Lanturn's bodily fluids, a strong luminescent effect is produced.

name: Kecleon

abilities.name: Colorful Body
abilities.text: Kecleon's type is Grass Fire Water Lightning Psychic Fighting Darkness Metal Colorless.

attacks.name: Triple Smash
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: It can freely change its body's color. The zigzag pattern on its belly doesn't change, however.

name: Rattata

attacks.name: Lunge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Cautious in the extreme, its hardy vitality lets it live in any kind of environment.

name: Onix

attacks.name: Screech
attacks.text: During your next turn, the Defending Pokémon takes 20 more damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Rage
attacks.text: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

flavorText: It usually lives underground. It searches for food while boring its way through the ground at 50 miles per hour.

name: Mareanie

attacks.name: Spike Cannon
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It's found crawling on beaches and seafloors. The coral that grows on Corsola's head is as good as a five-star banquet to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Cat Fleas
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

name: Tangela

attacks.name: Nutritional Support
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Grass Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Poisonpowder
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It tangles any moving thing with its vines. Their subtle shaking is ticklish if you get ensnared.

name: Skrelp

attacks.name: Hide
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It looks just like rotten kelp. It hides from foes while storing up power for its evolution.

name: Keldeo

attacks.name: Swords Dance
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Aqua Blade attack's base damage is 120.

flavorText: When it is resolute, its body fills with power and it becomes swifter. Its jumps are then too fast to follow.

attacks.name: Supersonic
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

name: Charizard

abilities.name: Battle Sense
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may look at the top 3 cards of your deck and put 1 of them into your hand. Discard the other cards.

attacks.name: Royal Blaze
attacks.text: This attack does 50 more damage for each Leon card in your discard pile.

flavorText: It spits fire that is hot enough to melt boulders. It may cause forest fires by blowing flames.

name: Terrakion

attacks.name: Sacred Sword
attacks.text: This Pokémon can't use Sacred Sword during your next turn.

flavorText: This Pokémon came to the defense of Pokémon that had lost their homes in a war among humans.

attacks.name: Burn Off
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, each Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Paralyzing Gaze
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Mareep

attacks.name: Static Electricity
attacks.text: For each Mareep in play, you may search your deck for a Lightning Energy card and attach it to Mareep. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Thundershock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: If static electricity builds in its body, its fleece doubles in volume. Touching it will shock you.

name: Hariyama

attacks.name: Push Out
attacks.text: Your opponent switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It is known for its fantastic strength, but as it grows older, it focuses more on training Makuhita.

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Zorua

attacks.name: Nasty Plot
attacks.text: Search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: It changes so it looks like its foe, tricks it, and then uses that opportunity to flee.

name: Ursaring

attacks.name: Adrenalash
attacks.text: During your next turn, each of this Pokémon's attacks does 50 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: In its territory, it leaves scratches on trees that bear delicious berries or fruits.

name: Cubone

attacks.name: Snivel
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon attacks Cubone during your opponent's next turn, any damage done by the attack is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance). (Benching either Pokémon ends this effect.)

attacks.name: Rage
attacks.text: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Cubone.

flavorText: Because it never removes its skull helmet, no one has ever seen this Pokémon's real face.

name: Binacle

attacks.name: Allotment
attacks.text: Draw a card.

flavorText: They stretch and then contract, yanking their rocks along with them in bold hops. They eat seaweed that washes up on the shoreline.

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Shining Magikarp
rules: You can't have more than 1 Shining Magikarp in your deck.

attacks.name: Gold Scale
attacks.text: Your opponent may draw 2 cards. Either way, you may draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Dragon Bond
attacks.text: Search your deck for a card named Gyarados, Dark Gyarados, or Shining Gyarados. Show it to your opponent and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: An underpowered, pathetic Pokémon. It may jump high on rare occasions, but never more than seven feet.

name: Murkrow

attacks.name: Mean Look
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: Seen as a symbol of bad luck, it's generally disliked. Yet it gives presents—objects that sparkle or shine—to Trainers it's close to.

name: Pancham

attacks.name: Bad-Influence Evolution
attacks.text: If you have any Darkness Pokémon on your Bench, search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon. (This counts as evolving this Pokémon.) Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: It does its best to be taken seriously by its enemies, but its glare is not sufficiently intimidating. Chewing on a leaf is its trademark.

name: Raticate

attacks.name: Crunch
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Shadowy Bite
attacks.text: This attack does 60 damage times the number of Special Energy cards in your opponent's discard pile.

flavorText: Its rear feet have three toes each. They are webbed, enabling it to swim across rivers.

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage.

attacks.name: Thermal Blast
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon, if any. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Sand Pit
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

name: Dunsparce

attacks.name: Burrow
attacks.text: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

flavorText: It digs into the ground with its tail and makes a mazelike nest. It can fly just a little.

name: Rhyhorn

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: It is inept at turning because of its four short legs. It can only charge and run in one direction.

name: Salazzle-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Diabolical Claws
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage for each Prize card you have taken.

attacks.name: Queen's Haze-GX
attacks.text: Discard all Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Light Sunflora

attacks.name: Reflected Sunlight
attacks.text: Attach up to 2 Grass Energy cards from your hand to 1 of your Grass Pokémon.

flavorText: Although it is extremely active during the day, it stops moving entirely as soon as the sun sets.

name: Spearow

attacks.name: Mirror Move
attacks.text: If Spearow was attacked last turn, do the final result of that attack on Spearow to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: Eats bugs in grassy areas. It has to flap its short wings at high speeds to stay airborne.

name: Archen
rules: Put this card onto your Bench only with the effect of Plume Fossil

attacks.name: Acrobatics
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: Said to be an ancestor of bird Pokémon, they were unable to fly and moved about by hopping from one branch to another.

name: Togepi

attacks.name: Plead
attacks.text: Ask your opponent if you may draw 2 cards. If yes, draw 2 cards. If no, this attack does 20 damage to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: A proverb claims that happiness will come to anyone who can make a sleeping Togepi stand up.

name: Whirlipede

abilities.name: Poison Point
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent's attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), this Attacking Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Storing energy for evolution, it sits. But, when predators approach, it moves to stab them with poison spikes.

name: Unown [E]

abilities.name: EQUIP
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Unown E is on your Bench, you may discard all cards attached to Unown E and attach Unown E to 1 of your Pokémon as a Pokémon Tool card. As long as Unown E is attached to a Pokémon, that Pokémon gets +10 HP.

attacks.name: Hidden Power
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, whenever your opponent flips a coin, treat it as tails.

flavorText: Shaped like ancient writing, it is a huge mystery whether language or UNOWN came first.

name: Banette

abilities.name: Curse of Devolution
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may devolve 1 of your opponent's Benched evolved Pokémon by putting the highest Stage Evolution card on it into your opponent's hand.

flavorText: Resentment at being cast off made it spring into being. Some say that treating it well will satisfy it, and it will once more become a stuffed toy.

name: Diglett

attacks.name: Dig Under
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

attacks.name: Trip Over
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage.

flavorText: A Pokémon that lives underground. Because of its dark habitat, it is repelled by bright sunlight.

attacks.name: Surprise
attacks.text: Choose 1 card from your opponent's hand without looking. Look at the card you chose, then have your opponent shuffle that card into his or her deck.

attacks.name: Blot
attacks.text: Remove 1 damage counter from Bellsprout.

name: Rattata

abilities.name: Trickery
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may switch 1 of your Prizes with the top card of your deck. This power can't be used if Rattata is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage; if tails, this attack does 10 damage.

flavorText: Loves to eat walnuts, chestnuts, cheese, and milk.

name: Absol-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Dark Fang
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard a random card from your opponent's hand.

attacks.name: Dark Edge
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 20 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

name: Mismagius

abilities.name: Twisted Incantation
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may have your opponent shuffle his or her hand into his or her deck and draw a card for each of his or her remaining Prize cards.

attacks.name: Curse Deeply
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Its cries sound like incantations to torment the foe. It appears where you least expect it.

name: Bronzong

abilities.name: Heal Block
abilities.text: Damage can't be healed from any Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's). (Damage counters can still be moved.)

attacks.name: Oracle Inflict
attacks.text: Does 10 more damage for each card in your opponent's hand.

flavorText: It brought rains by opening portals to another world. It was revered as a bringer of plentiful harvests.

name: Lunatone

abilities.name: Heal Block
abilities.text: If you have Solrock in play, Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's) can't be healed.

flavorText: Because it turns active on nights of the full moon, it is said to have some link to the lunar phases.

name: Clemont
rules: Search your deck for up to 4 Lightning Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Guzzlord-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Eat Sloppily
attacks.text: Discard the top 5 cards of your deck. If any of those cards are Energy cards, attach them to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Glutton-GX
attacks.text: If your opponent's Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from this attack, take 2 more Prize cards. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Impostor Professor Oak
rules: Your opponent shuffles his or her hand into his or her deck, then draws 7 cards.

attacks.name: Pebble Throw
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Double Headbutt
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

name: Pawniard
flavorText: Ignoring their injuries, groups attack by sinking the blades that cover their bodies into their prey.

name: Sawk

attacks.name: Defensive Stance
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon. Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Karate Chop
attacks.text: Does 70 damage minus 10 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

flavorText: Tying their belts gets them pumped and makes their punches more destructive. Disturbing their training angers them.

name: Lillie
rules: Draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand. If it's your first turn, draw cards until you have 8 cards in your hand.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Faded Town
rules: At any time between turns, put 2 damage counters on each Mega Evolution Pokémon.
This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

name: Tentacruel

attacks.name: Supersonic
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Jellyfish Sting
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: The tentacles are normally kept short. On hunts, the are extended to ensnare and immobilize prey.

attacks.name: Focus Energy
attacks.text: During your next turn, Machoke's Mega Punch attack's base damage is 80.

name: Eelektross

abilities.name: Electric Swamp
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon is in your hand and you have at least 4 Lightning Energy cards in play, you may play this Pokémon onto your Bench. If you do, move any number of Lightning Energy from your other Pokémon to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hover Over
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: With their sucker mouths, they suck in prey. Then they use their fangs to shock the prey with electricity.

name: Snubbull

attacks.name: Ruckus
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Small Pokémon flee from its scary face. It is, however, considered by women to be cute.

name: Boltund V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Electrify
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Lightning Energy cards and attach them to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Bolt Storm
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Lightning Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.

name: Munchlax

abilities.name: Baby Evolution
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put Snorlax from your hand onto Munchlax (this counts as evolving Munchlax) and remove all damage counters from Munchlax.

attacks.name: Lick
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It hides food under its long body hair. However, it forgets it has hidden the food.

name: Nidoran ♀

attacks.name: Friend Search
attacks.text: Look at the top 5 cards of your deck, choose 1 Pokémon you find there, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

attacks.name: Double Kick
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Although not very combative, it will torment its foes with poison spikes if it is threatened in any way.

attacks.name: Hypnosis
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Psychic Boom
attacks.text: Does 10 damage times the amount of Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

name: Maxie
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Search your hand or discard pile for a Pokémon with Team Magma in its name and put it onto your Bench. Treat the new Benched Pokémon as a Basic Pokémon. If it is a Stage 2 Pokémon, put 2 damage counters on that Pokémon.

name: Drednaw VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Solid Shell
abilities.text: This Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: G-Max Headbutt
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 80 more damage.

name: Beheeyem

attacks.name: Lock Up
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Damakinesis
attacks.text: Move 6 damage counters from any of your Pokémon to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: Apparently, it communicates by flashing its three fingers, but those patterns haven't been decoded.

name: Aegislash-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Mighty Shield
abilities.text: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from each of your opponent's Pokémon that has Special Energy attached to it.

attacks.name: Slash Blast
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each Metal Energy attached to this Pokémon.

name: Drowzee

attacks.name: Hypnosis
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It can tell what people are dreaming by sniffing with its big nose. It loves fun dreams.

name: Krokorok
flavorText: They live in groups of a few individuals. Protective membranes shield their eyes from sandstorms.

name: Sudowoodo

abilities.name: Roadblock
abilities.text: Your opponent can't have more than 4 Benched Pokémon. If they have 5 or more Benched Pokémon, they discard Benched Pokémon until they have 4 Pokémon on the Bench. If more than one effect changes the number of Benched Pokémon allowed, use the smaller number.

flavorText: To avoid attack, it mimics a tree. It will run off if splashed with water, which it hates.

attacks.name: Headache
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent can't play a Trainer card from his or her hand until the end of your opponent's next turn.

name: Zygarde-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Cell Connector
attacks.text: Attach 2 Fighting Energy cards from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Verdict-GX
attacks.text: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Pokémon-GX and Pokémon-EX during your opponent's next turn. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Marshtomp

abilities.name: Plunge
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Marshtomp is on your Bench, you may flip a coin. If heads, move all Energy cards attached to your Active Pokémon to Marshtomp. If you do, switch Marshtomp with that Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Its sturdy legs give it sure footing, even in mud. It burrows into dirt to sleep.

name: Cobalion

attacks.name: Quick Guard
attacks.text: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Basic Pokémon during your opponent's next turn. This Pokémon can't use Quick Guard during your next turn.

attacks.name: Revenge Blast
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken.

flavorText: It has a body and heart of steel. It worked with its allies to punish people when they hurt Pokémon.

name: Tapu Koko V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Spike Draw
attacks.text: Draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Thunderous Bolt
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

name: Koffing

attacks.name: Offensive Gas
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.

attacks.name: Gas Bomb
attacks.text: Koffing does 30 damage to itself, and don't apply Weakness and Resistance to this damage.

flavorText: Lighter-than-air gases in its body keep it aloft. The gases not only smell, they are also explosive.

name: Stunfisk

attacks.name: Muddy Water
attacks.text: Does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Rumble
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It conceals itself in the mud of the seashore. Then it waits. When prey touch it, it delivers a jolt of electricity.

name: Duosion
flavorText: Since they have two divided brains, at times they suddenly try to take two different actions at once.

name: Purrloin

attacks.name: Knock Off
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard a random card from your opponent's hand.

flavorText: They steal from people for fun, but their victims can't help but forgive them. Their deceptively cute act is perfect.

name: Emcee's Chatter
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.
Flip a coin. If heads, draw 3 cards. If tails, draw 2 cards.

name: Strength Charm
rules: Attach Strength Charm to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it. If that Pokémon is Knocked Out, discard this card.

Whenever an attack from the Pokémon that Strength Charm is attached to does damage to the Active Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance), the attack does 10 more damage. At the end of the turn in which this happens, discard Strength Charm.

name: Stunfisk

attacks.name: Trap Bolt
attacks.text: If, before doing damage, your opponent's Active Pokémon has more remaining HP than this Pokémon, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: Its skin is very hard, so it is unhurt even if stepped on by sumo wrestlers. It smiles when transmitting electricity.

abilities.name: Fire Veil
abilities.text: If Growlithe is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent's attack (even if Growlithe is Knocked Out), the Attacking Pokémon is now Burned.

abilities.name: Scramble
abilities.text: As long as your opponent has any Pokémon-ex as his or her Active Pokémon, the Retreat Cost for Rattata is 0.

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

name: Keldeo-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Rush In
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon is on your Bench, you may switch this Pokémon with your Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Secret Sword
attacks.text: Does 20 more damage for each Water Energy attached to this Pokémon.

name: Nidoran ♂

attacks.name: Pheromone Poison
attacks.text: If Nidoran ♀ is on your Bench, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It raises its big ears to check its surroundings. It will strike first if it senses any danger.

name: Wobbuffet

attacks.name: Mirror Barrier
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: To keep its pitch-black tail hidden, it lives quietly in the darkness. It is never first to attack.

attacks.name: Firebreathing
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage.

name: Marill

attacks.name: Defense Curl
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to Marill during your opponent's next turn. (Any other effects of attacks still happen.)

attacks.name: Bubble Bomb
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed. If tails, Marill does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: The end of its tail serves as a buoy that keeps it from drowning, even in a vicious current.

name: Duskull

attacks.name: Future Sight
attacks.text: Look at the top 4 cards of either player's deck and put them back in any order.

flavorText: If it finds bad children who won't listen to their parents, it will spirit them away—or so it's said.

attacks.name: Bounce
attacks.text: After your attack, you may switch Spoink with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

name: Phanpy

attacks.name: Endure
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, if this Pokémon would be Knocked Out by damage from an attack during your opponent's next turn, it is not Knocked Out, and its remaining HP becomes 10.

flavorText: It is strong despite its compact size. It can easily pick up and carry an adult human on its back.

name: Ferroseed

attacks.name: Self Destruct
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 60 damage to itself.

flavorText: When threatened, it attacks by shooting a barrage of spikes, which gives it a chance to escape by rolling away.

name: Gligar

attacks.name: Double Shot
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage to 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It usually clings to cliffs. When it spots its prey, it spreads its wings and glides down to attack.

name: Rhydon

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: It begins walking on its hind legs after evolution. It can punch holes through boulders with its horn.

name: Blaine's Last Stand
rules: You can play this card only when it is the last card in your hand.
Draw 2 cards for each Fire Pokémon you have in play.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

attacks.name: Singe
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Tri Attack
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Peck Attack
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. If 1 of them is heads, this attack does 20 damage. If both are heads, this attack does 50 damage. If both are tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

name: Guzzlord-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Eat Sloppily
attacks.text: Discard the top 5 cards of your deck. If any of those cards are Energy cards, attach them to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Glutton-GX
attacks.text: If your opponent's Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from this attack, take 2 more Prize cards. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Pikachu

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage.

flavorText: It raises its tail to check its surroundings. The tail is sometimes struck by lightning in this pose.

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

name: Teddiursa

attacks.name: Nap Time
attacks.text: Remove all damage counters from Teddiursa. Teddiursa is now Asleep.

flavorText: If it finds honey, its crescent mark glows. It always licks its paws because they are soaked with honey.

name: Honchkrow

attacks.name: Rip and Run
attacks.text: Discard a random card from your opponent's hand. Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: If its Murkrow cronies fail to catch food for it, or if it feels they have betrayed it, it will hunt them down wherever they are and punish them.

name: Omanyte

abilities.name: Call Up
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, search your discard pile for Helix Fossil, Dome Fossil, or Old Amber and put it onto your Bench. This power can't be used if Omanyte is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Wash Over
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: A Pokémon that was resurrected from a fossil using modern science. It swam in ancient seas.

attacks.name: Fishing Tail
attacks.text: Search your discard pile for a Basic Pokémon, Evolution card, or basic Energy card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand.

attacks.name: Trip Over
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage.

name: Forest of Giant Plants
rules: Each player's Grass Pokémon can evolve during his or her first turn or the turn he or she plays those Pokémon.
This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

name: Pokémon Breeder
rules: Draw 2 cards and heal 20 damage from your Active Pokémon. If you have no cards in your deck, you can't play this card.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Dunsparce

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Spring Out
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It digs into the ground with its tail and makes a mazelike nest. It can fly just a little.

name: Magearna-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Mystic Heart
abilities.text: Prevent all effects of your opponent's attacks, except damage, done to each of your Pokémon that has any Metal Energy attached to it. (Existing effects are not removed.)

attacks.name: Soul Blaster
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Soul Blaster attack's base damage is 60.

name: Sentret
flavorText: When acting as a lookout, it warns others of danger by screeching and hitting the ground with its tail.

name: Scolipede

abilities.name: Poison Point
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent's attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), this Attacking Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Venoshock
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavorText: It clasps its prey with the claws on its neck until it stops moving. Then it finishes it off with deadly poison.

name: Solrock

attacks.name: Double Draw
attacks.text: Draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Solar Heat
attacks.text: If there is any Stadium card in play, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: Solar energy is the source of its power, so it is strong during the daytime. When it spins, its body shines.

name: Teddiursa

attacks.name: Fake Tears
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent can't play any Trainer cards from his or her hand during your opponent's next turn, and any damage done to Teddiursa by attack is reduced by 30 (after applying weakness and resistance).

flavorText: If it finds honey, its crescent mark glows. It always licks its paws because they are soaked with honey.

name: Chatot

attacks.name: Misinformation
attacks.text: Discard all Pokémon Tool cards attached to each of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Tone-Deaf
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Its tongue is just like a human's. As a result, it can cleverly mimic human speech.

name: Krookodile

attacks.name: Dark Clamp
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Bombast
attacks.text: Does 40 damage times the number of Prize cards you have taken.

flavorText: They never allow prey to escape. Their jaws are so powerful, they can crush the body of an automobile.

name: Magikarp

attacks.name: Sea Spray
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, draw a card.

flavorText: It is said to be the world's weakest Pokémon. No one knows why it has managed to survive.

name: Slowpoke

attacks.name: Afternoon Nap
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Psychic Energy card and attach it to Slowpoke. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: Although it knows how to fish, no other intelligent behavior has been detected in it.

name: Masquerain

abilities.name: Intimidating Pattern
abilities.text: As long as Masquerain is your Active Pokémon, any damage done by an opponent's attack is reduced by 20 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Skim Attack
attacks.text: Draw 2 cards.

flavorText: Its antennae have eye patterns on them. Its four wings enable it to hover and fly in any direction.

name: Roggenrola

attacks.name: Smack Down
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has Fighting Resistance, this attack does 50 more damage.

flavorText: The hexagonal cavity is its ear. It walks in the direction of sounds it hears, but if the sounds cease, it panics and topples over.

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage; if tails, this attack does 10 damage.

name: Chatot

attacks.name: Me First
attacks.text: Draw a card.

attacks.name: Tone-Deaf
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: It can learn and speak human words. If they gather, they all learn the same saying.

name: Seadra

attacks.name: Aqua Pump
attacks.text: You may discard up to 2 Water Energy cards from your hand. If you do, this attack does 30 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy card you discarded.

flavorText: Its spines provide protection. Its fins and bones are prized as traditional medicine ingredients.

name: Omanyte

attacks.name: Tickle
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Omanyte lived in the seas of antiquity. Its fossils have been found bearing bite marks from Archeops, so apparently Archeops preyed on it.

attacks.name: Horn Thrust
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Double-edge
attacks.text: Giovanni's Nidoran ♀ does 20 damage to itself.

name: M Absol-EX
rules: Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Disaster Wing
attacks.text: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck. If that card is a Trainer card, this attack does 80 more damage.

name: Dracozolt

attacks.name: Amping Up
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Amping Up attack does 90 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Giga Impact
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: In ancient times, it was unbeatable thanks to its powerful lower body, but it went extinct anyway after it depleted all its plant-based food sources.

attacks.name: Shining Claws
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

name: Silvally

attacks.name: Gear Scan
attacks.text: Search your deck for an Item card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: X-Scissor
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: Although its name was Type: Null at first, the boy who evolved it into this form gave it the name by which it is now known.

name: Machop
flavorText: When bored, this super-strong Pokémon trains by lifting rocks.

name: Holon's Magnemite
rules: You may attach this as an Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon. While attached, this card is a Special Energy card and provides Colorless Energy.

attacks.name: Linear Attack
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Klefki

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Dull Light
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: These key collectors threaten any attackers by fiercely jingling their keys at them.

name: Abra

attacks.name: Psyshock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Using its ability to read minds, it will identify impending danger and teleport to safety.

name: Togedemaru

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Random Spark
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: When it's surprised or agitated, the 14 fur spikes on its back will stand up involuntarily.

name: Farfetch'd

attacks.name: Leek Slap
attacks.text: This Pokémon can't use Leek Slap during your next turn.

flavorText: It always walks about with a plant stalk clamped in its beak. The stalk is used for building its nest.

name: Skarmory

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Blow Through
attacks.text: If there is any Stadium card in play, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: Despite being clad entirely in iron-hard armor, it flies at speed of over 180 mph.

name: Venonat

attacks.name: Stun Spore
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Leech Life
attacks.text: Remove a number of damage counters from Venonat equal to the damage done to the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Lives in the shadows of tall trees where it eats insects. It is attracted by light at night.

name: M Altaria-EX
rules: Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Mist Purge
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any Special Energy attached to it, this attack does 30 more damage and heal 30 damage from each of your Pokémon.

name: Toxapex

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Venom Fever
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 50 damage for each damage counter on that Pokémon.

flavorText: Those attacked by Toxapex's poison will suffer intense pain for three days and three nights. Post-recovery, there will be some aftereffects.

name: Phantump

attacks.name: Ascension
attacks.text: Search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon. (This counts as evolving this Pokémon.) Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: According to old tales, these Pokémon are stumps possessed by the spirits of children who died while lost in the forest.

name: Munchlax

abilities.name: Baby Evolution
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put Snorlax from your hand onto Munchlax (this counts as evolving Munchlax) and remove all damage counters from Munchlax.

attacks.name: Rest
attacks.text: Remove all Special Conditions and 6 damage counters from Munchlax. Munchlax is now Asleep.

flavorText: In its desperation to gulp down food, it forgets about the food it has hidden under its fur.

name: Bisharp

attacks.name: Dragon Slayer
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is a Dragon Pokémon, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavorText: This pitiless Pokémon commands a group of Pawniard to hound prey into immobility. It then moves in to finish the prey off.

attacks.name: Calm Mind
attacks.text: Remove 2 damage counters from Ralts.

name: Donphan

attacks.name: Earthquake
attacks.text: This attack also does 20 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: The longer and bigger its tusks, the higher its rank in its herd. The tusks take long to grow.

name: Bulbasaur

attacks.name: Shake Vine
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Bullet Seed
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: For some time after its birth, it grows by gaining nourishment from the seed on its back.

name: Archeops

abilities.name: Ancient Power
abilities.text: Each play can't play any Pokémon from his or her hand to evolve his or her Pokémon.

attacks.name: Rock Slide
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: They are intelligent and will cooperate to catch prey. From the ground, they use a running start to take flight.

name: Zoroark

attacks.name: Corner
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Night Claw
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, discard 2 Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Each has the ability to fool a large group of people simultaneously. They protect their lair with illusory scenery.

name: Barbaracle

attacks.name: Seven Shock
attacks.text: If you have exactly 7 cards in your hand, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Barbaracle's legs and hands have minds of their own, and they will move independently. But they usually follow the head's orders.

name: Terrakion-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Rock Tumble
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

attacks.name: Pump-up Smash
attacks.text: Attach 2 basic Energy cards from your hand to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like.

name: Arbok

attacks.name: Wrap
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Heavy Choke
attacks.text: If this Pokémon used Wrap during your last turn, this attack does 120 more damage.

flavorText: The latest research has determined that there are over 20 possible arrangements of the patterns on its stomach.

name: Crushing Hammer
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Yamper

attacks.name: Roar
attacks.text: Your opponent switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon is very popular as a herding dog in the Galar region. As it runs, it generates electricity from the base of its tail.

name: Lycanroc-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Bloodthirsty Eyes
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Dangerous Rogue-GX
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

attacks.name: Trembler
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage to each Defending Pokémon, and each Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Gardevoir VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Max Cure
attacks.text: Heal 50 damage from this Pokémon.

name: Zorua

attacks.name: Lunge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: To protect themselves from danger, they hide their true identities by transforming into people and Pokémon.

name: Ekans

attacks.name: Shed Skin
attacks.text: Remove 2 damage counters from Ekans.

attacks.name: Poison Fang
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It sneaks through grass without making a sound and strikes unsuspecting prey from behind.

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.

abilities.name: Heat Up
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may count the total number of Energy cards attached to all of your Pokémon and all of your opponent's Pokémon. If your opponent has more total Energy cards attached, you may search your deck for 1 Fire Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Benched Pokémon, if any. Shuffle your deck afterward. This power can't be used if Typhlosion is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Super Singe
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

name: Phanpy
flavorText: It is strong despite its compact size. It can easily pick up and carry an adult human on its back.

attacks.name: Hypnoblast
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Gift of Spite
