attacks.text: Count the number of your Pokémon in play with damage counters on them. Put that many damage counters on the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Defense Curl
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to Spheal during your opponent's next turn.

name: Shinx

attacks.name: Swagger
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: The extension and contraction of its muscles generates electricity. It glows when in trouble.

attacks.name: Double Chop
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Calm Mind
attacks.text: Remove 2 damage counters from Meditite (remove 1 if there is only 1).

name: Diglett
flavorText: Lives about three feet underground, where it feeds on plant roots. It sometimes appears above ground.

name: Item Finder
rules: Discard 2 of the other cards from your hand in order to put a Trainer card from your discard pile into your hand.

name: Unown [M]

abilities.name: MAGNET
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Unown M is your Active Pokémon, you may flip a coin. If heads, switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with 1 of the Defending Pokémon. This power can't be used if Unown M is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Hidden Power
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage. If tails, this attack does 30 damage to 1 of your Pokémon, and this attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

flavorText: Shaped like ancient writing, it is a huge mystery whether language or UNOWN came first.

name: Burned Tower
rules: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

Once during each player's turn, that player may flip a coin. If heads, the player searches his or her discard pile for a basic Energy card, shows it to his or her opponent, and put it into his or her hand.

name: Toxel

attacks.name: Tight Jaw
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It stores poison in an internal poison sac and secretes that poison through its skin. If you touch this Pokémon, a tingling sensation follows.

name: Power Tree
rules: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

Once during each player's turn, if the player has no Special Energy cards in his or her discard pile, that player searches his or her discard pile for a basic Energy card, show it to the opponent, and put it into his or her hand.

name: Pancham

attacks.name: Comet Punch
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It does its best to be taken seriously by its enemies, but its glare is not sufficiently intimidating. Chewing on a leaf is its trademark.

name: Squirtle
flavorText: Cute Pokémon that charms people with its innocent eyes. Loves swimming all day long.

name: Arctozolt

abilities.name: Biting Whirlpool
abilities.text: Whenever your opponent attaches an Energy card from their hand to 1 of their Pokémon, put 2 damage counters on that Pokémon.

flavorText: The shaking of its freezing upper half is what generates its electricity. It has a hard time walking around.

name: Rayquaza

abilities.name: Ozone Wall
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's.)

attacks.name: Dragon Pulse
attacks.text: Discard the top 3 cards of your deck.

flavorText: It lives in the ozone layer far above the clouds and cannot be seen from the ground.

attacks.name: Poison Fang
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

name: Sandshrew

attacks.name: Defense Curl
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to Sandshrew by attacks during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: Disliking water, it lives in deep burrows in arid areas. It can roll itself instantly into a ball.

name: Thundurus

attacks.name: Thunderous Gale
attacks.text: If Tornadus is on your Bench, this attack does 50 more damage.

attacks.name: Raging Thunder
attacks.text: This attack does 40 damage to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: The spikes on its tail discharge immense bolts of lightning. It flies around the Unova region firing off lightning bolts.

attacks.name: Thundershock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Spark
attacks.text: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose 1 of them and this attack does 10 damage to it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Dusclops

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Its body is entirely hollow. When it opens its mouth, it sucks everything in as if it were a black hole.

name: Furret

attacks.name: Buried Treasure Hunt
attacks.text: Look at the top 4 cards of your deck and put 2 of them into your hand. Discard the other cards.

attacks.name: Slam
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: The mother puts its offspring to sleep by curling up around them. It corners foes with speed.

name: Voltorb
flavorText: Usually seen only in power plants, it is sometimes seen in towns, apparently using the power lines to travel.

attacks.name: Stun Spore
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Trip Over
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.

name: Caitlin
rules: Put as many cards from your hand as you like on the bottom of your deck in any order. Then, draw a card for each card you put on the bottom of your deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Cottonee
flavorText: Perhaps because they feel more at ease in a group, they stick to others they find. They end up looking like a cloud.

name: Liepard

attacks.name: Feint Attack
attacks.text: Does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, or any other effects on that Pokémon.

attacks.name: Claw Rend
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: These Pokémon vanish and appear unexpectedly. Many Trainers are drawn to their beautiful form and fur.

attacks.name: Supersonic
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

name: Bulbasaur

attacks.name: Leech Seed
attacks.text: Unless all damage from this attack is prevented, you may remove 1 damage counter from Bulbasaur.

flavorText: A strange seed was planted on its back at birth. Thus, a plant sprouted and now grows with this Pokémon.

name: Rhyperior

attacks.name: Rock Shower
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Stone Edge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavorText: It puts rocks in holes in its palms and uses its muscles to shoot them. Geodude are shot at rare times.

name: Scraggy

attacks.name: Rising Lunge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: Its skin has a rubbery elasticity, so it can reduce damage by defensively pulling its skin up to its neck.

name: Boldore

attacks.name: Rock Hurl
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: It explores caves in search of underground water. It's not comfortable around water, so this Pokémon takes great care in lapping it up.

attacks.name: Psybeam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

name: Jigglypuff
flavorText: The songs they sing are totally different depending on the region they live in. Some even sound like they're shouting!

name: Metang

abilities.name: Metallic Lift
abilities.text: If Metang has any Metal Energy attached to it, the Retreat Cost for Metang is 0.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.text: Does 40 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: It is formed by two Beldum joining together. Its steel body won't be scratched if it collides with a jet.

name: Team Aqua Ball
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a Pokémon that has Team Aqua in its name, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. If tails, search your deck for a Basic Pokémon that has Team Aqua in its name, show it to your opponent and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Totodile

attacks.name: Aqua Tail
attacks.text: Flip a coin for each Water Energy attached to Totodile. This attack does 30 damage plus 20 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: It is small but rough and tough. It won't hesitate to take a bite out of anything that moves.

attacks.name: Blot
attacks.text: Remove 2 damage counters from Oddish.

name: Starmie

attacks.name: Star Boomerang
attacks.text: You may return Starmie and all cards attached to it to your hand.

attacks.name: Core Flash
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon that has any Poké-Powers or Poké-Bodies. This attack does 50 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: At the center of its body is a red core, which sends mysterious radio signals into the night sky.

attacks.name: Double Kick
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Retaliation
attacks.text: You can't use this attack unless Giovanni's Nidoran ♂ has 2 or more damage counters on it.

name: Trevenant

abilities.name: Nervous Seed
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, your opponent's Basic Pokémon's attacks cost Colorless more.

attacks.name: Energy Press
attacks.text: This attack does 10 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Using its roots as a nervous system, it controls the trees in the forest. It's kind to the Pokémon that reside in its body.

name: Toxtricity

attacks.name: Poison Shout
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: When this Pokémon sounds as if it's strumming a guitar, it's actually clawing at the protrusions on its chest to generate electricity.

name: Inkay

abilities.name: Upside-Down Evolution
abilities.text: Once during your turn, (before your attack), if this Pokémon is Confused, you may search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon. (This counts as evolving this Pokémon.) Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Confusion Wave
attacks.text: Both Active Pokémon are now Confused.

flavorText: Opponents who stare at the flashing of the light-emitting spots on its body become dazed and lose their will to fight.

abilities.name: Rain Dish
abilities.text: At any time between turns, remove 1 damage counter from Lotad.

name: Sentret

attacks.name: Scout
attacks.text: Look at your opponent's hand.

flavorText: A very cautious Pokémon, it raises itself up using its tail to get a better view of its surroundings.

name: Zinnia
rules: You can play this card only if 1 of your Pokémon was Knocked Out during your opponent's last turn.
Attach up to 2 basic Energy cards from your hand to 1 of your Dragon Pokémon.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Doduo

attacks.name: Fury Attack
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: A two-headed Pokémon that was discovered as a sudden mutation. It runs at a pace of over 60 miles per hour.

name: Energy Returner
rules: Search your discard pile for 4 basic Energy cards, show them to your opponent, and shuffle them into your deck.

name: Yamper
flavorText: This Pokémon is very popular as a herding dog in the Galar region. As it runs, it generates electricity from the base of its tail.

name: Ralts

attacks.name: Nap
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It is highly attuned to the emotions of people and Pokémon. It hides if it senses hostility.

attacks.name: Steady Punch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage.

name: Defender
rules: Attach Defender to 1 of your Pokémon. Discard this card at the end of your opponent's next turn. Any damage done to the Pokémon Defender is attached to by an opponent's attack is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

name: Alolan Raichu

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: It only evolves to this form in the Alola region. According to researchers, its diet is one of the causes of this change.

attacks.name: Poison Vine
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

abilities.name: Scavenger Hunt
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put 2 cards from your hand into your deck. Then, search your deck for an Energy card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward. This power can't be used if Furret is affected by a Special Condition.

name: Cherubi

attacks.name: Sleep Powder
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Leech Seed
attacks.text: If this attack does any damage to the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance), remove 1 damage counter from Cherubi.

flavorText: The small ball holds the nutrients needed for evolution. Apparently, it is very sweet and tasty.

name: Doduo

attacks.name: Fury Attack
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: A bird that makes up for its poor flying with its fast foot speed. Leaves giant footprints.

name: Togedemaru

attacks.name: Thunder Shock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: When it's surprised or agitated, the 14 fur spikes on its back will stand up involuntarily.

name: Holon's Voltorb
rules: You may attach this as an Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon. While attached, this card is a Special Energy card and provides Colorless Energy.

attacks.name: Thundershock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Hex Maniac
rules: Until the end of your opponent's next turn, each Pokémon in play, in each player's hand, and in each player's discard pile has no Abilities. (This includes cards that come into play on that turn.)
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Marill

attacks.name: Bubble Drain
attacks.text: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: The tip of its tail is filled with oil that is lighter than water, so it acts as a float.

name: Tyrunt

attacks.name: Crunch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Its immense jaws have enough destructive force that it can chew up an automobile. It lived 100 million years ago.

name: Feebas

attacks.name: Fast Evolution
attacks.text: Search your deck for an Evolution card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: It is famous for its shabby appearance. While populous, they tend to cluster in set locations.

name: Lass
rules: You and your opponent show each other your hands, then shuffle all the Trainer cards from your hands into your decks.

name: Zubat

attacks.name: Venoshock
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 50 more damage.

flavorText: It has no eyeballs, so it can't see. It checks its surroundings via the ultrasonic waves it emits from its mouth.

name: Black Kyurem-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Dragon Fang
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Freeze Shock
attacks.text: This Pokémon can't attack during your next turn.

name: Gliscor

attacks.name: Finishing Stinger
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has no damage counters on it before this attack does damage, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It observes prey while hanging inverted from branches. When the chance presents itself, it swoops!

name: Poké Ball
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, you may search your deck for any Basic Pokémon or Evolution card. Show that card to your opponent, then put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Natu

attacks.name: Telekinesis
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for this attack. (Any other effects that would happen after applying Weakness and Resistance still happen.)

flavorText: Because its wings aren't yet fully grown, it has to hop to get around. It is always staring at something.

name: Gothita
flavorText: Their ribbonlike feelers increase their psychic power. They are always staring at something.

name: Sneasel

attacks.name: Sneaky Smash
attacks.text: You can use this attack only if you go second, and only on your first turn. Discard an Energy from 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Ambush
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: It uses its claws to poke holes in eggs so it can slurp out the insides. Breeders consider it a scourge and will drive it away or eradicate it.

name: Energy Switch
rules: Move a basic Energy from 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Zoroark

attacks.name: Nasty Plot
attacks.text: Search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Foul Play
attacks.text: Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon's attacks and use it as this attack.

flavorText: Each has the ability to fool a large group of people simultaneously. They protect their lair with illusory scenery.

name: Tyrunt
rules: Put this card onto your Bench only with the effect of Jaw Fossil

attacks.name: Crunch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon was restored from a fossil. If something happens that it doesn't like, it throws a tantrum and runs wild.

name: Unown [T]

abilities.name: THROW
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Unown T is your Active Pokémon, you may discard a card from your hand. Then, flip a coin. If heads, put 2 damage counters on 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. This power can't be used if Unown T is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Hidden Power
attacks.text: Look at your opponent's hand and choose 1 card, then have your opponent shuffle that card into his or her deck. Then, show your opponent your hand and he or she chooses 1 card. Shuffle that card into your deck.

flavorText: Shaped like ancient writing, it is a huge mystery whether language or UNOWN came first.

name: Florges-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Lead
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Supporter card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Bright Garden
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage times the number of Grass Pokémon and Fairy Pokémon you have in play.

name: Snorlax

abilities.name: Gormandize
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may draw cards until you have 7 cards in your hand. If you use this Ability, your turn ends.

attacks.name: Body Slam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It is not satisfied unless it eats over 880 pounds of food every day. When it is done eating, it goes promptly to sleep.

name: Magnemite
rules: Magnet: Magnemite's Retreat Cost is Colorless less for each Magnemite on your Bench.

attacks.name: Magnetic Bomb
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 10 more damage. If tails, Magnemite does 10 damage to itself.

name: Latios

attacks.name: Supersonic Flight
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It understands human speech and is highly intelligent. It is a tender Pokémon that dislikes fighting.

name: Jigglypuff

attacks.name: Sing
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Double Slap
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: When it wavers its big, round eyes, it begins singing a lullaby that makes everyone drowsy.

name: Reuniclus

attacks.name: Future Sight
attacks.text: Look at the top 5 cards of your deck and put them back on top of your deck in any order.

attacks.name: Net Force
attacks.text: Does 40 damage times the number of Reuniclus you have in play.

flavorText: When Reuniclus shake hands, a network forms between their brains, increasing their psychic power.

attacks.name: Fury Attack
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Snarl
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage to the Defending Pokémon. If tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Electrike

attacks.name: Roar
attacks.text: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Thunder Fang
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Using electricity stored in its fur, it stimulates its muscles to heighten its reaction speed.

name: Snorunt

attacks.name: Ice Breath
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Double Headbutt
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It is said that several Snorunt gather under giant leaves and live together in harmony.

abilities.name: Investigate
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may look at the top 2 cards of any player's deck or at up to 2 of any player's Prizes. Put any cards you looked at back in the same order. This power can't be used if Noctowl is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Triple Smash
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each heads.

name: Tyrogue
rules: If this Baby Pokémon is your Active Pokémon and your opponent announces an attack, your opponent flips a coin (before doing anything else). If tails, your opponent's turn ends.

attacks.name: Smash Punch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It is always bursting with energy. To make itself stronger, it keeps on fighting even if it loses.

name: Donphan

attacks.name: Spinning Turn
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Wreck
attacks.text: If there is any Stadium card in play, this attack does 60 more damage. Discard the Stadium card.

flavorText: It attacks by curling up, then rolling into its foe. It can blow apart a house in one hit.

name: Kabu
rules: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 4 cards. If your Active Pokémon is your only Pokémon in play, draw 8 cards instead.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Mawile

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Crunch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It uses its docile-looking face to lull foes into complacency, then bites with its huge, relentless jaws.

name: Murkrow
flavorText: If spotted, it will lure an unwary person into chasing it, then lose the pursuer on mountain trails.

name: Munchlax

abilities.name: Baby Evolution
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put Snorlax from your hand onto Munchlax (this counts as evolving Munchlax) and remove all damage counters from Munchlax.

attacks.name: Errand-Running
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Trainer card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: It hides food under its long body hair. However, it forgets it has hidden the food.

name: Donphan

attacks.name: Flail
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Rapid Spin
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you do, your opponent switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: The longer and bigger its tusks, the higher its rank in its herd. The tusks take long to grow.

name: Pidgey

attacks.name: Messenger
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Pokémon, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle Pidgey and all cards attached to it back into your deck.

flavorText: Common in grassy areas and forests, it is very docile and will chase off enemies by flapping up sand.

name: Stunfisk

attacks.name: Trickle
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Thundershock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It conceals itself in the mud of the seashore. Then it waits. When prey touch it, it delivers a volt of electricity.

name: Bruxish

attacks.name: Mind Bend
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Follow the Wound
attacks.text: This attack does 60 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon that has any damage counters on it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It stuns its prey with psychokinesis and then grinds them to mush with its strong teeth. Even Shellder's shell is no match for it.

name: Riolu
flavorText: It can discern the physical and emotional states of people, Pokémon, and other natural things from the shape of their aura waves.

name: Pokémon Catcher
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Lycanroc-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Twilight Eyes
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may discard an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Splintered Shards-GX
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage for each Energy card in your opponent's discard pile. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Strange Cave
rules: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

Once during each player's turn, that player may put an Omanyte, Kabuto, Aerodactyl, Aerodactyl ex, Lileep, or Anorith onto his or her Bench from his or her hand. Treat the new Benched Pokémon as Basic Pokémon.

name: Bisharp

attacks.name: Cut Down
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: This pitiless Pokémon commands a group of Pawniard to hound prey into immobility. It then moves in to finish the prey off.

name: Taillow

attacks.name: Double Peck
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It dislikes cold seasons. They migrate to other lands in search of warmth, flying over 180 miles a day.

abilities.name: Dark Aura
abilities.text: All Energy attached to Tyranitar is Darkness Energy instead of its usual type.

attacks.name: Stamp
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 50 damage plus 10 more damage and does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon, if any. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Look for Friends
attacks.text: Reveal cards from your deck until you reveal a Basic Pokémon. Show that card to your opponent and put it into your hand. Shuffle the other revealed cards into your deck. (If you don't reveal a Basic Pokémon, shuffle all the revealed cards back into your deck.)

name: Mantine

attacks.name: Giant Wave
attacks.text: Mantine can't attack during your next turn.

flavorText: This Pokémon is perfectly adapted to its ocean home. It can gather up enough speed to burst above the water like a whale.

name: Pikachu

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.

attacks.name: Volt Tackle
attacks.text: Pikachu does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: If it looses crackling power from the electric pouches on its cheeks, it is being wary.

name: Omastar

abilities.name: Fossil Bind
abilities.text: As long as you have fewer Pokémon in play than your opponent, they can't play any Item cards from their hand.

flavorText: Its heavy shell is thought to be the reason this ancient Pokémon died out. It's apparently a distant ancestor of Octillery.

attacks.name: Yawn
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

name: Burmy Plant Cloak

abilities.name: Wear Cloak
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Burmy Plant Cloak is your Active Pokémon, you may search your discard pile for a basic Grass Energy card and attach it to Burmy Plant Cloak.

attacks.name: Plant Cloak Tackle
attacks.text: If Burmy Plant Cloak has any Grass Energy attached to it, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.

flavorText: To shelter itself from cold, wintry winds, it covers itself with a cloak made of twigs and leaves.

name: Tapu Koko

attacks.name: Nature Dive
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is an Ultra Beast, this attack does 100 more damage, and discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Although it's called a guardian deity, if a person or Pokémon puts it in a bad mood, it will become a malevolent deity and attack.

name: Miltank

attacks.name: Powerful Friends
attacks.text: If you have any Stage 2 Pokémon on your Bench, this attack does 70 more damage.

flavorText: If it is around babies, the milk it produces contains much more nutrition than usual.

name: Zubat
flavorText: Lives in dark caves. Can occasionally be seen at night, when it flies in search of food.

name: Cover Fossil
rules: Look at the bottom 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal a Tirtouga you find there and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Swalot

attacks.name: Swallow Up
attacks.text: Before doing damage, count the remaining HP of the Defending Pokémon and Swalot. If the Defending Pokémon has fewer remaining HP than Swalot's, this attack does 80 damage instead.

attacks.name: Gunk Shot
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It swallows anything whole. It sweats toxic fluids from its follicles to douse foes.

name: Wigglytuff

attacks.name: Sleep Pulse
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Double Smash
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 90 damage for each heads.

flavorText: When it gets angry, it inhales with all its might, and its body gradually inflates. Sometimes they can grow 20 times larger!

name: Zorua

attacks.name: Rising Lunge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.

flavorText: It changes into the forms of others to surprise them. Apparently, it often transforms into a silent child.

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw 3 cards.

attacks.name: Water Arrow
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Drowzee

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Puts enemies to sleep, then eats their dreams. Occasionally gets sick from eating bad dreams.

name: Vigoroth

attacks.name: Wake-up Punch
attacks.text: If Vigoroth evolved from Slakoth during this turn and Slakoth was Asleep, this attack's base damage is 60 instead of 10.

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Its heart beats at a tenfold tempo, so it cannot sit still even for a moment.

name: Mienfoo
flavorText: In fights, they dominate with onslaughts of flowing, continuous attack. With their sharp claws, they cut enemies.

name: Mareep
flavorText: Its soft wool coat captures air, allowing it to remain cool during the summer and warm in the winter.

name: Chimchar

attacks.name: Ember
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, discard a Fire Energy attached to Chimchar.

flavorText: It agilely scales sheer cliffs to live atop craggy mountains. Its fire is put out when it sleeps.

name: Caterpie

attacks.name: String Shot
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Its short feet are tipped with suction pads that enable it to tirelessly climb slopes and walls.

name: Weavile

attacks.name: Icy Wind
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Evil Admonition
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage for each of your opponent's Pokémon that has an Ability.

flavorText: They dwell in cold places. This Pokémon's main food source in Alola is Vulpix and Sandshrew, which they carefully divide among their group.

name: Honchkrow

attacks.name: Whirlwind
attacks.text: You may have your opponent switch the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Diving Swipe
attacks.text: Discard a random card from your opponent's hand.

flavorText: If one utters a deep cry, many Murkrow gather quickly. For this, it is called "Summoner of Night."

name: Kabuto

attacks.name: Ramming Shell
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 20 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: This Pokémon thrived 300 million years ago. It's said that living specimens can still be seen in a certain region—a rare sight.

name: Chansey

attacks.name: Scrunch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Double-Edge
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 80 damage to itself.

flavorText: It lays several eggs a day. The eggs are apparently rich in nutrients and extremely delicious.

name: Lugia

attacks.name: Wind Pressure
attacks.text: If your opponent has 5 or fewer cards in their hand, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It is said to be the guardian of the seas. It is rumored to have been seen on the night of a storm.

attacks.name: Minus Energy
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon. Your opponent returns that card to his or her hand.

attacks.name: Thundershock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Water of Evolution
attacks.text: Put an Omastar from your hand onto Omanyte. This counts as evolving Omanyte.

attacks.name: Drag Off
attacks.text: Before doing damage, you may choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon and switch it with the Defending Pokémon.

name: Rayquaza-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Mega Ascension
attacks.text: Search your deck for M Rayquaza-EX, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Aeroscream
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, discard 2 Energy attached to this Pokémon.

name: Chandelure-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Cursed Drop
attacks.text: Put 4 damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Eerie Glow
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned and Confused.

name: Magnemite

attacks.name: Random Spark
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: The units at its sides generate electromagnetic waves that keep it airborne. It feeds on electricity.

name: Buzzwole-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Jet Punch
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Knuckle Impact
attacks.text: This Pokémon can't attack during your next turn.

attacks.name: Absorption-GX
attacks.text: This attack does 40 damage for each of your remaining Prize cards. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Azumarill

attacks.name: Play Rough
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

attacks.name: Bubble Drain
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: The bubble-like pattern on its stomach helps it camouflage itself when it's in the water.

attacks.name: Shell Grab
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Here Comes Team Rocket!
rules: Each player plays with his or her Prize cards face up for the rest of the game.

name: Toxtricity V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Poison Jab
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Electric Riot
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 90 more damage.

attacks.name: Lullaby
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Stifling Fluff
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Pokémon Breeder
rules: Put a Stage 2 Evolution card from your hand on the matching Basic Pokémon. You can only play this card when you would be allowed to evolve that Pokémon anyway.

attacks.name: Careless Tackle
attacks.text: Erika's Bellsprout does 10 damage to itself.

name: Xurkitree

attacks.name: Three Mirrors
attacks.text: If your opponent has exactly 3 Prize cards remaining, this attack does 90 more damage.

attacks.name: Signal Beam
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Although it's alien to this world and a danger here, it's apparently a common organism in the world where it normally lives.

name: Claw Fossil
rules: Play Claw Fossil as if it were a Basic Pokémon. While in play, Claw Fossil counts as a Colorless Pokémon (as well as a Trainer card). Claw Fossil has no attacks of its own, can't retreat, and can't be affected by any Special Conditions. If Claw Fossil is Knocked Out, it doesn't count as a Knocked Out Pokémon. (Discard it anyway.) At any time during your turn before your attack, you may discard Claw Fossil from play.

abilities.name: Jagged Stone
abilities.text: If Claw Fossil is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent's attack (even if Claw Fossil is Knocked Out), put 1 damage counter on the Attacking Pokémon.

name: Team Aqua Belt
rules: Attach Team Aqua Belt to 1 of your Pokémon with Team Aqua in its name that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it. If that Pokémon is Knocked Out, discard this card.

At any time between turns, if the Pokémon Team Aqua Belt is attached to is your Active Pokémon, search your deck for a card that evolves from that Pokémon and put it on that Pokémon. (This counts as evolving that Pokémon.) Shuffle your deck afterward, then discard Team Aqua Belt.

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Nidoran ♀ or Nidoran ♂ card and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Double Stab
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

name: Gigalith

attacks.name: Rock Artillery
attacks.text: Discard any amount of Fighting Energy from your Pokémon. This attack does 50 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

attacks.name: Rock Tumble
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: Known for its hefty horsepower, this Pokémon is a popular partner for construction workers.

name: Vullaby

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Its wings are too tiny to allow it to fly. As the time approaches for it to evolve, it discards the bones it was wearing.

name: Energy Exchanger
rules: Choose an Energy card from your hand, show it to your opponent, and put it on top of your deck. Search your deck for an Energy card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Nidoran ♀

attacks.name: Offer Help
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Supporter card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: While it does not prefer to fight, even one drop of the poison it secretes from barbs can be fatal.

attacks.name: Lick
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Fast-Acting Poison
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. If both are heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.

name: Piers
rules: Search your deck for an Energy card and a Darkness Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Electabuzz

attacks.name: Thunder Shock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Electricity leaks from it in amounts far greater than the amount of electricity it eats.

attacks.name: Leech Seed
attacks.text: If this attack damages the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance), remove 1 damage counter from Venusaur, if it has any.

attacks.name: Fury Strikes
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

name: Swellow

abilities.name: Big Wing
abilities.text: If Swellow has no Energy attached to it, Swellow's Retreat Cost is 0.

attacks.name: Mach Descent
attacks.text: If Swellow was on your Bench this turn, this attack's base damage is 60 instead of 30.

flavorText: It circles the sky in search of prey. When it spots one, it dive steeply to catch the prey.

name: Tyrantrum

attacks.name: Chew Up
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has any Special Energy attached to it, this attack does 90 more damage.

attacks.name: Giga Impact
attacks.text: This Pokémon can't attack during your next turn.

flavorText: Thanks to its gargantuan jaws, which could shred thick metal plates as if they were paper, it was invincible in the ancient world it once inhabited.

name: Larvitar

attacks.name: Leer
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Born deep underground, it comes aboveground and becomes a pupa once it has finished eating the surrounding soil.

name: Oddish

attacks.name: Hide
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Oddish.

attacks.name: Absorb
attacks.text: Remove a number of damage counters from Oddish equal to half the damage done to the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance) (rounded up to the nearest 10). If Oddish has fewer damage counters than that, remove all of them.

flavorText: Awakened by moonlight, it roams actively at night. In the day it stays quietly underground.

name: Rhyperior

ancientTrait.name: Ω Barrier
ancientTrait.text: Whenever your opponent plays a Trainer card (excluding Pokémon Tools and Stadium cards), prevent all effects of that card done to this Pokémon.

abilities.name: Rock Wall
abilities.text: Any damage done to your Pokémon by an opponent's attack is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Hammer Arm
attacks.text: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

flavorText: It puts rocks in holes in its palms and uses its muscles to shoot them. Geodude are shot at rare times.

name: Lunatone

abilities.name: Premonition
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may look at the top 2 cards of your deck and put them back on top of your deck in any order.

flavorText: The phase of the moon apparently has some effect on its power. It's active on the night of a full moon.

name: Evosoda
rules: Search your deck for a card that evolves from 1 of your Pokémon and put it onto that Pokémon. (This counts as evolving that Pokémon.) Shuffle your deck afterward. You can't use this card during your first turn or on a Pokémon that was put into play this turn.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

attacks.name: Confusion Gas
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Blot
attacks.text: Remove 1 damage counter from Lotad.

name: Buneary

attacks.name: Bounce
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Its ears are always rolled up. They can be forcefully extended to shatter even a large boulder.

name: Engineer's Adjustments
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.
Discard an Energy card from your hand. Then, draw 4 cards.

name: Piplup

attacks.name: Water Splash
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.

flavorText: It lives along shores in northern countries. A skilled swimmer, it dives for over 10 minutes to hunt.

name: Deino

attacks.name: Push Down
attacks.text: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Lacking sight, it's unaware of its surroundings, so it bumps into things and eats anything that moves.

name: Reshiram-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Flame Charge
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Fire Energy cards and attach them to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Scorching Column
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Vermilion-GX
attacks.text: You may attach up to 5 Fire Energy cards from your hand to your Pokémon in any way you like. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Energy Retrieval
rules: Put 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Boltund

attacks.name: Big Bite
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

attacks.name: Fighting Fangs
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon V or Pokémon-GX, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: This Pokémon generates electricity and channels it into its legs to keep them going strong. Boltund can run nonstop for three full days.

attacks.name: Yawn
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

name: Swellow

attacks.name: Clutch
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Aerial Ace
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: It dives at a steep angle as soon as it spots its prey. It catches its prey with sharp claws.

name: Inkay

attacks.name: Puncture
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: Opponents who stare at the flashing of the light-emitting spots on its body become dazed and lose their will to fight.

name: Grimer

attacks.name: Division
attacks.text: Search your deck for Grimer and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Poison Gas
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It was born when sludge in a dirty stream was exposed to the moon's X-rays. It appears among filth.

name: Whimsicott

attacks.name: Light Steps
attacks.text: Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Like the wind, it can slip through any gap, no matter how small. It leaves balls of white fluff behind.

name: Swirlix

attacks.name: Lick Away
attacks.text: Remove all Special Conditions from this Pokémon.

flavorText: To entangle its opponents in battle, it extrudes white threads as sweet and as sticky as cotton candy.

name: Solgaleo & Lunala-GX
rules: TAG TEAM rule: When your TAG TEAM is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Cosmic Burn
attacks.text: This Pokémon can't use Cosmic Burn during your next turn.

attacks.name: Light of the Protector-GX
attacks.text: If you played Lillie's Full Force from your hand during this turn, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to each of your Pokémon during your opponent's next turn. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: White Kyurem-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Dragon Stream
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Ice Burn
attacks.text: Discard 2 Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon. The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

name: Ponyta
flavorText: It is a weak runner immediately after birth. It gradually becomes faster by chasing after its parents.

attacks.name: Super Singe
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

name: Dusknoir

abilities.name: Spectral Breach
abilities.text: All Special Energy attached to Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's) provide Colorless Energy and have no other effect.

flavorText: At the bidding of transmissions from the spirit world, it steals people and Pokémon away. No one knows whether it has a will of its own.

name: Golbat

attacks.name: Leech Life
attacks.text: Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Every once in a while, you'll see a Golbat that's missing some fangs. This happens when hunger drives it to try biting a Steel-type Pokémon.

name: Nosepass

attacks.name: Rising Lunge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: The magnet in Nosepass's nose provides an unerring compass, making it an excellent partner for Trainers going on a journey.

name: Unown [E]
rules: You may have up to 4 Basic Pokémon cards in your deck with Unown in their names.

abilities.name: [Engage]
abilities.text: When you play Unown [E] from your hand, your opponent may shuffle his or her hand into his or her deck and then draw 4 cards. Either way, you may shuffle your hand into your deck and draw 4 cards.

flavorText: Their shapes look like hieroglyphs on ancient tablets. It is said that the two are somehow related.

name: Burmy Sandy Cloak

abilities.name: Wear Cloak
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Burmy Sandy Cloak is your Active Pokémon, you may search your discard for a basic Fighting Energy card and attach it to Burmy Sandy Cloak.

attacks.name: Sandy Cloak Tackle
attacks.text: If Burmy Sandy Cloak has any Fighting Energy attached to it, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.

flavorText: To shelter itself from cold, wintry winds, it covers itself with a cloak made of twigs and leaves.

name: Mew V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: X Ball
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage for each Energy attached to both Active Pokémon.

name: Kirlia

attacks.name: Beckon
attacks.text: Put a Supporter card from your discard pile into your hand.

attacks.name: Quick Turn
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It has a psychic power that enables it to distort the space around it and see into the future.

name: Glameow

attacks.name: Captivate
attacks.text: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with 1 of the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: It claws if displeased and purrs when affectionate. Its fickleness is very popular among some.

name: Trevenant BREAK
rules: BREAK Evolution Rule: Trevenant BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

attacks.name: Silent Fear
attacks.text: Put 3 damage counters on each of your opponent's Pokémon.

name: Scrafty

attacks.name: Crushing Blow
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: Groups of them beat up anything that enters their territory. Each can spit acidic liquid from its mouth.

name: Gothorita

attacks.name: Destructive Beam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: They use hypnosis to control people and Pokémon. Tales of Gothorita leading people astray are told in every corner.

name: Sophocles
rules: Discard 2 cards from your hand. If you do, draw 4 cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

attacks.name: Yawn
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Flail
attacks.text: Does 10 damage times the number of damage counters on Slakoth.

attacks.name: Body Slam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Knock Off
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 card from your opponent's hand without looking and discard it.

name: Tangela

attacks.name: Absorb
attacks.text: Remove 1 damage counter from Tangela.

attacks.name: Sleep Powder
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: The blue vines shrouding its body are covered in a growth of fine hair. It is known to be ticklish.

name: Vulpix

attacks.name: Singe
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Ember
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, discard a Fire Energy attached to Vulpix.

flavorText: If it is attacked by an enemy that is stronger than itself, it feigns injury to fool the enemy and escapes.

name: Pheromosa-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Fast Raid
attacks.text: If you go first, you can use this attack on your first turn.

attacks.name: Cruel Spike
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Beauty-GX
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Zoroark

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Bonds between these Pokémon are very strong. It protects the safety of its pack by tricking its opponents.

name: Lucario

abilities.name: Stance
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may prevent all effects of your opponent's attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon until the end of your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: Not only does it perceive auras, but it has also gained the power to control them. It employs them in battle.

name: Milotic

attacks.name: Cleansing Ring
attacks.text: You may discard 2 cards from your hand. If you do, remove 4 damage counters from 1 of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Scale Blow
attacks.text: Does 90 damage minus 10 damage for each card in your hand.

flavorText: Its lovely scales are described as rainbow colored. They change color depending on the viewing angle.

name: Wigglytuff

attacks.name: Round
attacks.text: Does 20 damage times the number of your Pokémon that have the Round attack.

attacks.name: Hypnoblast
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Its fine fur feels sublime to the touch. It can expand its body by inhaling air.

name: Tyrantrum

abilities.name: Tyrannical Heart
abilities.text: As long as you don't have more Pokémon in play than your opponent, this Pokémon's attacks do 60 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance), and it takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Crunch
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Nothing could stop this Pokémon 100 million years ago, so it behaved like a king.

attacks.name: Energy Draw
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a basic Energy card and attach it to Remoraid. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Slowpoke

attacks.name: Whimsy Tackle
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It lazes vacantly near water. If something bites its tail, it won't even notice for a whole day.

name: Aron
flavorText: It usually lives deep in mountains. However, hunger may drive it to eat railroad tracks and cars.

abilities.name: Enervating Pollen
abilities.text: As long as Gloom is in play, Resistance on each player's Active Pokémon only reduces damage by 10.

attacks.name: Sleep Sap
attacks.text: Both the Defending Pokémon and Gloom are now Asleep (after doing damage).

name: Jirachi

attacks.name: Stardust
attacks.text: Discard a Special Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon. If you do, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Dream Dance
attacks.text: Both Active Pokémon are now Asleep.

flavorText: It is said to have the ability to grant any wish for just one week every thousand years.

name: Celesteela-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Rocket Fall
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Colorless in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

attacks.name: Blaster-GX
attacks.text: Turn all of your Prize cards face up. (Those Prize cards remain face up for the rest of the game.) (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Iris
rules: During this turn, your Pokémon's attacks do 10 more damage to the Active Pokémon for each Prize card your opponent has taken (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Barboach
flavorText: Its two whiskers provide a sensitive radar. Even in muddy waters, it can detect its prey's location.

name: Bill's Maintenance
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.
If you have any cards in your hand, shuffle 1 of them into your deck, then draw 3 cards.

name: Kabutops

abilities.name: Fossilized Memories
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, your opponent can't play any Supporter cards from their hand.

attacks.name: Rock Slide
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Its body had begun to change so it could function on land. But it didn't adapt in time and went extinct.

name: Castaway
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.
Search your deck for a Supporter card, a Pokémon Tool card, and a basic Energy card. Show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Crobat

abilities.name: Sound Veil
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may prevent all effects of your opponent's attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon until the end of your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Severe Poison
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 4 damage counters instead of 1 on that Pokémon between turns.

flavorText: Its fangs are so sharp, if it bites you in the dark and sucks your blood, you won't notice any pain or realize you've been bitten.

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Toxic Spore
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

name: Piplup
rules: Pecha Berry: If Piplup is Poisoned, remove the Special Condition Poisoned from Piplup at the end of each player's turn.

attacks.name: Splatter
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

name: Meloetta

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.text: Does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Echoed Voice
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Echoed Voice attack does 50 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: The melodies sung by Meloetta have the power to make Pokémon that hear them happy or sad.

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

abilities.name: Harvest Bounty
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if you attach an Energy card to your Active Pokémon as part of your turn, you may attach an additional Energy card to that Pokémon at the same time. This power can't be used if Venusaur is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Body Slam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Whiscash

attacks.name: Water Pulse
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Landslip
attacks.text: Discard the top 3 cards of your deck. This attack does 100 damage for each Energy card you discarded in this way.

flavorText: A glutton that devours anything that moves, it quietly lurks at the bottom of swamps, lying in wait for prey.

attacks.name: Double Bubble
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads. If either of the coins is heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Mewtwo & Mew-GX
rules: TAG TEAM rule: When your TAG TEAM is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Perfection
abilities.text: This Pokémon can use the attacks of any Pokémon-GX or Pokémon-EX on your Bench or in your discard pile. (You still need the necessary Energy to use each attack.)

attacks.name: Miraculous Duo-GX
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has at least 1 extra Energy attached to it (in addition to this attack's cost), heal all damage from all of your Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Imakuni?
rules: Your Active Pokémon is now Confused.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Drifblim

attacks.name: Shadow Steal
attacks.text: Does 50 damage times the number of Special Energy cards in your opponent's discard pile.

attacks.name: Plentiful Placement
attacks.text: Put 4 damage counters on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: At dusk, swarms of them are carried aloft on winds. When noticed, they suddenly vanish.

attacks.name: Rising Lunge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage.

name: Onix

attacks.name: Screech
attacks.text: Until the end of your next turn, if an attack damages the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance), that attack does 20 more damage to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Rage
attacks.text: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Onix.

flavorText: It twists and squirms through the ground. The thunderous roar of its tunneling echoes a long way.

attacks.name: Sludge Grip
attacks.text: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon and switch it with the Defending Pokémon. The new Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

name: Solrock

attacks.name: Heat Burn
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Explosion
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 90 damage to itself.

flavorText: Solar energy is the source of its power, so it is strong during the daytime. When it spins, its body shines.

name: Jirachi

attacks.name: Stardust
attacks.text: Discard a Special Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon. If you do, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Dream Dance
attacks.text: Both Active Pokémon are now Asleep.

flavorText: It is said to have the ability to grant any wish for just one week every thousand years.

name: Happiny

abilities.name: Baby Evolution
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put Chansey from your hand onto Happiny (this counts as evolving Happiny) and remove all damage counters from Happiny.

attacks.name: Hospitality
attacks.text: Switch the Defending Pokémon with 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. Remove 2 damage counters from the new Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: It likes to carry around a small rock. It may wander around others' feet and cause them to stumble.

name: Chimecho

attacks.name: Auspicious Tone
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Pokémon and a Supporter card, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Hypnoblast
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Emitting ultrasonic cries, it floats on winds to travel great distances.

name: Tauros

attacks.name: Herd Charge
attacks.text: Flip a coin for each Tauros you have in play. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Thrash
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage. If tails, Tauros does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: Once it takes aim at its foe, it makes a headlong charge. It is famous for its violent nature.

attacks.name: Shadow Bind
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat until the end of your opponent's next turn.

name: Swellow

ancientTrait.name: Δ Plus
ancientTrait.text: If your opponent's Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack of this Pokémon, take 1 more Prize card.

flavorText: It dives at a steep angle as soon as it spots its prey. It catches its prey with sharp claws.

name: Grookey

attacks.name: Full On
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: When it uses its special stick to strike up a beat, the sound waves produced carry revitalizing energy to the plants and flowers in the area.

name: Nidoran ♂

attacks.name: Leer
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Horn Hazard
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It scans its surroundings by raising its ears out of the grass. Its toxic horn is for protection.

name: Crabrawler
flavorText: It punches so much, its pincers often come off from overuse, but they grow back quickly. What little meat they contain is rich and delicious.

name: Cresselia

abilities.name: My Way
abilities.text: If there is any Stadium card in play, this Pokémon has no Retreat Cost.

attacks.name: Moonlight Gain
attacks.text: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Those who sleep holding Cresselia's feather are assured of joyful dreams. It is said to represent the crescent moon.

name: Mandibuzz

attacks.name: Blindside
attacks.text: Does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon that has any damage counters on it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Punishment
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is a Stage 2 Pokémon, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: It makes a nest out of bones it finds. It grabs weakened prey in its talons and hauls it to its nest of bones.

name: Battle Frontier
rules: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play.
Each player's Colorless Evolved Pokémon, Darkness Evolved Pokémon, and Metal Evolved Pokémon can't use any Poké-Powers or Poké-Bodies.

name: Good Rod
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, search your discard pile for a Pokémon, show it to your opponent, and put it on top of your deck. If tails, search your discard pile for a Trainer card, show it to your opponent, and put it on top of your deck.

name: Mysterious Fossil
rules: Play Mysterious Fossil as if it were a Basic Pokémon. While in play, Mysterious Fossil counts as a Colorless Pokémon (as well as a Trainer card). Mysterious Fossil has no attacks of its own, can't retreat, and can't be affected by any Special Conditions. If Mysterious Fossil is Knocked Out, it doesn't count as a Knocked Out Pokémon. (Discard it anyway.) At any time during your turn before your attack, you may discard Mysterious Fossil from play.

name: Unown [I]
rules: You may have up to 4 Basic Pokémon cards in your deck with Unown in their names.

abilities.name: [Increase]
abilities.text: When you play Unown [I] from your hand, you may search your deck for a card with Unown in its name and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward. You can't use this power if your Bench is full.

flavorText: Their shapes look like hieroglyphs on ancient tablets. It is said that the two are somehow related.

name: Altaria-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Bright Tone
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Pokémon-GX and Pokémon-EX.

attacks.name: Sonic Edge
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Euphoria-GX
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep. Heal all damage from all of your Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Plume Fossil
rules: Look at the bottom 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal an Archen you find there and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Pupitar

attacks.name: Dust Devil
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to each non-Fighting Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's). (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Its shell is as hard as sheet rock, and it is also very strong. Its thrashing can topple a mountain.

abilities.name: Baby Evolution
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put Pikachu from your hand onto Pichu (this counts as evolving Pichu) and remove all damage counters from Pichu.

attacks.name: Paste
attacks.text: Search your discard pile for an Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon that has δ on its card.

name: Xurkitree-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Flashing Head
abilities.text: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent's Pokémon that have any Special Energy attached to them.

attacks.name: Rumbling Wires
attacks.text: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Lighting-GX
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals their hand. Add a card you find there to their Prize cards face down. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Toxtricity VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: G-Max Riot
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 80 more damage.

name: Malamar

attacks.name: Mental Trash
attacks.text: Your opponent flips 4 coins. For each tails, he or she discards a card from his or her hand.

attacks.name: Distortion Beam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep. If tails, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: It wields the most compelling hypnotic powers of any Pokémon, and it forces others to do whatever it wants.

name: Hawlucha

abilities.name: Shining Spirit
abilities.text: Damage from this Pokémon's attacks isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

attacks.name: Flying Press
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon isn't a Pokémon-EX, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Although its body is small, its proficient fighting skills enable it to keep up with big bruisers like Machamp and Hariyama.

attacks.name: Surprise Slash
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, look at your opponent's hand. If he or she has any Trainer cards there, choose 1 of them. You opponent shuffles that card into his or her deck.

attacks.name: Lunge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

name: Stunky

attacks.name: Gas Bond
attacks.text: Both Active Pokémon are now Confused.

flavorText: It protects itself by spraying a noxious fluid from its rear. The stench lingers for 24 hours.

name: Snubbull

attacks.name: Roar
attacks.text: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It has an active, playful nature. Many women like to frolic with it because of its affectionate ways.

name: Charizard V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Fire Spin
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Stun Poison
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed and Poisoned.

name: Phanpy

attacks.name: Crushing Step
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage; if tails, this attack does 10 damage.

flavorText: It hits people with its nose as a sign of affection but doesn't know its own strength and so sometimes knocks people off their feet.

name: Larvitar

attacks.name: Chip Away
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Born deep underground, it comes aboveground and becomes a pupa once it has finished eating the surrounding soil.

name: Exeggcute

attacks.name: Hypnosis
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Leech Seed
attacks.text: Unless all damage from this attack is prevented, you may remove 1 damage counter from Exeggcute.

flavorText: Often mistaken for eggs. When disturbed, they quickly gather and attack in swarms.

name: Lairon
flavorText: It loves iron ore. Groups of them fight for territory by bashing one another with their steel bodies.

name: Abra

attacks.name: Play Search
attacks.text: Look at the top 5 cards of your deck, choose 1 of them, and put it into your hand. Put the other 4 cards back on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Ultra Evolution
attacks.text: Search your deck for Alakazam and put it on Abra (this counts as evolving Abra). Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: It sleeps for 18 hours a day. Even when awake, it teleports itself while remaining seated.

name: Misdreavus

attacks.name: Lullaby
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Nightmare Feast
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is Asleep, this attack does 50 damage and remove 5 damage counters from Misdreavus. If the Defending Pokémon is not Asleep, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It loves to sneak up on people late at night, then startle them with its shrieklike cry.

name: Deino

attacks.name: Slam
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Lacking sight, it's unaware of its surroundings, so it bumps into things and eats anything that moves.

name: Super Scoop Up
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, put 1 of your Pokémon and all cards attached to it into your hand.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Hawlucha

attacks.name: Sky Drop
attacks.text: This attack does 20 less damage for each Colorless in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

flavorText: With its wings, it controls its position in the air. It likes to attack from above, a maneuver that is difficult to defend against.

name: Sudowoodo

attacks.name: Watch and Learn
attacks.text: If your opponent's Pokémon used an attack during his or her last turn, use it as this attack.

flavorText: It disguises itself as a tree to avoid attack. It hates water, so it will disappear if it starts raining.

attacks.name: Poisonpowder
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

rules: Trade 1 of the Basic Pokémon or Evolution cards in your hand for 1 of the Basic Pokémon or Evolution cards from your deck. Show both cards to your opponent. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Slurpuff

abilities.name: Tasting
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may draw a card. If this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, draw 1 more card.

attacks.name: Light Pulse
attacks.text: Prevent all effects of your opponent's attacks, except damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It can distinguish the faintest of scents. It puts its sensitive sense of smell to use by helping pastry chefs in their work.

name: Minun

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Lightning Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Trash Charge
attacks.text: Search your discard pile for a Lightning Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon.

flavorText: It cheers on friends. If its friends are losing, its body lets off more and more sparks.

attacks.name: Distortion Beam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep. If tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

name: Electivire

attacks.name: Electrocharge
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Lightning Energy cards and attach them to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: High-Voltage Knuckle
attacks.text: Discard 3 Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: When it gets excited, it thumps its chest. With every thud, thunder roars and electric sparks shower all around.

name: Tangela

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

attacks.name: Stun Spore
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: The blue vines shrouding its body are covered in a growth of fine hair. It is known to be ticklish.

abilities.name: Baby Evolution
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put Jynx from your hand onto Smoochum (this counts as evolving Smoochum) and remove all damage counters from Smoochum.

attacks.name: Alluring Kiss
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and basic Energy card, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Sawk

attacks.name: Quick Guard
