attacks.text: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Basic Pokémon during your opponent's next turn. This Pokémon can't use Quick Guard during your next turn.

attacks.name: Brick Break
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance or any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Desiring the strongest karate chop, they seclude themselves in mountains and train without sleeping.

name: Snubbull
flavorText: In contrast to its appearance, it's quite timid. When playing with other puppy Pokémon, it sometimes gets bullied.

name: Lopunny

abilities.name: Big Jump
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may return this Pokémon and all cards attached to it to your hand.

attacks.name: Sitdown Bounce
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon can't attack during your next turn.

flavorText: Extremely cautious, it quickly bounds off when it senses danger.

name: Cheerleader's Cheer
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.
Draw 3 cards. Your opponent may draw a card.

name: Vullaby

attacks.name: Rear Guard
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 30 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: They tend to guard their posteriors with suitable bones they have found. They pursue weak Pokémon.

name: Level Ball
rules: Search your deck for a Pokémon with 90 HP or less, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Gardevoir-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Link Blast
attacks.text: If this Pokémon and your opponent's Active Pokémon have the same amount of Energy attached to them, this attack does 70 more damage.

attacks.name: Luminous Blade
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

name: Houndour

attacks.name: Ember
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, discard a Fire Energy attached to Houndour.

flavorText: It conveys its feelings using different cries. It works in a pack to cleverly take down prey.

name: Flower Shop Lady
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.
Search your discard pile for 3 Pokémon and 3 basic Energy cards. Show them to your opponent and shuffle them into your deck.

name: Koffing

abilities.name: Blow-Away Bomb
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you discard this Pokémon with the effect of Roxie, you may put 1 damage counter on each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Place damage counters after the effect of Roxie.)

attacks.name: Poison Gas
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Its thin, balloon-like body is inflated by horribly toxic gases. It reeks when it is nearby.

name: Umbreon-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Strafe
attacks.text: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Shadow Bullet
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Dark Call-GX
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from your opponent's Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Team Aqua Conspirator
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Search your deck for up to 2 in any combination of Basic Pokémon with Team Aqua in its name and basic Energy cards, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Eggsplosion
attacks.text: Flip a number of coins equal to the number of Energy attached to Erika's Exeggcute. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.text: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

name: Mienshao

attacks.name: Feint
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: It wield the fur on its arms like a whip. Its arms attacks come with such rapidity that they cannot even be seen.

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

name: Meloetta

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.text: Does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Echoed Voice
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Echoed Voice attack does 50 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: The melodies sung by Meloetta have the power to make Pokémon that hear them happy or sad.

name: Diancie-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Sparkle Veil
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, any damage done to your Pokémon by an opponent's attack is reduced by 30 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Wonder Stage
attacks.text: If there is any Stadium card in play, this attack does 50 more damage.

name: Houndour
flavorText: It is smart enough to hunt in packs. It uses a variety of cries for communicating with others.

name: Porygon

attacks.name: Conversion 3
attacks.text: Choose Grass, Fire, Water, Lightning, Psychic, Fighting, Darkness, Metal, Fairy, or Dragon type. The Defending Pokémon's Weakness is now that type until the end of your next turn. (The amount of Weakness doesn't change.)

flavorText: Using the most advanced technologies, scientists finally succeeded in making the first artificial Pokémon.

name: Burmy Trash Cloak

abilities.name: Wear Cloak
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Burmy Trash Cloak is your Active Pokémon, you may search your discard for a basic Metal Energy card and attach it to Burmy Trash Cloak.

attacks.name: Trash Cloak Tackle
attacks.text: If Burmy Trash Cloak has any Metal Energy attached to it, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.

flavorText: To shelter itself from cold, wintry winds, it covers itself with a cloak made of twigs and leaves.

name: Charmander

attacks.name: Ember
attacks.text: Discard 1 Fire Energy card attached to Charmander or this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Obviously prefers hot places. If it gets caught in the rain, steam is said to spout from the tip of its tail.

attacks.name: Swift
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.

name: Zorua

attacks.name: Ascension
attacks.text: Search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon. (This counts as evolving this Pokémon.) Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: It changes into the forms of others to surprise them. Apparently, it often transforms into a silent child.

name: Clefairy

attacks.name: Sing
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Moon Impact
attacks.text: If Clefairy is evolved from Cleffa, this attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage.

flavorText: Thought to live with others on quiet mountains, it is popular for its adorable nature.

attacks.name: Whirlwind
attacks.text: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

name: Porygon

attacks.name: Recover
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to Porygon and remove 4 damage counters from Porygon.

flavorText: A manmade Pokémon that came about as a result of research. It is programmed with only basic motions.

name: Boltund

attacks.name: Electrodash
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: This Pokémon generates electricity and channels it into its legs to keep them going strong. Boltund can run nonstop for three full days.

name: Meowth

attacks.name: Double Scratch
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It is nocturnal in nature. If it spots something shiny, its eyes glitter brightly.

name: Rocket's Sneak Attack
rules: Look at your opponent's hand. If he or she has any Trainer cards, choose 1 of them. Your opponent shuffles that card into his or her deck.

name: Nosepass

attacks.name: Stiffen
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 40 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Its magnetic nose consistently faces north. Travelers check Nosepass to get their bearings.

attacks.name: Blot
attacks.text: If there are any damage counters on Erika's Oddish, remove 1 of them.

attacks.name: Sporadic Sponging
attacks.text: If Erika's Oddish has any damage counters on it, flip a coin. If heads, remove 1 of those damage counters.

attacks.name: Smelly Gas
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage to each Benched Pokémon (including your own). (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Pancham

attacks.name: Karate Chop
attacks.text: This attack does 10 less damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

flavorText: It grows up imitating the behavior of Pangoro, which it looks up to as a leader.

name: Shellos East Sea

attacks.name: Muddy Water
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It lives along bodies of water. Its body shape changed to suit its habitat.

name: Riolu

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: It has the peculiar power of being able to see emotions such as joy and rage in the form of waves.

name: Jigglypuff

attacks.name: Continuous Tumble
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: When it wavers its big, round eyes, it begins singing a lullaby that makes everyone drowsy.

name: Chesnaught

attacks.name: Spike Lariat
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 60 more damage.

attacks.name: Adamantine Press
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: When it takes a defensive posture with its fists guarding its face, it could withstand a bomb blast.

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

name: Gastly

attacks.name: Sleeping Gas
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Destiny Bond
attacks.text: Discard 1 Psychic Energy card attached to Gastly in order to use this attack. If a Pokémon Knocks Out Gastly during your opponent's next turn, Knock Out that Pokémon.

flavorText: Almost invisible, this gaseous Pokémon cloaks the target and puts it to sleep without notice.

name: Meditite

attacks.name: Bide
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, if this Pokémon would be Knocked Out by damage from an attack during your opponent's next turn, it is not Knocked Out, and its remaining HP becomes 10.

flavorText: It eats just one berry a day. By enduring hunger, its spirit is tempered and made sharper.

name: Unown [O]
rules: You may have up to 4 Basic Pokémon cards in your deck with Unown in their names.

abilities.name: [Observe]
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may look at 5 cards from the top of your opponent's deck and put them back in the same order.

flavorText: Their shapes look like hieroglyphs on ancient tablets. It is said that the two are somehow related.

name: Carvanha

attacks.name: Bite Away
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, choose a card from your opponent's hand without looking and discard it.

flavorText: They can swarm any foe that invades their territory. Their sharp fangs can tear out boat hulls.

name: Persian

attacks.name: Nasty Plot
attacks.text: Search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Shadow Claw
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard a random card from your opponent's hand.

flavorText: A very haughty Pokémon. Among fans, the size of the jewel in its forehead is a topic of much talk.

attacks.name: Magma Ring
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

name: Pincurchin V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Counterattack Kerzap
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an opponent's attack (even if it is Knocked Out), flip 3 coins. For each heads, put 3 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.

name: Weezing

abilities.name: Blow-Away Bomb
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you discard this Pokémon with the effect of Roxie, you may put 1 damage counter on each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Place damage counters after the effect of Roxie.)

attacks.name: Balloon Burst
attacks.text: Discard this Pokémon and all cards attached to it.

flavorText: If one of the twin Koffing inflates, the other one deflates. It constantly mixes its poisonous gases.

name: Malamar

attacks.name: Mental Panic
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Puncture
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: It wields the most compelling hypnotic powers of any Pokémon, and it forces others to do whatever it wants.

name: Legend Box
rules: Reveal the top 10 cards of your deck. If you reveal both halves of a Pokémon LEGEND, put those cards onto your Bench and attach all revealed Energy cards to that Pokémon LEGEND. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck. (You can play only 1 Pokémon LEGEND in this way.)

name: Double Full Heal
rules: Remove all Special Conditions from each of your Active Pokémon.

name: M Gardevoir-EX
rules: Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Despair Ray
attacks.text: Discard as many of your Benched Pokémon as you like. This attack does 10 more damage for each Benched Pokémon you discarded in this way.

name: Interviewer's Questions
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.
Look at the top 8 cards of your deck. Choose as many Energy cards as you like, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

name: Swablu

attacks.name: Bind Wound
attacks.text: Heal 20 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.

flavorText: It constantly grooms its cotton-like wings. It takes a shower to clean itself if it becomes dirty.

name: Granbull

attacks.name: Jaw Lock
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: Although it's popular with young people, Granbull is timid and sensitive, so it's totally incompetent as a watchdog.

name: Zweilous

attacks.name: Body Slam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: The two heads do not get along. Whichever head eats more than the other gets to be the leader.

name: Fletchling

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.

flavorText: These friendly Pokémon send signals to one another with beautiful chirps and tail-feather movements.

attacks.name: Sand-attack
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

name: Woobat

attacks.name: Odor Sleuth
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, put a card from your discard pile into your hand.

flavorText: The heart-shaped mark left on a body after a woobat has been attached to it is said to bring good fortune.

name: Mandibuzz

attacks.name: Bone Rush
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Dark Pulse
attacks.text: Does 10 more damage for each Darkness Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.

flavorText: Watching from the sky, they swoop to strike weakened Pokémon on the ground. They decorate themselves with bones.

attacks.name: Firebreathing
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage.

name: Crabrawler
flavorText: While guarding its weak points with its pincers, it looks for an opening and unleashes punches. When it loses, it foams at the mouth and faints.

name: Spinarak

attacks.name: Spider Web
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It lies still in the same pose for days in its web, waiting for its unsuspecting prey to wander close.

name: Scorbunny

attacks.name: Me First
attacks.text: Draw a card.

flavorText: A warm-up of running around gets fire energy coursing through this Pokémon's body. Once that happens, it's ready to fight at full power.

attacks.name: Plus Energy
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, attach a Lightning Energy card from your hand to any of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Dazzle Blast
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

name: Tentacruel

attacks.name: Tentacle Strike
attacks.text: Search your opponent's discard pile for up to 2 Energy cards and attach them to any of your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like. For each Energy card attached in this way, this attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Dangerous Poison
attacks.text: If Tentacruel has less Energy attached to it than the Defending Pokémon, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed and Poisoned.

flavorText: With 80 tentacles for ensnaring victims, it prevents escape until the prey is weakened by poison.

name: Pikachu

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage; if tails, this attack does 10 damage.

attacks.name: Agility
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Pikachu.

flavorText: It raises its tail to check its surroundings. The tail is sometimes struck by lightning in this pose.

name: Ninetales

abilities.name: Bright Look
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with his or her Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hexed Flame
attacks.text: Does 50 more damage for each Special Condition affecting the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: Its nine tails are said to be imbued with a mystic power. It can live for a thousand years.

name: Treecko

attacks.name: Tail Crush
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage.

flavorText: The soles of its feet are covered by countless tiny spikes, enabling it to walk on walls and ceilings.

name: Rayquaza-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Mega Ascension
attacks.text: Search your deck for M Rayquaza-EX, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Aeroscream
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, discard 2 Energy attached to this Pokémon.

name: Doduo

attacks.name: Fury Attack
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: A bird that makes up for its poor flying with its fast foot speed. Leaves giant footprints.

name: Celio's Network
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.
Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon or Evolution card (excluding Pokémon-ex), show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Dedenne

attacks.name: Nuzzle
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Spiral Drain
attacks.text: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Its whiskers serve as antennas. By sending and receiving electrical waves, it can communicate with others over vast distances.

name: Golett

attacks.name: Dynamic Punch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: These Pokémon are thought to have been created by the science of an ancient and mysterious civilization.

name: Root Fossil
rules: Play Root Fossil as if it were a Basic Pokémon. While in play, Root Fossil counts as a Colorless Pokémon (as well as a Trainer card). Root Fossil has no attacks of its own, can't retreat, and can't be affected by any Special Conditions. If Root Fossil is Knocked Out, it doesn't count as a Knocked Out Pokémon. (Discard it anyway.) At any time during your turn before your attack, you may discard Root Fossil from play.

abilities.name: Spongy Stone
abilities.text: At any time between turns, remove 1 damage counter from Root Fossil.

name: Pineco
flavorText: It waits suspended from tree branches for insects to fly into its mouth, often not moving for hours at a time.

name: Dragonite-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Pull Up
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench, you may put 2 Basic Pokémon (except for Dragonite-EX) from your discard pile into your hand.

attacks.name: Hyper Beam
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

name: Onix

attacks.name: Harden
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, if Onix would be damaged by an attack, prevent that attack's damage done to Onix if that damage is 40 or less.

attacks.name: Bind
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: When it travels underground, it causes rumbling and tremors. It can move at 50 mph.

attacks.name: Sand Attack
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

name: Chinchou

attacks.name: Thunder Wave
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It lives in the depths beyond the reach of sunlight. It flashes lights on its antennae to communicate with others of its kind.

name: Darkrai-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Restoration
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon is in your discard pile, you may put it onto your Bench. Then, attach a Darkness Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Dark Cleave
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

attacks.name: Dead End-GX
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, that Pokémon is Knocked Out. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Skuntank

attacks.name: Sticky Smokescreen
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips 2 coins. If either of them is tails, that attack does nothing.

flavorText: It sprays a stinky fluid from its tail. The fluid smells worse the longer it is allowed to fester.

name: Nidorina

attacks.name: Jump Tackle
attacks.text: Nidorina does 10 damage to itself, and don't apply Weakness and Resistance to this damage.

attacks.name: Stress Poison
attacks.text: If Nidorina has any damage counters on it, this attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: When it senses danger, it raises all the barbs on its body. These barbs grow slower than NIDORINO's.

name: Kingdra-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Big Storm
attacks.text: Discard any Stadium card in play.

attacks.name: Dragon Trail
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each basic Lightning Energy attached to this Pokémon.

name: Sandshrew
flavorText: It digs deep burrows to live in. When in danger, it rolls up its body to withstand attacks.

name: Zygarde

abilities.name: Earthen Aura
abilities.text: Damage from this Pokémon's attacks isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

attacks.name: Peace Maker
attacks.text: If your opponent has an Ultra Beast in play, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: This is Zygarde's form when about 10% of its cells have been gathered. It runs across the land at speeds greater than 60 mph.

name: Spearow

attacks.name: Claw
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It flaps its small wings busily to fly. Using its beak, it searches in grass for prey.

name: Lucky Helmet
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.
Whenever the Pokémon this card is attached to is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent's attack (even if that Pokémon is Knocked Out), draw 2 cards.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Lucario

abilities.name: Reflexive Retaliation
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent's attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 2 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.

attacks.name: Aura Sphere
attacks.text: Does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: A well-trained one can sense auras to identify and take in the feelings of creatures over half a mile away.

attacks.name: Splash About
attacks.text: If Swablu has less Energy attached to it than the Defending Pokémon, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.

name: Copycat
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.
Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw a number of cards equal to the number of cards in your opponent's hand.

attacks.name: Stretch Vine
attacks.text: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose 1 of them and this attack does 20 damage to it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Pokémon Catcher
rules: Switch your opponent's Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: The Boss's Way
rules: Search your deck for an Evolution card with Dark in its name. Show it to your opponent and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Switch
rules: Switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Suspicious Food Tin
rules: Heal 80 damage from 1 of your Pokémon that has at least 1 Psychic Energy attached. If you healed any damage in this way, discard a Psychic Energy from it.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Rockruff

attacks.name: Corner
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Wild Kick
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It's considered to be a good Pokémon for beginners because of its friendliness, but its disposition grows rougher as it grows up.

name: Aggron

attacks.name: Revenge Cannon
attacks.text: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on all of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Buster Swing
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: It claims an entire mountain as its own. The more wounds it has, the more it has battled, so don't take it lightly.

name: Espeon & Deoxys-GX
rules: TAG TEAM rule: When your TAG TEAM is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Psychic Club
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each of your Benched Psychic Pokémon.

attacks.name: Cross Division-GX
attacks.text: Put 10 damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like. If this Pokémon has at least 3 extra Energy attached to it (in addition to this attack's cost), put 20 damage counters on them instead. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Klink

attacks.name: Bind
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: The two minigears that mesh together are predetermined. Each will rebound from other minigears without meshing.

attacks.name: Honcho's Command
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 in any combination of Stadium cards or Trainer cards that has Team Galactic's Invention in its name, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Target Attack
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. If that Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Maintenance
rules: Shuffle 2 cards from your hand into your deck. (If you can't shuffle 2 cards into your deck, you can't play this card.) Then, draw a card.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Riolu
flavorText: It has the peculiar power of being able to see emotions such as joy and rage in the form of waves.

name: Zorua

attacks.name: Paralyzing Gaze
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Shadow Bind
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: To protect themselves from danger, they hide their true identities by transforming into people and Pokémon.

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Ember
attacks.text: Discard a Fire Energy attached to Vulpix.

name: Gible

attacks.name: Never Enough
attacks.text: Discard a card from your hand. If you do, draw 2 cards.

flavorText: It nests in small, horizontal holes in cave walls. It pounces to catch prey that stray too close.

abilities.name: Poison Pollen
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. This power can't be used if Vileplume is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Petal Dance
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads. Vileplume is now Confused.

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Corner
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat until the end of your opponent's next turn.

name: Woobat

attacks.name: Unamplified Soundwave
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) If you have any cards in your hand, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: While inside a cave, if you look up and see lots of heart-shaped marks lining the walls, it's evidence that Woobat live there.

attacks.name: Friend Search
attacks.text: Look at the top 5 cards of your deck. Choose a Basic Pokémon or Evolution card you find there, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Put the other 4 cards back on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Continuous Charge
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 30 damage plus 20 more damage for each heads. Put a damage counter on Piloswine for each heads.

name: Gastly

abilities.name: Swelling Spite
abilities.text: When this Pokémon is Knocked Out, search your deck for up to 2 Haunter and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It's said that gas emanating from a graveyard was possessed by the grievances of the deceased and thus became a Pokémon.

name: Cleffa

abilities.name: Baby Evolution
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put Clefairy from your hand onto Cleffa (this counts as evolving Cleffa) and remove all damage counters from Cleffa.

attacks.name: Eeek
attacks.text: Draw a card.

flavorText: Its silhouette is like a star. It is believed to arrive riding on shooting stars.

name: Pincurchin

attacks.name: Shocking Needles
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads. If at least 2 of them are heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It feeds on seaweed, using its teeth to scrape it off rocks. Electric current flows from the tips of its spines.

name: Igglybuff

abilities.name: Baby Evolution
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put Jigglypuff from your hand onto Igglybuff (this counts as evolving Igglybuff) and remove all damage counters from Igglybuff.

attacks.name: Inquire
attacks.text: Draw a card. If you didn't play any Supporter card from your hand during this turn, draw 2 more cards.

flavorText: It has a soft and bouncy body. Once it starts bouncing, it becomes impossible to stop.

name: Scoop Up
rules: Choose 1 of your Pokémon in play and return its Basic Pokémon card to your hand. (Discard all cards attached to that card.)

name: Meditite
flavorText: It eats just one berry a day. By enduring hunger, its spirit is tempered and made sharper.

attacks.name: Minor Errand-Running
attacks.text: Search your deck for a basic Energy card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Psyduck

attacks.name: Tripping Headbutt
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. If tails, this attack does 30 damage to 1 of your Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It has mystical powers but doesn't recall that it has used them. That is why it always looks puzzled.

attacks.name: Ember
attacks.text: Discard a Fire Energy card attached to Growlithe.

name: Aipom

attacks.name: Throw Off
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tool cards attached to that Pokémon.

flavorText: It uses its tail to pluck fruits that are out of reach. Its tail is more adept than its real hands.

name: Team Aqua Hideout
rules: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play.
Each Pokémon that does not have Team Aqua in its name pays Colorless more to retreat.

name: Salamence-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Dragon Lift
abilities.text: Your Pokémon in play have no Retreat Cost, except Pokémon-GX and Pokémon-EX.

attacks.name: Bright Flame
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Flame Jet-GX
attacks.text: This attack does 120 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Pupitar

attacks.name: Payback
attacks.text: If your opponent has exactly 1 Prize card remaining, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: Its shell is as hard as sheet rock, and it is also very strong. Its thrashing can topple a mountain.

name: Passimian

attacks.name: Fling
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Team Play
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage for each of your Benched Passimian.

flavorText: They form groups of roughly 20 individuals. Their mutual bond is remarkable—they will never let down a comrade.

name: Hawlucha-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Counterattack
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent's attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 2 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.

attacks.name: Moonsault Stomp
attacks.text: If there is any Stadium card in play, this attack does 40 more damage.

name: Houndoom

attacks.name: Fire Fang
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: The flames it breathes when angry contain toxins. If they cause a burn, it will hurt forever.

name: Murkrow

attacks.name: Switcheroo
attacks.text: Move a Pokémon Tool card attached to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon to another of your opponent's Pokémon (excluding Pokémon that already has a Pokémon Tool attached to it). (If an effect of this attack is prevented, this attack does nothing.)

flavorText: If spotted, it will lure an unwary person into chasing it, then lose the pursuer on mountain trails.

attacks.name: Soothing Wave
attacks.text: Each Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

name: Buzzwole-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Jet Punch
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Knuckle Impact
attacks.text: This Pokémon can't attack during your next turn.

attacks.name: Absorption-GX
attacks.text: This attack does 40 damage for each of your remaining Prize cards. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Furfrou

attacks.name: Tight Jaw
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Trimming its fluffy fur not only makes it more elegant but also increases the swiftness of its movements.

attacks.name: Sleep Powder
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Grasping Vine
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, draw 2 cards.

name: Altaria

ancientTrait.name: Δ Evolution
ancientTrait.text: You may play this card from your hand to evolve a Pokémon during your first turn or the turn you play that Pokémon.

abilities.name: Clear Humming
abilities.text: Each of your Colorless Pokémon has no Weakness.

flavorText: On sunny days, it flies freely through the sky and blends into the clouds. It sings in a beautiful soprano.

name: Yveltal

attacks.name: Oblivion Wing
attacks.text: Attach a Darkness Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Darkness Blade
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon can't attack during your next turn.

flavorText: When this legendary Pokémon's wings and tail feathers spread wide and glow red, it absorbs the life force of all living creatures.

name: Charmander

attacks.name: Lava Burn
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: The fire on the tip of its tail is a measure of its life. If healthy, its tail burns intensely.

name: Swoobat

attacks.name: Wave Amplification
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Returning Echo attack does 60 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Returning Echo
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, return this Pokémon and all cards attached to it to your hand.

flavorText: Anyone who comes into contact with the ultrasonic waves emitted by a courting male experiences a positive mood shift.

name: Medicham

attacks.name: Strike of Enlightenment
attacks.text: If this Pokémon's remaining HP is 30 or less, this attack does 160 more damage.

attacks.name: Spinning Kick
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: Through yoga training, it gained the psychic power to predict its foe's next move.

name: Goldeen
flavorText: Its tail fin billows like an elegant ballroom dress, giving it the nickname "Water Queen."

name: Klefki

abilities.name: Wonder Lock
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon is on your Bench, you may discard all cards attached to this Pokémon and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon as a Pokémon Tool card. Prevent any damage done to the Pokémon this card is attached to by attacks from your opponent's Mega Evolution Pokémon. If this card is attached to a Pokémon, discard this card at the end of your opponent's turn.

flavorText: It never lets go of a key that it likes, so people give it the keys to vaults and safes as a way to prevent crime.

name: Challenge!
rules: Ask your opponent if he or she accepts your challenge. If your opponent declines (or if both Benches are full), draw 2 cards. If your opponent accepts, each of you searches your decks for any number of Basic Pokémon cards and puts them face down onto your Benches. (A player can't do this if his or her Bench is full.) When you both have finished, shuffle your decks and turn those cards face up.

name: Meowth

attacks.name: Turmoil Strike
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon that has any damage counters on it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Adores round objects. It wanders the streets on a nightly basis to look for dropped loose change.

name: Registeel

attacks.name: Turbo Arm
attacks.text: Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Iron Hand
attacks.text: If Regice is on your Bench, heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Tempered by pressure underground over tens of thousands of years, its body cannot be scratched.

name: Regigigas-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Giga Power
attacks.text: You may do 20 more damage. If you do, this Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.

attacks.name: Raging Hammer
attacks.text: Does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

name: Cessation Crystal
rules: Attach Cessation Crystal to 1 of your Pokémon (excluding Pokémon-ex) that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it. If the Pokémon Cessation Crystal is attached to is a Pokémon-ex, discard this card.
As long as Cessation Crystal is attached to an Active Pokémon, each player's Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's) can't use any Poké-Powers or Poké-Bodies.

name: Tyrantrum-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Despotic Fang
abilities.text: Damage from this Pokémon's attacks isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Dragon Fang
attacks.text: Discard 3 Energy attached to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Foul Gas
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. If tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Misfire
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, put 6 damage counters on Weezing.

name: Clefairy
rules: Moon Stone: Clefairy can evolve during the turn you play it.

attacks.name: Double Slap
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

name: Starly

attacks.name: Whirlwind
attacks.text: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Usually with a large flock, it is barely noticeable when alone. Its cries are very strident.

name: Ampharos

attacks.name: Random Spark
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Electricannon
attacks.text: You may discard all Lightning Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: The tip of its tail shines brightly. In the olden days, people sent signals using the tail's light.

name: Lunatone

attacks.name: Future Sight
attacks.text: Look at the top 4 cards of either player's deck and put them back in any order.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: The phase of the moon apparently has some effect on its power. It's active on the night of a full moon.

attacks.name: Thundershock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Darkrai ◇
rules: ◇ (Prism Star) Rule: You can't have more than 1 ◇ card with the same name in your deck. If a ◇ card would go to the discard pile, put it in the Lost Zone instead.

abilities.name: Nightmare Star
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may attach 2 Darkness Energy cards from your hand to it.

attacks.name: Abyssal Sleep
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep. Your opponent flips 2 coins instead of 1 between turns. If either of them is tails, that Pokémon is still Asleep.

flavorText: It can lull people to sleep and make them dream. It is active during nights of the new moon.

name: Blacksmith
rules: Attach 2 Fire Energy cards from your discard pile to 1 of your Fire Pokémon.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Vaporeon

abilities.name: Watery Shroud
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).

attacks.name: Hydro Pump
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each Water Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: When Vaporeon's fins begin to vibrate, it is a sign that rain will come within a few hours.

name: Treecko
flavorText: The soles of its feet are covered by countless tiny spikes, enabling it to walk on walls and ceilings.

attacks.name: Double Stab
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.text: Rhyhorn does 10 damage to itself.

name: Jigglypuff

attacks.name: Lullaby
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: When its huge eyes light up, it sings a mysteriously soothing melody that lulls its enemies to sleep.

name: Dual Ball
rules: Flip 2 coins. For each heads, search your deck for a Basic Pokémon, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. If you do, shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Stunky

attacks.name: Acid Spray
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: It sprays a foul fluid from its rear. Its stench spreads over a mile radius, driving Pokémon away.

name: Pikachu

attacks.name: Speed Bolt
attacks.text: If Pikachu evolved from Pichu during this turn, prevent all effects of an attack, including damage, done to Pikachu during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It lives in forests with others. It stores electricity in the pouches on its cheeks.

name: Lanette's Net Search
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.
Search your deck for up to 3 different types of Basic Pokémon cards (excluding Baby Pokémon), show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Nidorino

attacks.name: Toxic
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 2 damage counters instead of 1 on the Defending Pokémon between turns.

attacks.name: Frustration
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon that doesn't have any damage counters on it. This attack does 40 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It has a violent disposition and stabs foes with its horn, which oozes poison upon impact.

attacks.name: Hypnoblast
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

name: Baltoy

attacks.name: Psy Bolt
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It moves by spinning on its foot. It is a rare Pokémon that was discovered in ancient ruins.

name: Rainbow Energy
rules: Attach Rainbow Energy to 1 of your Pokémon. While in play, Rainbow Energy provides every type of Energy but provides only 1 Energy at a time. (Has no effect other than providing Energy.) When you attach this card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon, put 1 damage counter on that Pokémon.

attacks.name: Plasma
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, search your discard pile for a Lightning Energy card and attach it to Pikachu.

name: Weedle

attacks.name: Spike Stab
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. If tails, this attack does nothing (not even damage).

flavorText: Its poison stinger is very powerful. Its bright-colored body is intended to warn off its enemies.

name: Judge
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.
Each player shuffles his or her hand into his or her deck and draws 4 cards.

name: Super Energy Removal
rules: Discard 1 Energy card attached to 1 of your Pokémon in order to choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon and up to 2 Energy cards attached to it. Discard those Energy cards.

name: Gabite

attacks.name: Sand Tomb
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: As it digs to expand its nest, it habitually digs up gems that it then hoards in its nest.

name: Illumise

abilities.name: Scent Conduct
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a Grass Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward. This power can't be used if Illumise is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Firefly Scent
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon has any Poké-Bodies, that Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: With its sweet aroma, it guides VOLBEAT to draw signs with light in the night sky.

name: Professor Juniper
rules: Discard your hand and draw 7 cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Diancie

attacks.name: Sparkle
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Diamond Storm
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from each of your Fairy Pokémon.

flavorText: A sudden transformation of Carbink, its pink, glimmering body is said to be the loveliest sight in the whole world.

name: Yveltal-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Evil Ball
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage times the amount of Energy attached to both Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Y Cyclone
attacks.text: Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

name: Ultra Ball
rules: Discard 2 cards from your hand. If you do, search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Riolu

attacks.name: Feint
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: They communicate with one another using their auras made tangible by their emotions.

name: Aron

attacks.name: Steel Tackle
attacks.text: Aron does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: It usually lives deep in mountains. However, hunger may drive it to eat railroad tracks and cars.

attacks.name: Teary Eyes
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to Teddiursa by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

abilities.name: Dense
abilities.text: Any damage done to Slowpoke by attacks from your opponent's Evolved Pokémon is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Attract
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon and switch the Defending Pokémon with it.

name: Lysandre
rules: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with his or her Active Pokémon.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Kricketot

attacks.name: Lullaby
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Its legs are short. Whenever it stumbles, its stiff antennae clack with a xylophone-like sound.

name: Golurk

attacks.name: Hammer Arm
attacks.text: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Hurricane Punch
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It is said that Golurk were ordered to protect people and Pokémon by the ancient people who made them.

name: Exeggcute

attacks.name: Leech Seed
attacks.text: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Six of them form a single Pokémon. Should one of the six be lost, the next morning there will once more be six.

name: Team Aqua Technical Machine 01
rules: Attach this card to 1 of your Pokémon that has Team Aqua in its name. That Pokémon may use this card's attack instead of its own. At the end of your turn, discard Team Aqua Technical Machine 01.

attacks.name: Miracle
attacks.text: Choose 1 Special Condition. The Defending Pokémon is now affected by that Special Condition.

name: Rayquaza-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Intensifying Burn
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon-EX, this attack does 50 more damage.

attacks.name: Dragon Pulse
attacks.text: Discard the top 3 cards of your deck.

name: Sandslash

attacks.name: Sand-Attack
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Earthquake
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: The spikes on its body are made up of its hardened hide. It rolls up and attacks foes with its spikes.

name: Sandygast

attacks.name: Shore Up
attacks.text: Attach a Fighting Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Sand Tomb
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It takes control of anyone who puts a hand in its mouth. And so it adds to the accumulation of its sand-mound body.

name: Ninjask

attacks.name: Circling Dive
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Switch Ninjask with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Parallel Drain
attacks.text: Remove from 1 of your Pokémon the number of damage counters equal to the damage you did to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: Because it moves so quickly, it sometimes becomes unseeable. It congregates around tree sap.

name: Shining Ho-Oh

abilities.name: Golden Wing
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon and is Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack, move up to 2 basic Energy cards from this Pokémon to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Fire Blast
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: A legend says that its body glows in seven colors. A rainbow is said to form behind it when it flies.

name: Gastly

attacks.name: Smog
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It's said that gas emanating from a graveyard was possessed by the grievances of the deceased and thus became a Pokémon.

name: Dratini
flavorText: Long considered a mythical Pokémon until recently, when a small colony was found living underwater.

name: White Kyurem-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Shred
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Raging Blade
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any damage counters on it, this attack does 80 more damage.

attacks.name: Dragon Nova-GX
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned and Paralyzed. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Gallade

attacks.name: Nerve Shot
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Pandemonium Blade
attacks.text: Does 20 more damage for each of your Benched Pokémon that has any damage counters on it.

flavorText: When trying to protect someone, it extends its elbows as if they were swords and fights savagely.

name: Drapion

attacks.name: Venoshock
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 40 more damage.

attacks.name: Venomous Fang
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It takes great pride in its strength. Even though it can tear foes apart, it finishes them off with powerful poison.

name: Natu

attacks.name: Future Sight
attacks.text: Look at the top 4 cards of either player's deck and put them back in any order.

flavorText: Although it still can't fly, its jumping power is outstanding. It jumps way up into trees and plucks the buds from the branches.

name: Hydreigon

attacks.name: Tractorbeam
attacks.text: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with the Defending Pokémon. This attack does 40 damage to the new Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Obsidian Fang
attacks.text: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tool cards attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: It responds to movement by attacking. This scary, three-headed Pokémon devours everything in its path!

name: Ruins of Alph
rules: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.
Each Pokémon in play has no Resistance.

name: Crabominable

attacks.name: Gutsy Hammer
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself for each damage counter on it.

attacks.name: Double Stomp
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: It aimed for the top but got lost and ended up on a snowy mountain. Being forced to endure the cold, this Pokémon evolved and grew fur.

name: Pancham

attacks.name: Arm Thrust
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: There's no point to the leaf in its mouth, aside from an effort to look cool. It's mischievous, so it's not well suited to inexperienced Trainers.

name: Klang

attacks.name: Bind
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Gear Grind
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 60 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: By changing the direction in which it rotates, it communicates its feelings to others. When angry, it rotates faster.

attacks.name: Water Gun
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage for each Water Energy attached to Starmie but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. You can't add more than 40 damage in this way.

attacks.name: Core Blast
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage plus 20 more damage for each Special Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

name: Morpeko

attacks.name: Attack the Wound
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 50 more damage.

flavorText: As it eats the seeds stored up in its pocket-like pouches, this Pokémon is not just satisfying its constant hunger. It's also generating electricity.

name: Ferroseed

attacks.name: Pin Missile
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: They stick their spikes into cave walls and absorb the minerals they find in the rock.

name: Combee

attacks.name: Soliciting Nectar
attacks.text: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with 1 of the Defending Pokémon. The new Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: A Pokémon formed by three others. It busily carries sweet floral honey to VESPIQUEN.

attacks.name: Team Assembly
attacks.text: Search your deck for Omanyte, Kabuto, or any Basic Pokémon and put as many of them as you like onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward. Treat the new Benched Pokémon as Basic Pokémon.

attacks.name: Bind
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Hypnotic Gaze
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

name: Zygarde

attacks.name: Glare
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Calm Strike
attacks.text: If you have used your GX attack, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: It's thought to be monitoring the ecosystem. There are rumors that even greater power lies hidden within it.

name: Lycanroc-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Bloodthirsty Eyes
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Dangerous Rogue-GX
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Max Revive
rules: Put a Pokémon from your discard pile on top of your deck.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.

attacks.name: Thunder Wave
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage.

name: Blastoise Spirit Link
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.
Your turn does not end if the Pokémon this card is attached to becomes M Blastoise-EX.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Zoroark

attacks.name: Brutal Bash
attacks.text: Does 20 damage times the number of Darkness Pokémon you have in play.

attacks.name: Dark Rush
attacks.text: Does 20 damage times the number of damage counters on this Pokémon.

flavorText: Bonds between these Pokémon are very strong. It protects the safety of its pack by tricking its opponents.

name: Morpeko

attacks.name: Torment
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks. During your opponent's next turn, that Pokémon can't use that attack.

attacks.name: Spark
attacks.text: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: As it eats the seeds stored up in its pocket-like pouches, this Pokémon is not just satisfying its constant hunger. It's also generating electricity.

attacks.name: Sharp Stinger
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned; if tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Porygon
flavorText: This Pokémon was created by human computer engineering. Its program is only capable of simple action and reaction.

name: Shuppet

attacks.name: Disable
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon's attacks. That Pokémon can't use that attack during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Haunt
attacks.text: Put 1 damage counter on the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: It uses its horn to feed on envy and malice, or so it's said. It's very active at night.

name: Guzzlord-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Eat Sloppily
attacks.text: Discard the top 5 cards of your deck. If any of those cards are Energy cards, attach them to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Glutton-GX
attacks.text: If your opponent's Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from this attack, take 2 more Prize cards. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Unown [J]

abilities.name: JUNK
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you put Unown J from your hand onto your Bench, you may flip a coin. If heads, search your discard pile for a Trainer card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand.

attacks.name: Hidden Power
attacks.text: Remove the Special Condition Asleep from the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: Shaped like an ancient writing, it is a huge mystery whether language or Unown came first.

name: Staryu
flavorText: At night, the center of its body slowly flickers with the same rhythm as a human heartbeat.

name: Lanturn

attacks.name: Salvage
attacks.text: Shuffle 4 Item cards from your discard pile into your deck.

attacks.name: Signal Beam
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: This Pokémon flashes a bright light that blinds its prey. This creates an opening for it to deliver an electrical attack.

name: Medicham

attacks.name: Pure Power
attacks.text: Put 4 damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Windmill Kick
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Through yoga training, it gained the psychic power to predict its foe's next move.

attacks.name: Jump On
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage.

abilities.name: Baby Evolution
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put Wobbuffet from your hand onto Wynaut (this counts as evolving Wynaut) and remove all damage counters from Wynaut.

attacks.name: Flail
attacks.text: Does 10 damage times the number of damage counters on Wynaut.

name: Elgyem

attacks.name: Psych Up
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Psych Up attack does 20 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Rumors of its origin are linked to a UFO crash site in the desert 50 years ago.

name: Sentret

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: A very cautious Pokémon, it raises itself up using its tail to get a better view of its surroundings.

name: Swoobat

abilities.name: Unaware
abilities.text: Prevent all effects of your opponent's attacks, except damage, done to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Emitting powerful sound waves tires it out. Afterward, it won't be able to fly for a little while.

name: Stunky

attacks.name: Poison Gas
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It sprays a nose-curling, stinky fluid from its rear to repel attackers.

name: Nuzleaf

attacks.name: Blind
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Feint Attack
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on that Pokémon.

flavorText: The sound of its grass flute makes its listeners uneasy. It lives deep in forests.

attacks.name: Charge
attacks.text: Take 1 Lightning Energy card from your discard pile and attach it to Lt. Surge's Pikachu.

attacks.name: Lightning Tail
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Escavalier

attacks.name: Fury Attack
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.

attacks.name: Iron Tackle
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: Wearing the shell covering they stole from Shelmet, they defend themselves and attack with two lances.

name: Blacksmith
rules: Attach 2 Fire Energy cards from your discard pile to 1 of your Fire Pokémon.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Sobble

attacks.name: Growl
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon's attacks do 20 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: When scared, this Pokémon cries. Its tears pack the chemical punch of 100 onions, and attacks won't be able to resist weeping.

name: Claydol

attacks.name: Psy Bolt
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Miraculous Spin
attacks.text: This attack does 40 damage for each Steven's Resolve card in your discard pile.

flavorText: It is said that it originates from clay dolls made by an ancient civilization.

name: Drowzee

abilities.name: Long-Distance Hypnosis
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep; if tails, your Active Pokémon is now Asleep. The power can't be used if Drowzee is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Nightmare
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Not popular with girls because of its attitude. Can often be heard muttering to itself in a low voice.

name: Brock's Grit
rules: Shuffle 6 in any combination of Pokémon and basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Xerneas

attacks.name: Geomancy
attacks.text: Choose 2 of your Benched Pokémon. For each of those Pokémon, search your deck for a Fairy Energy card and attach it to that Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Rainbow Spear
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Legends say it can share eternal life. It slept for a thousand years in the form of a tree before its revival.

name: Defender
rules: Attach Defender to 1 of your Pokémon. At the end of your opponent's next turn, discard Defender. Damage done to that Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

name: Mareep

abilities.name: Fluffy Pillow
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, you may leave your opponent's Active Pokémon Asleep.

flavorText: It stores lots of air in its soft fur, allowing it to stay cool in summer and warm in winter.

name: Dragon Talon
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.
If the Dragon Pokémon this card is attached to is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent's attack (even if that Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 3 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Exeggutor

attacks.name: Mind Bend
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Full Clean
attacks.text: Discard your hand.

flavorText: It engages its enemies using psychic powers. Each of its three heads fires off psychokinetic energy, tripling its power.

name: Kangaskhan
flavorText: If it is safe, the young gets out of the belly pouch to play. The adult keeps a close eye on the youngster.

abilities.name: Mirror Coat
abilities.text: If Wobbuffet becomes Poisoned or Burned by the Defending Pokémon's attack during your opponent's turn, the Defending Pokémon becomes affected by the same Special Condition.

attacks.name: Return Attack
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. If either is heads, this attack does 10 damage times the number of damage counters on Wobbuffet.

name: Interviewer's Questions
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.
Look at the top 8 cards of your deck. Choose as many Energy cards as you like, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

name: Wingull

attacks.name: Slashing Strike
attacks.text: During your next turn, Wingull can't use Slashing Strike.

flavorText: It soars high in the sky, riding on updrafts like a glider. It carries food tucked in its bill.

attacks.name: Spiral Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

name: Digger
rules: Flip a coin. If tails, do 10 damage to your Active Pokémon. If heads, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, your opponent does 10 damage to his or her Active Pokémon. If heads, you flip a coin. Keep doing this until a player gets tails.

name: Clefairy

attacks.name: Shining Fingers
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It is said that happiness will come to those who see a gathering of Clefairy dancing under a full moon.

name: Special Delivery Pikachu

attacks.name: Happy Delivery
attacks.text: Search your deck for an Item card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Pikachu that can generate powerful electricity have cheek sacs that are extra soft and super stretchy.

attacks.name: Steel Headbutt
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 10 more damage.

name: Zygarde-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Cell Connector
attacks.text: Attach 2 Fighting Energy cards from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Verdict-GX
attacks.text: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Pokémon-GX and Pokémon-EX during your opponent's next turn. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Shuckle

attacks.name: Withdraw
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to Shuckle during your opponent's next turn. (Any other effects of attacks still happen.)

attacks.name: Wrap
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: The Berries it stores in its vaselike shell decompose and become a gooey liquid.

attacks.name: Mysterious Powder
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Sleepy Ball
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Singe
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Heat Tackle
attacks.text: Slugma does 10 damage to itself.

name: Sunkern

attacks.name: Cure Kernels
attacks.text: Remove 2 damage counters from 1 of your Pokémon.

flavorText: It may plummet from the sky. If attacked by a Spearow, it will violently shake its leaves.

name: Jigglypuff

attacks.name: Hypnotic Gaze
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Expand
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to Jigglypuff by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: When it wavers its big, round eyes, it begins singing a lullaby that makes everyone drowsy.

name: Medicham

ancientTrait.name: Ω Barrage
ancientTrait.text: This Pokémon may attack twice a turn. (If the first attack Knocks Out your opponent's Active Pokémon, you may attack again after your opponent chooses a new Active Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Calm Mind
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Yoga Kick
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

flavorText: Through yoga training, it gained the psychic power to predict its foe's next move.

name: Haunter

attacks.name: Sinister Fog
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 1 damage counter on each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: On moonless nights, Haunter searches for someone to curse, so it's best not to go out walking around.

name: Scizor-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Danger Perception
abilities.text: If this Pokémon's remaining HP is 100 or less, its attacks do 80 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Steel Wing
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Cross-Cut-GX
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is an Evolution Pokémon, this attack does 100 more damage. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Ultra Ball
rules: Discard 2 cards from your hand. If you do, search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Beheeyem

attacks.name: Mind Bullet
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon times the amount of Energy attached to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Psybeam
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: It uses psychic power to control an opponent's brain and tamper with its memories.

name: Crystal Beach
rules: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.
Each Special Energy card that provides 2 or more Energy (both yours and your opponent's) now provides only 1 Colorless Energy. This isn't affected by any Poké-Powers or Poké-Bodies.

name: Duskull

attacks.name: Disable
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon's attacks. That Pokémon can't use that attack during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Night Shade
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It doggedly pursues its prey wherever it goes. However, the chase is abandoned at sunrise.

name: Cottonee
flavorText: It shoots cotton from its body to protect itself. If it gets caught up in hurricane-strength winds, it can get sent to the other side of the Earth.

name: Kartana-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Slice Off
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may discard a Special Energy from 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Gale Blade
attacks.text: You may shuffle this Pokémon and all cards attached to it into your deck.

attacks.name: Blade-GX
attacks.text: Take a Prize card. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

attacks.name: Rapid Spin
attacks.text: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon, if any. You switch Staryu with 1 of your Benched Pokémon, if any.

name: Steelix

attacks.name: Metal Defender
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon has no Weakness.

flavorText: The iron it ingested with the soil it swallowed transformed its body and made it harder than diamonds.

name: Sage's Training
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.
Look at the top 5 cards of your deck. Choose any 2 cards you find there and put them into your hand. Discard the other cards.
