name: Fiery Torch
rules: Discard a Fire Energy card from your hand. (If you can't discard a Fire Energy card, you can't play this card.) Draw 2 cards.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Life Herb
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 of your Pokémon, and remove all Special Conditions and 6 damage counters from that Pokémon (all if there are less than 6).

name: Xatu

attacks.name: Creepy Wind
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Life Drain
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, put damage counters on your opponent's Active Pokémon until its remaining HP is 10.

flavorText: It's said that while this Pokémon has the power to predict the future, it's not powerful enough to change the future it sees.

attacks.name: Splatter
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

name: Skuntank

attacks.name: Smogscreen
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

flavorText: It attacks by spraying a horribly smelly fluid from the tip of its tail. Attacks from above confound it.

name: Ponyta

attacks.name: Agility
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of an attack, including damage, done to Ponyta during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: About an hour after birth, its fiery mane and tail grow out, giving it an impressive appearance.

name: Kommo-o-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Adamantine Press
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Shred
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Ultra Uppercut-GX
attacks.text: (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Rare Candy
rules: Choose 1 of your Basic Pokémon in play. If you have a Stage 2 card in your hand that evolves from that Pokémon, put that card on the Basic Pokémon. (This counts as evolving that Pokémon.) You can't use this card during your first turn or on a Basic Pokémon that was put into play this turn.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

attacks.name: Bind
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Rock Smash
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage.

name: Pidove

attacks.name: Scout
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals his or her hand.

flavorText: These Pokémon live in cities. They are accustomed to people. Flocks often gather in parks and plazas.

name: Bisharp

attacks.name: Fury Cutter
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. If 1 of them is heads, this attack does 10 more damage. If 2 of them are heads, this attack does 30 more damage. If all of them are heads, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: Bisharp pursues prey in the company of a large group of Pawniard. Then Bisharp finishes off the prey.

name: Lycanroc

attacks.name: Dangerous Claws
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Basic Pokémon, this attack does 30 more damage.

attacks.name: Corner
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: The more intimidating the opponent it faces, the more this Pokémon's blood boils. It will attack with no regard for its own safety.

attacks.name: Rage
attacks.text: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Totodile.

name: Gligar

attacks.name: Wicked Jab
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It clamps on to its chosen prey then jabs the stinger on its tail into the prey while it's stunned with surprise.

name: Hoopa-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Scoundrel Ring
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench, you may search your deck for up to 3 Pokémon-EX (except for Hoopa-EX), reveal them, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Hyperspace Fury
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 100 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Olympia
rules: Switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you do, heal 30 damage from the Pokémon you moved to your Bench.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Palossand

abilities.name: Wall of Sand
abilities.text: This Pokémon takes 20 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Absorb Vitality
attacks.text: Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Buried beneath the castle are masses of dried-up bones from those whose vitality it has drained.

name: Ferroseed
flavorText: When threatened, it attacks by shooting a barrage of spikes, which gives it a chance to escape by rolling away.

name: Turtwig
flavorText: It undertakes photosynthesis with its body, making oxygen. The leaf on its head wilts if it is thirsty.

abilities.name: Submerge
abilities.text: As long as Quagsire GL is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to Quagsire GL by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).

attacks.name: Punch and Run
attacks.text: You may switch Quagsire GL with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

name: Lucario
flavorText: A well-trained one can sense auras to identify and take in the feelings of creatures over half a mile away.

name: Lum Berry
rules: Attach Lum Berry to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it. If that Pokémon is Knocked Out, discard this card.
At any time between turns, if the Pokémon this card is attached to is affected by any Special Conditions, remove all of them. Then, discard Lum Berry.

attacks.name: Growth Spurt
attacks.text: Attach a Grass Energy card from your hand to Seedot.

attacks.name: Trip Over
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.

name: Paint Roller
rules: Discard any Stadium card in play. Then, draw a card.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Mudbray

attacks.name: Double Kick
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.

flavorText: The mud stuck to Mudbray's hooves enhances its grip and its powerful running gait.

name: Klinklang

abilities.name: Shift Gear
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may move a Metal Energy attached to 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Gear Grind
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 80 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Its red core functions as an energy tank. It fires the charged energy through its spikes into an area.

name: Skorupi

attacks.name: Paralyzing Clamp
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It burrows under the sand to lie in wait for prey. Its tail claws can inject its prey with a savage poison.

name: Numel

attacks.name: Draw In
attacks.text: Search your discard pile for up to 2 Fire Energy cards and attach them to Numel.

flavorText: Its humped back stores intensely hot magma. In rain, the magma cools, slowing its movement.

name: Barboach

attacks.name: Mud Spit
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It coats its entire body with a slimy fluid so it can squirm and slip away if grabbed.

name: Machop
flavorText: Loves to build its muscles. It trains in all styles of martial arts to become even stronger.

name: Morpeko V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Spark
attacks.text: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Electro Wheel
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, switch it with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

name: Wooper

attacks.name: Slime
attacks.text: If an attack would do damage to Wooper during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, prevent all damage to Wooper from that attack. (Any other effects of that attack happen.)

flavorText: When it walks around on the ground, it coats its body with a slimy, poisonous film.

attacks.name: Focus Energy
attacks.text: During your next turn, Lt. Surge's Rattata's Gnaw attack's base damage is doubled.

name: Lycanroc-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Twilight Eyes
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may discard an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Splintered Shards-GX
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage for each Energy card in your opponent's discard pile. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

attacks.name: Group Attack
attacks.text: Does 10 damage times the number of Koga's Zubats you have in play. Before doing damage, you may search your deck for any number of Basic Pokémon named Koga's Zubat and put them onto your Bench. (You can't get more cards with this attack than you have room on your Bench.) If you do, shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Gothita

attacks.name: Fortunate Eye
attacks.text: Look at the top 5 cards of your opponent's deck and put them back in any order.

flavorText: Though they're still only babies, there's psychic power stored in their ribbonlike feelers, and sometimes they use that power to fight.

name: Unown [R]

abilities.name: RETIRE
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if Unown R is on your Bench, you may discard Unown R and all cards attached to it. (This doesn't count as a Knocked Out Pokémon.) Then, draw a card.

attacks.name: Hidden Power
attacks.text: Move any number of basic Energy cards attached to your Pokémon to your other Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: Shaped like an ancient writing, it is a huge mystery whether language or Unown came first.

attacks.name: Bind
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Rage
attacks.text: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Onix.

name: Corsola

attacks.name: Rally
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 different types of Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: Many live in the clean seas of the south. They apparently can't live in polluted waters.

name: Scraggy
flavorText: Its skin has a rubbery elasticity, so it can reduce damage by defensively pulling it skin up to its neck.

name: M Rayquaza-EX
rules: Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

ancientTrait.name: Δ Evolution
ancientTrait.text: You may play this card from your hand to evolve a Pokémon during your first turn or the turn you play that Pokémon.

attacks.name: Emerald Break
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage times the number of your Benched Pokémon.

name: Deino

attacks.name: Gnaw Off
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.

flavorText: They cannot see, so they tackle and bite to learn about their surroundings. Their bodies are covered in wounds.

name: Garchomp

attacks.name: Turbo Assault
attacks.text: Attach an Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Bite Off
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon-EX, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: It flies at speeds equals to a jet fighter plane. It never allows its prey to escape.

name: Psyduck

attacks.name: Migraine
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused. If tails, Psyduck is now Confused.

flavorText: Although possessed of great mental powers, it doesn't know how to use them.

name: Meloetta

attacks.name: Sing
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Its melodies are sung with a special vocalization method that can control the feelings of those who hear it.

name: Team Magma Ball
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a Pokémon that has Team Magma in its name, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. If tails, search your deck for a Basic Pokémon that has Team Magma in its name, show it to your opponent and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Link Blast
attacks.text: If Ralts and the Defending Pokémon have a different amount of Energy attached to them, this attack's base damage is 10 instead of 40.

name: Lapras

attacks.name: Grind
attacks.text: Does 10 damage times the amount of Energy attached to Lapras.

attacks.name: Ice Beam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It loves crossing the sea with people and Pokémon on its back. It understands human speech.

name: Terrakion

attacks.name: Retaliate
attacks.text: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack during his or her last turn, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: Its charge is strong enough to break through a giant castle wall in one blow. This Pokémon is spoken of in legends.

name: Excadrill-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Dig Out
attacks.text: Discard the top card of your deck. If that card is a basic Energy card, attach it to 1 of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Break Ground
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Alolan Diglett

attacks.name: Iron Head
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Its golden hairs function as sensors. It pokes them out of its burrow to monitor its surroundings.

name: Skarmory-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Joust
attacks.text: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tool cards attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Tailspin Piledriver
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 40 more damage.

name: Battle Frontier
rules: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.
Each player's Colorless Evolved Pokémon, Darkness Evolved Pokémon, and Metal Evolved Pokémon can't use any Poké-Powers or Poké-Bodies.

name: React Energy
rules: React Energy provides Colorless Energy.

attacks.name: Pickup Power
attacks.text: Search your discard pile for an Energy card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand.

name: Totodile
flavorText: Its powerful, well-developed jaws are capable of crushing anything. Even its trainer must be careful.

name: Crystal Shard
rules: Attach Crystal Shard to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it. If that Pokémon is Knocked Out, discard this card.
As long as Crystal Shard is attached to a Pokémon, that Pokémon's type is Colorless. If that Pokémon attacks, discard this card at the end of the turn.

name: Stunfisk

attacks.name: Muddy Water
attacks.text: Does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon).

attacks.name: Rumble
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It conceals itself in the mud of the seashore. Then it waits. When prey touch it, it delivers a jolt of electricity.

name: Alolan Marowak

attacks.name: Spirit Smash
attacks.text: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck. If the card you discarded is a Pokémon, this attack does damage equal to that Pokémon's HP to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: The rich greenery of the Alola region is hard on Marowak. It controls fire to stay alive.

name: Unown [U]

abilities.name: UNSEEN
abilities.text: As long as Unown U is on your Bench, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done by your opponent's Pokémon to any Unown on your Bench.

attacks.name: Hidden Power
attacks.text: Search your deck for any 1 card and discard it. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: Shaped like an ancient writing, it is a huge mystery whether language or Unown came first.

name: Latios-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Fast Raid
attacks.text: If you go first, you can use this attack on your first turn.

attacks.name: Light Pulse
attacks.text: Prevent all effects of your opponent's attacks, except damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

name: Poké Ball
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Trapinch
flavorText: It makes an inescapable conical pit and lies in wait at the bottom for prey to come tumbling down.

name: Pinsir

attacks.name: Gripthrow
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent returns the Defending Pokémon and all cards attached to it to his or her hand.

attacks.name: Sever
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is a Stage 2 Pokémon, this attack does 50 damage plus 30 more damage.

flavorText: It grips prey with its pincers until the prey is torn in half. What it can't tear, it tosses far.

name: Landorus

attacks.name: Abundant Harvest
attacks.text: Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Gaia Hammer
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's). (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Lands visited by Landorus grant such bountiful crops that it has been hailed as "The Guardian of the Fields."

name: Lycanroc
flavorText: Its quick movements confuse its enemies. Well equipped with claws and fangs, it also uses the sharp rocks in its mane as weapons.

name: Wingull
flavorText: It soars high in the sky, riding on updrafts like a glider. It carries food tucked in its bill.

name: Patrat
flavorText: Using food stored in cheek pouches, they can keep watches for days. They use their tails to communicate with others.

name: Charizard Spirit Link
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.
Your turn does not end if the Pokémon this card is attached to becomes M Charizard-EX.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Xerneas BREAK
rules: BREAK Evolution Rule: Xerneas BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

attacks.name: Life Stream
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage times the amount of Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.

name: Aron

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: It usually lives deep in mountains. However, hunger may drive it to eat railroad tracks and cars.

name: Vullaby

attacks.name: Whirlwind
attacks.text: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Its wings are too tiny to allow it to fly. As the time approaches for it to evolve, it discards the bones it was wearing.

attacks.name: Bind Wound
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, remove 2 damage counters from 1 of your Pokémon (1 if it has only 1).

attacks.name: Dogpile
attacks.text: Count the number of Pokémon on your Bench. This attack does 10 times that number of damage to the Defending Pokémon, and Chansey does 10 times that number of damage to itself.

name: Magnemite

attacks.name: Thunder Wave
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Selfdestruct
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to each Pokémon on each player's Bench. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Magnemite does 40 damage to itself.

flavorText: Uses anti-gravity to stay suspended. Appears without warning and uses attacks like Thunder Wave.

name: Drake's Stadium
rules: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.
Any damage done to Colorless Active Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's) by an opponent's attack is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

name: Zweilous

attacks.name: Slam
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: The two heads do not get along. Whichever head eats more than the other gets to be the leader.

name: Vulpix

attacks.name: Singe
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Ember
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, discard a Fire Energy attached to Vulpix.

flavorText: If it is attacked by an enemy that is stronger than itself, it feigns injury to fool the enemy and escapes.

name: Double Colorless Energy
rules: Double Colorless Energy provides ColorlessColorless Energy.

name: Minccino

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: These Pokémon prefer a tidy habitat. They are always sweeping and dusting, using their tails as brooms.

name: Mudsdale

attacks.name: Enhanced Stomp
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool card attached to it, this attack does 60 more damage.

attacks.name: High Horsepower
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 40 damage to itself.

flavorText: It spits a mud that provides resistance to both wind and rain, so the walls of old houses were often coated with it.

name: Alolan Rattata
flavorText: When the sun goes down, it becomes active. It runs around town on a chase for good food for the boss of its nest—Raticate.

attacks.name: Stomp
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage.

name: Slowpoke

attacks.name: Psyshock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Water Gun
attacks.text: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each Water Energy attached to Slowpoke but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. You can't add more than 20 damage in this way.

flavorText: It lazes vacantly near water. If something bites its tail, it won't even notice for a whole day.

name: Diglett

attacks.name: Down Draw
attacks.text: Draw a card from the bottom of your deck.

flavorText: A Pokémon that lives underground. Because of its dark habitat, it is repelled by bright sunlight.

name: Energy Retrieval
rules: Trade 1 of the other cards in your hand for up to 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile.

name: Fossil Egg
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a card that evolves from Mysterious Fossil and put it onto your Bench or put a card that evolves from Mysterious Fossil from your hand onto your Bench. Either way, treat the new card as a Basic Pokémon. If you searched your deck, shuffle it. (You can't play this card if your Bench is full.)

attacks.name: Agility
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of an attack, including damage, done to Surskit during your opponent's next turn.

name: Lost Remover
rules: Put 1 Special Energy card attached to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon in the Lost Zone.

name: Morpeko VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Max Discharge
attacks.text: This attack also does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Reversal Trigger
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.
When the Team Plasma Pokémon this card is attached to is Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack, search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Fiery Flint
rules: You can play this card only if you discard 2 other cards from your hand.
Search your deck for up to 4 Fire Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Aerodactyl
rules: Put this card onto your Bench only with the effect of Old Amber Aerodactyl

attacks.name: Jet Draft
attacks.text: Discard a Special Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: This vicious Pokémon is said to have flown in ancient skies while shrieking high-pitched cries.

name: Gothorita

attacks.name: Whiny Voice
attacks.text: Choose a random card from your opponent's hand. Your opponent reveals that card and shuffles it into their deck.

attacks.name: Double Spin
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It's said that when stars shine in the night sky, this Pokémon will spirit away sleeping children. Some call it the Witch of Punishment.

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage.

name: Gliscor

attacks.name: Poison Ring
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Night Slash
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It dances silently through the sky. When it approaches prey, it can land a critical hit in an instant.

name: Alolan Raichu

abilities.name: Surge Surfer
abilities.text: If there is any Stadium card in play, this Pokémon has no Retreat Cost.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage times the amount of Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It only evolves to this form in the Alola region. According to researchers, its diet is one of the causes of this change.

attacks.name: Triple Bone
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads to that Pokémon. Don't apply Weakness and Resistance.

attacks.name: Bone Rush
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.

name: Pancham
flavorText: It does its level best to glare and pull a scary face, but it can't help grinning if anyone pats its head.

name: Team Magma Belt
rules: Attach Team Magma Belt to 1 of your Pokémon with Team Magma in its name that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it. If that Pokémon is Knocked Out, discard this card.

At any time between turns, if the Pokémon Team Magma Belt is attached to is your Active Pokémon, search your deck for a card that evolves from that Pokémon and put it on that Pokémon. (This counts as evolving that Pokémon.) Shuffle your deck afterward, then discard Team Magma Belt.

name: Alolan Grimer

attacks.name: Chemical Breath
attacks.text: This attack does 50 more damage for each Special Condition affecting your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: The crystals on Grimer's body are lumps of toxins. If one falls off, lethal poisons leak out.

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

attacks.name: Rising Lunge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage.

name: Roselia

attacks.name: Petal Spikes
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep and Poisoned. If Budew is anywhere under Roselia, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep and Poisoned.

flavorText: Roselia raised on clean drinking water are known to grow vividly colored flowers.

name: Mr. Stone's Project
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Search your deck for up to 2 Basic cards, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward. Or, search your discard pile for up to 2 Basic cards, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand.

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage; if tails, this attack does 10 damage.

attacks.name: Shadow Crush
attacks.text: You may discard 1 Psychic Energy card attached to Shuppet. If you do, your opponent discards 1 Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

name: Shaymin-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Set Up
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench, you may draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.

attacks.name: Sky Return
attacks.text: Return this Pokémon and all cards attached to it to your hand.

name: Mawile

attacks.name: Astonish
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, choose a random card from your opponent's hand. Your opponent reveals that card and shuffles it into his or her deck.

attacks.name: Big Ol' Bite
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon. The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: Attached to its head is a huge set of jaws formed by horns. It can chew through iron beams.

name: Lickitung

attacks.name: Stretch Tongue
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

attacks.name: Knock Off
attacks.text: Choose 1 card from your opponent's hand without looking and discard it.

flavorText: When it extends its over-six-foot-long tongue, its tail quivers. There is a possibility they are connected.

name: Eevee

attacks.name: Tail Wag
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can't attack Eevee during your opponent's next turn. (Benching either Pokémon ends this effect.)

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage; if tails, this attack does 10 damage.

flavorText: Its genetic code is irregular. It may mutate if it is exposed to radiation from elemental stones.

name: Sealeo

attacks.name: Find Ice
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Water Energy cards, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Sheer Cold
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It habitually spins things on its nose. By doing so, it learns textures and odors.

name: Diancie ◇
rules: ◇ (Prism Star) Rule: You can't have more than 1 ◇ card with the same name in your deck. If a ◇ card would go to the discard pile, put it in the Lost Zone instead.

abilities.name: Princess's Cheers
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, your Fighting Pokémon's attacks do 20 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Diamond Rain
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from each of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It can instantly create many diamonds by compressing the carbon in the air between its hands.

name: Bidoof
rules: Wacan Berry: Any damage done to Bidoof by attacks from Lightning Pokémon is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

name: Sceptile Spirit Link
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.
Your turn does not end if the Pokémon this card is attached to becomes M Sceptile-EX.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

abilities.name: Psychoflow
abilities.text: As long as there is a Psychic Energy card attached to Abra, its Retreat Cost is 0.

name: Imposter Oak's Revenge
rules: Discard a card from your hand in order to play this card. Your opponent shuffles his or her hand into his or her deck, then draws 4 cards.

name: Remoraid

attacks.name: Fury Strikes
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It can shoot a burst of water from its mouth to propel itself backward at high speeds, allowing it to flee from surprised enemies.

name: Lucario

abilities.name: Dual Armor
abilities.text: If this Pokémon has any Metal Energy attached to it, this Pokémon's type is both Fighting and Metal.

attacks.name: Hurricane Kick
attacks.text: Does 30 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken.

flavorText: By reading the auras of all things, it can tell how others are feeling from over half a mile away.

name: Mareep

attacks.name: Thunder Shock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It stores lots of air in its soft fur, allowing it to stay cool in summer and warm in winter.

name: Meloetta

attacks.name: Acrobatics
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: Many famous songs have been inspired by the melodies that Meloetta plays.

name: Spoink

attacks.name: Sleep Pearl
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep. Switch Spoink with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It bounces around on its tail to keep its heart pumping. It carries a pearl from Clamperl on its head.

name: Ralts

attacks.name: Teleport
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: If its horns capture the warm feelings of people or Pokémon, its body warms up slightly.

name: Gengar

abilities.name: Shadow Pain
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may put 6 damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon-GX and Pokémon-EX in any way you like.

attacks.name: Twilight Poison
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep and Poisoned.

flavorText: You can hear tales told all over the world about how Gengar will pay a visit to children who are naughty.

name: Team Rocket's Trickery
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.
Draw 2 cards. Then, your opponent discards a card from his or her hand.

name: Electabuzz

attacks.name: Thunderpunch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 10 more damage. If tails, Electabuzz does 10 damage to itself.

attacks.name: Shock Wave
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed. If Electabuzz is evolved from Elekid, this attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Half of all blackouts occur when this Pokémon appears at power plants and eats electricity.

name: Pawniard

attacks.name: Cut Down
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Blades comprise this Pokémon's entire body. If battling dulls the blades, it sharpens them on stones by the river.

name: Jigglypuff

attacks.name: Double Slap
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It hugely inflates its stomach and sings a mysterious melody. If you hear this melody, you'll become sleepy right away.

name: Ferrothorn

attacks.name: Steel Feelers
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Gyro Ball
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. Then, your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: They attach themselves to cave ceilings, firing steel spikes at targets passing beneath them.

name: Lysandre
rules: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with his or her Active Pokémon.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: PlusPower
rules: Attach PlusPower to 1 of your Pokémon. Discard this card at the end of your turn.
If the Pokémon PlusPower is attached to attacks, the attack does 10 more damage to the Defending Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

name: Regirock

attacks.name: Enhanced Stomp
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool card attached to it, this attack does 20 more damage.

attacks.name: Hammer Arm
attacks.text: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

flavorText: The same rocks that form its body have been found in ground layers around the world.

name: Sandshrew

attacks.name: Double Scratch
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Sand Tomb
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: Disliking water, it lives in deep burrows in arid areas. It can roll itself instantly into a ball.

name: Arcanine ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Fire Remedy
abilities.text: Whenever you attach a Fire Energy from your hand to Arcanine ex, remove 1 damage counter and all Special Conditions from Arcanine ex.

attacks.name: Overrun
attacks.text: Does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Flame Swirl
attacks.text: Discard 2 Fire Energy or 1 React Energy card attached to Arcanine ex.

attacks.name: Paralyzing Gaze
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Whirlwind
attacks.text: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, he or she chooses 1 of them and switches it with the Defending Pokémon. (Do the damage before switching the Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Razor Wind
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

name: Kakuna

attacks.name: Stiffen
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to Kakuna by attacks is reduced by 30 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Spit Poison
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: While awaiting evolution, it hides from predators under leaves and in nooks of branches.

name: Kartana-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Slice Off
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may discard a Special Energy from 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Gale Blade
attacks.text: You may shuffle this Pokémon and all cards attached to it into your deck.

attacks.name: Blade-GX
attacks.text: Take a Prize card. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Alolan Dugtrio

attacks.name: Gold Rush
attacks.text: Discard any number of Metal Energy cards from your hand. This attack does 30 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

flavorText: These Pokémon are cherished in the Alola region, where they are thought to be feminine deities of the land incarnate.

name: Whimsicott

attacks.name: Triple Draw
attacks.text: Draw 3 cards.

attacks.name: Flying Fury
attacks.text: Before doing damage, you may discard any number of Pokémon Tools from your Pokémon. This attack does 40 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.

flavorText: It scatters cotton all over the place as a prank. If it gets wet, it'll become too heavy to move and have no choice but to answer for its mischief.

name: Turtwig
rules: Persim Berry: If Turtwig is Confused, remove the Special Condition Confused from Turtwig at the end of each player's turn.

name: Clefable

abilities.name: Prankish
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may put an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon on top of their deck.

flavorText: A timid fairy Pokémon that is rarely seen, it will run and hide the moment it senses people.

attacks.name: Sleep Powder
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

name: Scrafty

attacks.name: Machine Gun Headbutt
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads. This Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: It pulls up its shed skin to protect itself while it kicks. The bigger the crest, the more respected it is.

name: Tyranitar Spirit Link
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.
Your turn does not end if the Pokémon this card is attached to becomes M Tyranitar-EX.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Salazzle

attacks.name: Nasty Plot
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Severe Poison
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 4 damage counters instead of 1 on that Pokémon between turns.

flavorText: For some reason, only females have been found. It creates a reverse harem of male Salandit that it lives with.

name: Timburr

attacks.name: Knock Back
attacks.text: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Always carrying squared logs, they help out with construction. As they grow, they carry bigger logs.

name: Gothitelle

attacks.name: Mind Bend
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Bend
attacks.text: Choose 2 random cards from your opponent's hand. Your opponent reveals those cards and shuffles them into their deck.

flavorText: It has tremendous psychic power, but it dislikes conflict. It's also able to predict the future based on the movement of the stars.

attacks.name: Confusion Wave
attacks.text: Both Psyduck and the Defending Pokémon are now Confused.

name: Pokémon Center Lady
rules: Heal 60 damage and remove all Special Conditions from 1 of your Pokémon.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

name: Rockruff

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: This Pokémon has lived with people since times long ago. It can sense when its Trainer is in the dumps and will stick close by its Trainer's side.

name: Team Magma Conspirator
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Search your deck for up to 2 in any combination of Basic Pokémon with Team Magma in its name and basic Energy cards, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Roar
attacks.text: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon, if any.

attacks.name: Ambush
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage.

name: Nightly Garbage Run
rules: Choose up to 3 Basic Pokémon cards, Evolution cards, and/or basic Energy cards from your discard pile. Show them to your opponent and shuffle them into your deck.

name: Patrat

attacks.name: Hyper Fang
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Extremely cautious, they take shifts to maintain a constant watch of their nest. They feel insecure without a lookout.

name: Koffing

attacks.name: Smogscreen
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

flavorText: Lighter-than-air gases in its body keep it aloft. The gases not only smell, they are also explosive.

name: Rayquaza-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Dragon Strike
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon can't use Dragon Strike during your next turn.

name: Groudon

attacks.name: Wreck
attacks.text: If there is any Stadium card in play, this attack does 50 more damage. Then, discard that Stadium card.

attacks.name: Ground Slash
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: This legendary Pokémon is said to represent the land. It went to sleep after dueling Kyogre.

name: Unown [V]

abilities.name: VACATION
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may remove 2 damage counters from each of your Pokémon. If you do, your turn ends. This power can't be used if Unown V is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Hidden Power
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Shaped like an ancient writing, it is a huge mystery whether language or Unown came first.

name: Pawniard

attacks.name: Iron Head
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: They fight at Bisharp's command. They cling to their prey and inflict damage by sinking their blades into it.

name: Lost World
rules: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.
Once during each player's turn, if that player's opponent has 6 or more Pokémon in the Lost Zone, the player may choose to win the game.

name: Bisharp

attacks.name: Metal Sound
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Metal Wallop
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Metal Wallop attack does 40 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: This pitiless Pokémon commands a group of Pawniard to hound prey into immobility. It then moves in to finish the prey off.

name: Root Fossil Lileep
rules: Look at the bottom 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal a Lileep you find there and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Duskull
rules: Reaper Cloth: Duskull can evolve during the turn you play it.

attacks.name: Astonish
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 card from your opponent's hand without looking. Look at that card you chose, then have your opponent shuffle that card into his or her deck.

name: Lairon

attacks.name: Wreak Havoc
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

flavorText: For food, it digs up iron ore. It smashes it steely body against others to fight over territory.

name: Skorupi

attacks.name: Stalk
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Dangerous Claw
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed. If tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It grips prey with its tail claws and injects poison. It tenaciously hangs on until the poison takes.

attacks.name: Plus Energy
attacks.text: Attach a basic Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon.

name: Cinccino

abilities.name: Smooth Coat
abilities.text: If any damage is done to this Pokémon by attacks, flip a coin. If heads, prevent that damage.

attacks.name: Echoed Voice
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Echoed Voice attack does 50 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Their white fur is coated in a special oil that makes it easy for them to deflect attacks.

name: Pangoro

attacks.name: Clobber
attacks.text: You may discard an Item card from your hand. If you do, this attack does 40 more damage.

attacks.name: Hammer Arm
attacks.text: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

flavorText: Although it possesses a violent temperament, it won't put up with bullying. It uses the leaf in its mouth to sense the movements of its enemies.

name: Warp Energy
rules: This card provides Colorless Energy.
When you attach this card from your hand to your Active Pokémon, switch that Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

name: Dedenne

attacks.name: Tail Trickery
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Its upper whiskers are sensors that survey its surroundings. Its lower whiskers are organs that shoot electricity.

name: Natu

attacks.name: Me First
attacks.text: Draw a card.

flavorText: It is extremely good at climbing tree trunks and likes to eat the new sprouts on the trees.

name: Snubbull

attacks.name: Roar
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads and if your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, he of she chooses 1 of them and switches it with the Defending Pokémon. (Do the damage before switching the Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Lick
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Although it looks frightening, it is actually kind and affectionate. It is very popular.

name: Escavalier

attacks.name: Joust
attacks.text: Before doing damage, discard a Pokémon Tool card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Cavalry Lance
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon has no Weakness.

flavorText: They fly around at high speed, striking with their pointed spears. Even when in trouble, they face opponents bravely.

attacks.name: Rest
attacks.text: Remove all Special Conditions and all damage counters from Swinub. Swinub is now Asleep.

name: Hyper Devolution Spray
rules: Choose 1 of your evolved Pokémon. Take the highest Stage Evolution card from that Pokémon and put it into your hand. (You can't evolve a Pokémon the turn you devolve it.)

name: Stakataka-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Ultra Wall
abilities.text: Your Ultra Beasts take 10 less damage from your opponent's attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Assembly-GX
attacks.text: This attack does 50 more damage for each Prize card you have taken. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

attacks.name: Call for Friends
attacks.text: Search your deck for Grass Basic Pokémon and put as many of them as you like onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: String Shot
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Wigglytuff

attacks.name: Hypnoblast
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Punishing Slap
attacks.text: If any of your opponent's Pokémon have any Darkness Energy attached to them, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: It sheds its fine fur when the seasons change. The fur is gathered and spun into a luxurious yarn.

name: Full Heal
rules: Your Active Pokémon is no longer Asleep, Confused, Paralyzed, or Poisoned.

attacks.name: Double Scratch
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.text: Discard 1 Fire Energy card attached to Charmeleon.

name: Meowth

attacks.name: Pay Day
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, draw a card.

flavorText: Adores circular objects. Wanders the streets on a nightly basis to look for dropped loose change.

name: Mudbray

attacks.name: Stomp
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: The mud stuck to Mudbray's hooves enhances its grip and its powerful running gait.

name: Magnemite

attacks.name: Searching Magnet
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 Metal Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It sends out electromagnetic waves, which let it float through the air. Touching it while it's eating electricity will give you a full-body shock.

name: Nuzleaf

attacks.name: Razor Wind
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It lives deep in forests. With the leaf on its head, it makes a flute whose song makes listeners uneasy.

name: Dangerous Energy
rules: This card can only be attached to Darkness Pokémon. This card provides Darkness Energy only while this card is attached to a Darkness Pokémon.

Whenever the Darkness Pokémon this card is attached to is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon-EX (even if that Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 2 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon-EX.
(If this card is attached to anything other than a Darkness Pokémon, discard this card.)

name: Buried Fossil
rules: Evolves into Omanyte, Kabuto, Aerodactyl
You may play a Pokémon card that evolves from Mysterious Fossil on top of Buried Fossil. (This counts as evolving Buried Fossil.) Buried Fossil can't be affected by Special Conditions.

abilities.name: Reconstruction
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if you have a basic Energy card in your hand, you may search your deck for an Omanyte or Kabuto card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Then put a basic Energy card from your hand into your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Ferrothorn

attacks.name: Iron Defense
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Power Whip
attacks.text: Does 10 damage for each Energy attached to this Pokémon to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It fights by swinging around its three spiky feelers. A hit from these steel spikes can reduce a boulder to rubble.

name: Jynx

abilities.name: Ominous Posture
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may move 1 damage counter from 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Attract Smack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It sways its hips to a rhythm all its own. The precise movements of Jynx living in Alola are truly wonderful.

name: Snorlax

attacks.name: Toss and Turn
attacks.text: This attack can be used even if this Pokémon is Asleep. If it is, this attack does 90 more damage.

attacks.name: Swallow
attacks.text: Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Its stomach can digest any kind of food, even if it happens to be moldy or rotten.

name: Curse Powder
rules: Attach Curse Powder to 1 of your Evolved Pokémon (excluding Pokémon-ex) that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it. If the Pokémon Curse Powder is attached to is a Basic Pokémon or Pokémon-ex, discard Curse Powder.
If the Pokémon that Curse Powder is attached to is your Active Pokémon and is Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack, put 3 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.

name: Hydreigon

abilities.name: Dark Impulse
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may attach a Darkness Energy card from your discard pile to your Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Crazy Headbutt
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Their heads on their arms do not have brains. They use all three heads to consume and destroy everything.

name: Gastly

attacks.name: Pain Payback
attacks.text: Move 1 damage counter from Gastly to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon's body is 95% made up of gases, which are blown away by strong gusts of wind.

name: Makuhita
flavorText: It toughens its body by slamming into thick trees. Many snapped trees can be found near its nest.

name: Kirlia

attacks.name: Hypnosis
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: The cheerful spirit of its Trainer gives it energy for its psychokinetic power. It spins and dances when happy.

name: Energy Recycle System
rules: Search your discard pile for basic Energy cards. You may either show 1 basic Energy card to your opponent and put it into your hand, or show 3 basic Energy cards to your opponent and shuffle them into your deck.

name: Devolution Spray
rules: Devolve 1 of your evolved Pokémon and put the highest Stage Evolution card on it into your hand. (That Pokémon can't evolve this turn.)
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Minior

attacks.name: Swift
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, or any other effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Cosmicsplosion
attacks.text: This Pokémon is Knocked Out.

flavorText: It eats dust in the atmosphere. The composition of the dust determines the color of its core.

name: Darkness Energy
rules: If the Pokémon Darkness Energy is attached to attacks, the attack does 10 more damage to the Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance). Ignore this effect if the Pokémon that Darkness Energy is attached to isn't Darkness. Darkness Energy provides Darkness Energy. (Doesn't count as a basic Energy card.)

name: Switch Raft
rules: Switch your Active Water Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you do, heal 30 damage from the Pokémon you moved to your Bench.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Alolan Raticate

attacks.name: Evil Orders
attacks.text: Search your deck for a number of cards up to the number of your Benched Pokémon and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Endeavor
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: This gourmet Pokémon is particular about the taste and freshness of its food. Restaurants where Raticate live have a good reputation.

attacks.name: Quick Bind
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on that Pokémon.

attacks.name: Focus Energy
attacks.text: During your next turn, Lt. Surge's Rattata's Quick Attack's base damage is doubled.

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage; if tails, this attack does 10 damage.

name: Virizion

abilities.name: Justified
abilities.text: Each of this Pokémon's attacks does 50 more damage to Darkness Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Leaf Blade
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: This Pokémon fought humans in order to protect its friends. Legends about it continue to be passed down.

name: Double Full Heal
rules: Remove all Special Conditions from each of your Active Pokémon.

name: Swablu

attacks.name: Wing Flick
attacks.text: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Its wings bring cottony clouds to mind. It grooms with springwater and loves to sit on heads.

name: Flaaffy

attacks.name: Signal Beam
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: If its coat becomes fully charged with electricity, its tail lights up. It fires hair that zaps on impact.

name: Galarian Ponyta

attacks.name: Psy Bolt
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: This Pokémon will look into your eyes and read the contents of your heart. If it finds evil there, it promptly hides away.

name: Oran Berry
rules: Attach Oran Berry to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it. If that Pokémon is Knocked Out, discard this card.
At any time between turns, if the Pokémon this card is attached to has at least 2 damage counters on it, remove 2 damage counters from it. Then, discard Oran Berry.

name: Terrakion

attacks.name: Retaliate
attacks.text: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack during his or her last turn, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: Its charge is strong enough to break through a giant castle wall in one blow. This Pokémon is spoken of in legends.

name: Plusle

attacks.name: Greedy Draw
attacks.text: If you have the same number of cards or less in your hand as your opponent, draw cards until you have 1 more card than your opponent. (If you have more cards in your hand than your opponent, this attack does nothing.)

attacks.name: Hand Charge
attacks.text: Attach a Lightning Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon.

flavorText: It cheers on friends with pom-poms made of sparks. It drains power from telephone poles.

name: Regirock

abilities.name: Rock Peak Growl
abilities.text: Your Registeel's attacks do 10 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Tough Swing
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: Its entire body is made of rock. If any part chips off in battle, it attaches rocks to repair itself.

name: Druddigon

attacks.name: Proud Fang
attacks.text: If your opponent has any Pokémon BREAK in play, this attack does 60 more damage.

attacks.name: Giga Claw
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It warms its body by absorbing sunlight with its wings. When its body temperature falls, it can no longer move.

name: Pokémon Circulator
rules: Your opponent switches his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

name: Armaldo ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Dual Armor
abilities.text: As long as Armaldo ex has any React Energy cards attached to it, Armaldo ex is both Grass and Fighting type.

attacks.name: Spiral Drain
attacks.text: Remove 2 damage counters from Armaldo ex.

attacks.name: Vortex Chop
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon has any Resistance, this attack's base damage is 100 instead of 70.

name: Sandslash

attacks.name: Swift
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, or any other effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Curls up into a spiny ball when threatened. It can roll while curled up to attack or escape.

name: Magnetic Storm
rules: Each Pokémon in play has no Resistance.
This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

attacks.name: Ice Breath
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Poison Horn
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.text: Nidorino does 10 damage to itself.

attacks.name: Spin Ball
attacks.text: Flip 1 coin. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Double Spin
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

name: Drowzee

attacks.name: Hypnosis
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It can be spotted near recreational facilities, intending to eat the pleasant dreams of children who enjoyed themselves there that day.

name: Seel

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.text: Seel does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: Although clumsy on land, it moves easily through the arctic waters it calls home.

name: Exeggcute

attacks.name: Hypnosis
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Leech Seed
attacks.text: Unless all damage from this attack is prevented, you may remove 1 damage counter from Exeggcute.

flavorText: Often mistaken for eggs. When disturbed, they quickly gather and attack in swarms.

name: Wooper

attacks.name: Tail Whip
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: When it walks around on the ground, it coats its body with a slimy, poisonous film.

name: Lotad

attacks.name: Energy Ball
attacks.text: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy attached to Lotad but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. You can't add more than 20 damage in this way.

attacks.name: Synthesis
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a Grass Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: It looks like an aquatic plant and serves as a ferry to Pokémon that can't swim.

name: Solrock

attacks.name: Solar Generator
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Special Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Knock Away
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: It absorbs solar energy during the day. Always expressionless, it can sense what its foe is thinking.

name: Seedot

attacks.name: Harden
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, if Seedot would be damaged by an attack, prevent that attack's damage done to Seedot if that damage is 40 or less.

attacks.name: Astonish
attacks.text: Choose 1 card from your opponent's hand without looking. Look at that card you chose, then have your opponent shuffle that card into his or her deck.

flavorText: When it dangles from a tree branch, it looks just like an acorn. It enjoys scaring other Pokémon.

name: Shaymin-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Set Up
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench, you may draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.

attacks.name: Sky Return
attacks.text: Return this Pokémon and all cards attached to it to your hand.

name: Alolan Muk

abilities.name: Adventurous Appetite
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may look at the top 6 cards of your opponent's deck and discard any number of Item cards you find there. Your opponent shuffles the other cards back into their deck.

attacks.name: Gunk Shot
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: While it's unexpectedly quiet and friendly, if it's not fed any trash for a while, it will smash its Trainer's furnishings and eat up the fragments.

attacks.name: Disable
attacks.text: Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon's attacks. That Pokémon can't use that attack during your opponent's next turn.

name: Bronzor

attacks.name: Dull Light
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Implements shaped like it were discovered in ancient tombs. It is unknown if they are related.

abilities.name: Fluff
abilities.text: Whenever Swablu would be damaged or affected by an opponent's attack and already has at least 1 damage counter on it, flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of that attack, including damage, done to Swablu.

name: Dawn Stadium
rules: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.
Whenever any player attaches an Energy card from his or her hand to Grass Pokémon or Water Pokémon, remove 1 damage counter and all Special Conditions from that Pokémon.

name: Beginning Door
rules: Search your deck for Arceus, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Dratini

attacks.name: Shed Skin
attacks.text: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It is called the "Mirage Pokémon" because so few have seen it. Its shed skin has been found.

name: Silver Bangle
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.
The attacks of the Pokémon this card is attached to (excluding Pokémon-EX) do 30 more damage to Active Pokémon-EX (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Goop Gas Attack
rules: All Pokémon Powers stop working until the end of your opponent's next turn.

name: Gardevoir-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Secret Spring
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may attach a Fairy Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Infinite Force
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage times the amount of Energy attached to both Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Twilight-GX
attacks.text: Shuffle 10 cards from your discard pile into your deck. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Ampharos

abilities.name: Unseen Flash
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put 2 Lightning Energy cards from your hand in the Lost Zone. If you do, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Split Bomb
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage to 2 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: The tail's tip shines brightly and can be seen from far away. It acts as a beacon for lost people.

name: Klink

attacks.name: Smash Turn
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Interlocking two bodies and spinning around generates the energy they need to live.

name: Dual Ball
rules: Flip 2 coins. For each heads, search your deck for a Basic Pokémon card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Pidove

attacks.name: Homing Pidove
attacks.text: Look at the top card of your deck. Then, you may shuffle your deck.

flavorText: These Pokémon live in cities. They are accustomed to people. Flocks often gather in parks and plazas.

name: Maintenance
rules: Shuffle 2 of the other cards from your hand into your deck in order to draw a card.

name: Gallade

abilities.name: Double Type
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is in play, it is Psychic and Fighting type.

attacks.name: Power Cyclone
attacks.text: Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Because it can sense what its foe is thinking, its attacks burst out first, fast, and fierce.

name: Cleffa
rules: If your Active Pokémon is a Baby Pokémon and your opponent announces an attack, your opponent flips a coin (before doing anything else). If tails, your opponent's turn ends.

attacks.name: Energy Recycle
attacks.text: Shuffle a basic Energy card from your discard pile into your deck.

name: Silver Mirror
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.
Prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to the Pokémon this card is attached to (excluding Pokémon-EX) by your opponent's Team Plasma Pokémon.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Slugma
flavorText: This Pokémon is often seen crawling around in volcanic regions.

name: Landorus

attacks.name: Abundant Harvest
attacks.text: Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Gaia Hammer
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's). (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Lands visited by Landorus grant such bountiful crops that it has been hailed as "The Guardian of the Fields."

attacks.name: Horn Hazard
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

name: Golett
flavorText: They were sculpted from clay in ancient times. No one knows why, but some of them are driven to continually line up boulders.

name: Mudsdale

attacks.name: Kick Away
attacks.text: Your opponent switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Vigorous Dash
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 30 damage to itself. This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It spits a mud that provides resistance to both wind and rain, so the walls of old houses were often coated with it.

name: Metapod

attacks.name: Stiffen
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to Metapod during your opponent's next turn. (Any other effects of attacks still happen.)

attacks.name: Stun Spore
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It is vulnerable to attack because its shell is soft, exposing its weak and tender body.

name: Watchog

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Hyper Fang
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: They make the patterns on their bodies shine in order to threaten predators. Keen eyesight lets them see in the dark.

name: Balloon Berry
rules: Attach Balloon Berry to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it. If that Pokémon is Knocked Out, discard this card.
When the Pokémon Balloon Berry is attached to retreats, discard Balloon Berry instead of discarding Energy cards.

attacks.name: Thrash
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 10 more damage. If tails, Pupitar does 10 damage to itself.

name: Cubone

attacks.name: Whimsy Tackle
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It always wears the skull of its dead mother, so no one has any idea what its hidden face looks like.

name: Honedge
flavorText: Apparently this Pokémon is born when a departed spirit inhabits a sword. It attaches itself to people and drinks their life force.

name: Alolan Meowth

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: This Pokémon was not originally found in Alola. Human actions caused a surge in their numbers, and they went feral. They're prideful and crafty.

name: Rare Candy
rules: Choose 1 of your Basic Pokémon in play. If you have a Stage 1 or Stage 2 card that evolves from that Pokémon in your hand, put that card on the Basic Pokémon. (This counts as evolving that Pokémon.) If you choose a Stage 2 Pokémon in your hand, put that Pokémon on the Basic Pokémon instead of on a Stage 1 Pokémon.

name: Wynaut

abilities.name: Peppy Pick
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, choose a random card from your opponent's hand. Your opponent reveals that card and shuffles it into their deck. If you use this Ability, your turn ends.

flavorText: It tends to move in a pack with others. They cluster in a tight group to sleep in a cave.

name: Lycanroc

attacks.name: Dangerous Rogue
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Bathed in the setting sun of evening, Lycanroc has undergone a special kind of evolution. An intense fighting spirit underlies its calmness.

name: Unown [W]

abilities.name: WALL
abilities.text: As long as Unown W is your Active Pokémon, any damage done to your Pokémon by an opponent's attack is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Hidden Power
